name: Office of Student Organizations and Activities
description:
SOA is the hub for involvement on campus! We are excited that you are on campus and are looking to get involved and engaged at the University of Kentucky! 

Student Organizations and Activities (SOA) makes it possible for University of Kentucky students to gain the skills, relationships and experiences that will positively influence their educational and career trajectories. We do this by creating intentional spaces and opportunities where students can develop as individuals, leaders, and community members.

 

Check out our new registered student organization handbook here! 

Visit our website for important policies for student organizations.
summary: The Office of Student Organizations and Activities make it possible for University of Kentucky students to gain the skills, relationships, and experiences that will positively influence their educational and career trajectories. #WildlyInvolved

name: Fraternity & Sorority Life
description:
The Fraternity and Sorority Life Office is home to over 50 social fraternities and sororities within four governing councils and is a part of the Dean of Students Office within Student Success.

 

The mission of Fraternity and Sorority Life at the University of Kentucky is to empower the students throughout their membership experience to create a welcoming and inclusive community that fosters a sense of belonging. We accomplish this through our membership development programming, chapter coaching, advising and collaboration with campus partners as well as stakeholders.

 

Looking for how to join our community? Please visit our FSL website for more information!
summary: Fraternity & Sorority Life is home to 50+ social fraternities and sororities within four governing councils at the University of Kentucky.

name: Club Sports
description:
This BBNvolved page is the hub for resources, forms, and documents for Registered Club Sports/Sports Clubs. If you have any questions, please reach out to the Office of Student Organizations and Activities at studentorgs@uky.edu. 

For a schedule of upcoming registered Club Sports events, click here!

name: Pi Sigma Alpha
description:
Mission: To recognize academic excellence by, and provide leadership opportunities for, undergraduate and graduate students in political science and related fields; to stimulate scholarship and interest in politics, government, and democracy; to encourage diversity, equity, and inclusion in the discipline; and to support curricular and extracurricular activities that promote dialogue and civic engagement.

Values: Diversity, pluralism, and inclusion; Nonpartisanship; Academic excellence; Commitment to democratic principles; Transparency and accountability
summary: Pi Sigma Alpha, the Political Science Honor Society, promotes and recognizes academic excellence in the field of political science by creating networking opportunities, offering workshops, and fostering relationships between peers.

name: Economics Society
description: The Economics Society at the University of Kentucky is a social organization connecting students through their common interest in economics. Our organization offers job fairs and mentoring programs for students majoring in Economics, or any major interested in learning more about the field. We regularly bring in speakers and organize events for anyone looking to stimulate their interest in economics.
summary: Economics Society is for anyone with an interest in economics and wishes to attend conferences focused on the topics and attend weekly meetings centered around fun and academic guest speakers! We also secure internships and jobs for members!

name: MANRRS at the University of Kentucky
description:
The National Society of MANRRS focuses on developing and empowering individuals in agriculture, natural resources, and related sciences by nurturing potential, supporting unique journeys, and connecting members to opportunities that inspire leadership and innovation. 

 

 

The MANRRS Chapter at the University of Kentucky hosts activities that promote academic and professional growth in the greater Lexington area. Our chapter offers opportunities to enhance leadership, organizational, and public speaking skills, and to experience professional critique of scholarly work in a “user-friendly” environment.

Membership is open to all individuals interested in contributing to advancement in agriculture, natural resources, and related sciences. Membership categories include student, professional, associate, and business.

 

National MANRRS Non-Discrimination Statement:
MANRRS prohibits discrimination in the delivery of services and information on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, religion, age, disability, political beliefs, sexual orientation, and marital or family status.
summary: The National Society of MANRRS fosters academic and professional development in agriculture, natural resources, and related sciences. UK MANRRS connects agriculture and natural resource students, professionals, and allies through a supportive network.

name: PediaKATS - Student PPA
description: PediaKATS’s main effort is to advocate for the proper health education of pediatric patients and their caregivers in the greater Lexington area. This mission will be to develop student pharmacists at the University of Kentucky College of Pharmacy into pharmacists that can integrate the lessons learned through this organization into their professional lives. The second purpose is to allow a streamlined avenue into shadowing opportunities for student pharmacists with current pediatric pharmacists. This will allow students a view into the daily aspects of a pediatric pharmacist and give students a further avenue to develop professionally.
summary: PediaKATS’s main effort is to advocate for the proper health education of pediatric patients and their caregivers in the greater Lexington area. This mission will be to develop student pharmacists at the University of Kentucky College of Pharmacy into ..

name: UK Men's Club Volleyball
description: A men's volleyball club looking to travel across the region compete against other schools, while promoting an atmosphere of fun on and off the court.
summary: We are the University’s men’s volleyball club. As an organization we provide a competitive yet fun atmosphere for young men to play volleyball. We travel to compete in tournaments, ending the season by competing in the NCVF National Championship.

name: Pre-Pharmacy Club
description: The Pre-Pharmacy Club at the University of Kentucky is a student run organization supported by the UKY College of Pharmacy that aims to educate and connect students with interests in pharmacy. The club meets once a month every semester and has volunteer opportunities and social events throughout the year. During the meetings, we have a plethora of registered pharmacists who come and talk about their job experience and their experience going through pharmacy school. Our volunteer opportunities are usually health-care based and our social events are a fun way to get to know students. If you want to learn more about pharmacy and get connected with students like you, join the Pre-Pharmacy Club today!
summary: Pre-Pharmacy Club is a student-run organization supported by the UKY College of Pharmacy that aims to educate and connect students with interests in pharmacy. The club meets once a month every semester with other social and service events during the year

name: Geology Club
description:
Our main goal is to bring together individuals who have similar interests in the Earth and Environmental Sciences and let them grow as friends and peers as they explore these interests. We are supportive of everyone who comes to our meetings and work to build a strong community, focused in the Geology Department but far-reaching into other parts of the University of Kentucky and the local community. We also aim to reach out and gain interest in the Earth and Environmental Sciences in the youth of the state as well as the next generation of college students!

Typical meetings will contain announcements and opportunities, free food, and a short activity or discussion. We also try to take a short day trip occasionally to a local trail, geologic feature, or other activity of interest.
summary: Do you like rocks, terrible puns, and the great outdoors? Then Geology Club is for you! We strive to bring together people interested in Earth and Environmental Sciences as well as find time to explore the outdoors! And, you might just learn something :)

name: Lexington Campus Disciples
description: As a group that genuinely feels like a family, we at LCD are committed to following the Bible and creating a community of like-minded disciples as a safe haven in a campus environment. We strive to go back to the basics of Christianity and, no matter where you are in your faith, we recognize the importance of meeting others where they’re at. We have several fun retreats throughout the year as well as an international summer conference. For more information please visit http://www.thelexingtonchurch.org or follow us on Facebook & Instagram (@LCDministry). We hope to see you soon!
summary: As a group that genuinely feels like a family, we at LCD are all about following the Bible and creating a community of like-minded disciples as a safe haven in a campus environment.

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc.
description: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated is an international service organization that was founded on the campus of Howard University in Washington, D.C. in 1908. It is the oldest Greek lettered organization established by African-American college-educated women.
summary: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated is an international service organization that was founded on the campus of Howard University in Washington, D.C. in 1908. It is the oldest Greek lettered organization established by African-American college women.

name: Delta Delta Delta
description: The purpose of Delta Delta Delta shall be to establish a perpetual bond of friendship among its members, to develop a stronger and more womanly character, to broaden the moral and intellectual life, and to assist its members in every possible way. It shall also be the purpose of Delta Delta Delta to promote and develop mutually beneficial relationships between the Fraternity and the colleges and universities where the Fraternity has established chapters, to develop qualities of unselfish leadership among its members, and to encourage them to assume, with integrity and devotion to moral and democratic principles, the highest responsibilities of college women.
summary: The purpose of Delta Delta Delta shall be to establish a perpetual bond of friendship among its members, to develop a stronger and more womanly character, to broaden the moral and intellectual life, and to assist its members in every possible way.

name: University of Kentucky Club Baseball
description: Club Baseball plays through the National Club Baseball Association in the Mid-Atlantic West Conference for a World Series berth each year. Division II National Champions 2008 and 2009. Currently in the Division II Mid-Atlantic West Conference. Opponents include: Xavier, Ohio State DII, EKU, Indiana DII, and Akron.
summary: We play through the National Club Baseball Association for a World Series berth each year. Division II National Champions 2008 and 2009. We host home series and travel to opposing schools for away series.

name: Appalachian Law Caucus
summary: The Appalachian Law Caucus is dedicated to supporting students from Appalachia in transitioning to the study of law. The Caucus strives to increase awareness of issues that affect both Appalachians and the Appalachian community at large.

name: Wildcat Service Dogs
description:
Wildcat Service Dogs (WSD) is a student-run organization that strives to raise, train, and educate the public about service dogs. Our events and monthly meetings are open to anyone who would like to attend. To become an active member, students will go through a training course and an evaluation. We offer an extensive “Full Access Sitter” program where students are able to watch and handle any of our program dogs in public. After being a sitter for at least one semester, students in our program can also be selected as trainers to raise service dogs in training (SDiTs) for the first 12-24 months of their lives. These students are responsible for the part-time training, socializing, and housing of the dog. 


Our current partner is Paws with Purpose, a non-profit organization based in Louisville, KY that provides highly skilled assistance dogs for children and adults with physical disabilities or other special needs. For more information about Paws with Purpose, feel free to visit their website: https://www.pawswithpurpose.org/
summary: WSD strives to raise, train, and educate the public about service dogs. Students will go through a training course and an evaluation in order to join our "Full Access Sitter" program where they can watch and handle any of our program dogs in public.

name: DanceBlue: The University of Kentucky Dance Marathon
description:
DanceBlue is a University of Kentucky student-run organization that fundraises year-round and culminates into a 24-hour no-sitting, no-sleeping dance marathon. Money raised through DanceBlue is donated to the Golden Matrix Fund, established to support the kids of the DanceBlue Kentucky Children’s Hospital Hematology/Oncology Clinic both today and well into the future through an endowment. DanceBlue also helps to provide funds to support research at the UK Markey Cancer Center. Through 20 years of DanceBlue, more than $25 million has been raised to support this cause.

 

If you would like to become a part of the DanceBlue family, you can apply in the spring each year to be a Coordinator or on any of our Committees for the upcoming year or you can become a Team Captain, Adopt-A-Family Captain, dancer, or volunteer. You can also donate to our cause at any time, participate in our events and fundraisers, and attend our 24-hour dance marathon in the spring. If you would like regular updates on all things DanceBlue, request to join our organization on this page! Requesting to join does not guarantee any sort of position or dancer spot, but will update you on our events, fundraisers, and the process of how you might attempt to dance.

 

Follow us on Social Media!

Facebook: facebook.com/danceblue/

Instagram: @uk_danceblue

X: @uk_danceblue

TikTok: @uk_danceblue
summary: DanceBlue is a yearlong fundraising effort that culminates into a 24-hour no-sitting, no-sleeping dance marathon. Money raised benefits the DanceBlue Kentucky Children's Hospital Pediatric Oncology/Hematology Clinic through the Golden Matrix Fund.

name: Veterans Law Society
description: This organization shall bridge the gap of knowledge between service members and veterans of the Armed Forces and the community they serve. We will promote academic success of all veterans, service members, those interested in service, and all other students at the University of Kentucky College of Law.
summary: The Veterans Law Society is dedicated to supporting law student veterans and their families in transitioning to the study of law from military service. We strive to increase awareness of issues that affect both student veterans.

name: Badminton Association at UK
description:
Badminton Association at the University of Kentucky aims to popularize the sport, encourage international communication, provide systematic training for enthusiasts, and facilitate participation in regional and national tournaments.

Please join our Facebook group for events and updates!
summary: Badminton Association at UK exists to popularize the sport at tUniversity of Kentucky, to encourage international communication, to provide a platform for badminton lovers to get systematic training and to participate in inter-collegiate as well as natio

name: Kappa Sigma
description: Kappa Sigma is the largest National Fraternity. We strive to impart the values of fellowship, leadership, scholarship, and service in our members.
summary: Kappa Sigma is the largest National Fraternity. We strive to impart the values of fellowship, leadership, scholarship, and service in our members.

name: American Medical Association - Medical Student Section - Lexington Campus
description: AMA Mission & Guiding Principles Mission: To promote the art and science of medicine and the betterment of public health. Our guiding principles set the aspirations that we endeavor to achieve: AMA is one enterprise, highly capable, well coordinated and focused on high impact results. AMA believes that there is a national imperative to chart a successful course for health care delivery that will improve the health of the nation. AMA embraces the need for change and believes physician leadership is critical to the successful evolution of health care in a patient focused delivery system. AMA will build on its legacy of leading physician ethics, setting standards for medical education, and advancing medical science to serve as the premier voice for the core values of the medical profession. AMA has the unique combination of talent with practical skills and intellectual capabilities, the financial resources, and influential multi-sector relationships to be a leading voice in the transformation of health care. The AMA has a robust House of Delegates consisting of representation from every State and medical society, a solid base of physician members, a thriving advocacy influence, the most revered journals and resources in medicine, and respected practice tools. Together, we can shape a better, healthier future – not just for patients and physicians, but for the country as a whole.
summary: The Lexington Chapter of the American Medical Association - Medical Student Section at UK

name: Graduate Student Congress
description: The mission of the GSC is to unify and represent graduate and professional students and postdocs at UK in matters affecting our quality of life, and to facilitate interdisciplinary collaboration and professional development through seminars, forums, outreach programming, advocacy, and community enhancement.
summary: The representative body for all graduate students, professional students, and postdoctoral scholars at the University of Kentucky.

name: Gatton College Undergraduate Resource Center
description:
This group will serve as a hub for the Gatton College of Business and Economics Undergraduate Resource Center.  The purpose of this hub is to give Gatton students a way to connect to the Undergraduate Resource Center online where they can keep up with Gatton events and updates.

The Gatton Undergraduate Resource Center is the student's central location for advising and student services in the Gatton College of Business and Economics.
summary: This group will serve as a hub for the Gatton College of Business and Economics Undergraduate Resource Center.  The purpose of this hub is to give Gatton students a way to connect to the Undergraduate Resource Center online where they can keep up with ...

name: Alpha Delta Pi
description: For more than 150 years, extraordinary women have called Alpha Delta Pi home. Originally called the “Adelphean Society,” Alpha Delta Pi was the first secret society for women and forged the way for women in the fraternity system. Our founding took place at Wesleyan Female College in Macon, Georgia – the first college in the world chartered to grant degrees to women. We continue to initiate progress throughout the fraternity system, being the first sorority to offer a shortened new member education program. Alpha Delta Pi also builds and fosters a sisterhood of leaders. Sisters find Alpha Delta Pi a safe place to draw on their unique experiences and make their mark. We offer members the opportunity to obtain leadership experience as chapter officers as well as through sisters who are involved in various organizations on campus. Beginning with six young women and now including over 235,000 initiated members and more than 190 collegiate chapters throughout the United States and Canada, Alpha Delta Pi has expanded beyond belief while staying true to its founding ideals: character, knowledge, sisterhood, and service.
summary: For more than 150 years, extraordinary women have called Alpha Delta Pi home. Originally called the “Adelphean Society,” Alpha Delta Pi was the first secret society for women and forged the way for women in the fraternity system.

name: Agricultural and Medical Biotechnology Club
description: AMBT Club develops a community among and presents opportunities for students interested in fields related to agricultural and medical biotechnology - including industry, research, and preprofessional fields such as pre-medicine, pre-pharmacy, pre-veterinary, pre-dental, and pre-physician's assistant.
summary: The purpose of the AMBT Club is to present opportunities for students interested in agricultural and medical biotechnology related fields including agriculture, biology, chemistry, and preprofessional fields.

name: Triangle Fraternity
description: Established in 1920, the Kentucky Chapter of Triangle Fraternity is a social fraternity specifically for architects, scientists, and engineers. Check out our Instagram page @uktriangle
summary: A social fraternity specifically for architects, scientists, and engineers.

name: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc.
description: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity is the first Black, inter-collegiate Greek-lettered fraternity. It was founded by seven great men on December 4, 1906 at Cornell University in Ithaca, New York. Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc., develops leaders, promotes brotherhood and academic excellence, while providing service and advocacy for our communities. The Epsilon Chi Chapter was founded on the campus of the University of Kentucky April 1, 1965. Fraternity Motto: "First of All, Servants of All, We Shall Transcend All"
summary: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity is the first Black, inter-collegiate Greek-lettered fraternity. It was founded by seven great men on December 4, 1906 at Cornell University in Ithaca, New York.Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc., develops leaders, promotes brot...

name: Internal Medicine and Pediatrics Interest Group
description: The mission of the Med/Peds interest group is to bolster student awareness of the practice of medicine as an Internal Medicine-Pediatrics physician. The group works to increase student exposure to the specialty of Med/Peds and provide opportunities to network within it.
summary: The IMPIG mission is to provide medical students with continuing education in the practice of medicine as an Internal Medicine-Pediatrics physician. This group's aim will be to make students aware of and provide guidance and education on the specialty.

name: American Constitution Society
description: Representing a progressive blend of jurisprudence and a firm defense of the Living Constitution at the University of Kentucky College of Law. Promoting the vitality of the U.S. Constitution and the fundamental values it expresses: individual rights and liberties, genuine equality, access to justice, democracy and the rule of law.
summary: Representing a progressive blend of jurisprudence and a firm defense of the Living Constitution at the University of Kentucky College of Law.Promoting the vitality of the U.S. Constitution and the fundamental values it expresses: individual rights and ...

name: Reality Parties
description: Reality Parties was created by a group of UK students with the goal of creating safe, fun spaces for students to enjoy big, energetic parties without the campus trappings of alcohol and drugs. We love the excitement, energy, and community that a good party can create. Author and theologian C.S. Lewis once said that "Joy is the serious business of heaven," and we can think of few better ways to live out this joy than through big, life-affirming parties.
summary: Reality was created by a group of UK students with the goal of creating safe, fun spaces for students to enjoy big, energetic parties without the campus trappings of alcohol and drugs. We love the excitement, energy, and community a good party creates.

name: Pre-Vet Club
description: The Purpose of the Pre-Vet Club at the University of Kentucky is to serve and develop the future leaders of veterinary medicine, to provide an atmosphere of social enrichment, and to expand knowledge and experience in the veterinary field. We aspire to create a community of support, friendship and connection between future veterinarians at an undergraduate level. Collectively we will help empower students, as we prepare for vet school. The Pre-Vet Club hosts various events throughout the semester as well as meetings on the 3rd Wednesday of each month at 7 PM.
summary: The Purpose of the Pre-Vet Club at the University of Kentucky is to serve and develop the future leaders of veterinary medicine, to provide an atmosphere of social enrichment, and to expand knowledge and experience in the veterinary field.

name: Rho Chi Honor Society
description: The Rho Chi Society encourages and recognizes excellence in intellectual achievement and advocates critical inquiry in all aspects of pharmacy. The Society further encourages high standards of conduct and character and fosters fellowship among its members. The Society seeks universal recognition of its members as lifelong intellectual leaders in pharmacy, and as a community of scholars, to instill the desire to pursue intellectual excellence and critical inquiry to advance the profession.
summary: The Rho Chi Society encourages and recognizes excellence in intellectual achievement and advocates   critical inquiry in all aspects of pharmacy. The Society further encourages high standards of conduct and character and fosters fellowship among its me...

name: Masters of Business Administration Student Association
description: The MBA Student Association (MBASA) is a group of current University of Kentucky MBA students who represent the entire MBA class with the mission of enriching the quality of student life and building relationships among fellow students, faculty, administration, alumni, prospective students, and the business community. The MBASA are selected by their peers to help lead the class in areas of professional development, social events, community service and student engagement.
summary: The MBA Student Association (MBASA) is a group of current University of Kentucky MBA students who represent the entire MBA class.

name: Chi Psi
description: Chi Psi Fraternity is a strong presence at 33 of America’s finest institutions of higher learning, prevailing to the present day through innovation and a unique outlook. Taking the idea of brotherhood to heart, Chi Psi is focused on developing young men socially, intellectually, and morally. Chi Psi is a Fraternity of gentlemen in pursuit of excellence that enhances the college experience, develops lifelong relationships, and builds better men.
summary: Chi Psi at UK prides itself on brotherhood and is focused on developing men socially, intellectually, and morally, while also creating a positive impact on campus and throughout Lexington.

name: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc.*
description: Found on September 16, 1980, the Mu Theta Chapter of Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc promotes the founding principles of Brotherhood, Scholarship, and Service among collegiate men on the campus of the University of Kentucky as well as the Lexington community.
summary: Found on September 16, 1980, the Mu Theta chapter of Phi Beta Sigma promotes the founding principles of Brotherhood, Scholarship, and Service among collegiate men on the campus of the University of Kentucky as well as the Lexington community.

name: Snowcats - Extreme Outdoor Club
description:
Yo! We are the Extreme Outdoors club at UK! We are one of the largest student organizations at UK, taking 200+ students skiing every year. In addition to the main winter and spring break trip out West, we go white water rafting, hiking, rock climbing, paint-balling, and sky-diving. We are a very social and recreational club so anyone is free to join no matter of their actual experience on snow or even how often you can even do club events! We are just here to provide you with some crazy opportunities and experiences during and beyond your time here at UK.

 

GroupMe link: https://web.groupme.com/join_group/33903101/tcuS4o
summary: We are the Extreme Outdoors club at UK! We are one of the largest student organizations at UK and host everything from paintball and hiking trips to skydiving and skiing out west. We're stoked to meet you, so shoot us a message and come check us out!

name: American Sign Language & Deaf Culture Club
description: The American Sign Language & Deaf Culture Club aims to teach University of Kentucky students and staff American Sign Language fluency while also educating members about deaf culture and deaf advocacy issues. Members will have opportunities to work hands-on with our local deaf community while establishing a community of their own at UK!
summary: The ASLDCC aims to teach University of Kentucky students and staff American Sign Language fluency while also educating members about deaf culture, deaf advocacy issues, and various opportunities students can take to engage with out local deaf community.

name: Student Activities Board
description:
About SAB

The purpose of the Student Activities Board (SAB) is to enhance the college experience by providing students and other members of the campus and local communities with entertaining, educational and/or enriching programs that are reflective of contemporary issues and trends. SAB's four core values are Teamwork: to strive to create a network which is tied together by communication, friendship, and cooperation that works toward building lasting ties with the UK community. Enrichment: to not only provide entertainment but to use our events as a medium through which to enlighten and educate. Community: To create a sense of community between all students, striving to facilitate events that serve a variety of interests. Stewardship: to efficiently and effectively manage all financial affairs, give back to the student body, promote leadership, preserve integrity, and uphold accountability.

 

Applying for a position in SAB? 

Interested in joining SAB? SAB has a rolling application to join the board as a chair. The application is not live yet, but stay tuned for our 25-26 chair application this summer.
summary: The Student Activities Board (SAB) is the largest programming board at the University of Kentucky. SAB brings more than 80 entertaining, educational, & enriching programs to campus each year. These programs are designed to enhance the college experience.

name: Phi Delta Epsilon International Medical Fraternity - Undergraduate Chapter
description:
Phi Delta Epsilon International Medical Fraternity creates physicians of integrity with a life-long commitment to our guiding principles: Philanthropy, Deity, Equity, Education. The following principles are instated through Fellowship, Service, Mentoring, and Formal Training in Leadership, Science & Ethics. Motto: "Facta Non Verba, Deeds Not Words"

 

FALL 2025 RECRUITMENT:

APPLICATION DUE SEPTEMBER 7TH @ 11:59PM

Please go to ukphide.com for the updated recruitment event dates. Email phiderecruit.uky@gmail.com if you have any questions regarding the application or requirements!
summary: Phi Delta Epsilon International Medical Fraternity creates physicians of integrity with a life-long commitment to our guiding principles: Philanthropy, Deity, Equity, Education.

name: Chabad - Jewish Student Center
description: We don't just meet at Chabad. Chabad meets you wherever you are. Guided by the example of the Rebbe's love for all people Chabad welcomes all people, and students from all backgrounds of Judaism.
summary: Chabad @ UK is the Jewish home away from home for our students, providing authentic Jewish experiences and Jewish education, as well as an open door for questions, conversations and support. We don't just meet at Chabad. Chabad meets you wherever you are

name: Cats for Christ
description: Cats for Christ is a growing campus ministry serving God on the campus of the University of Kentucky! We are all about being in community, in the Word, and serving others. All are welcome!
summary: Cats for Christ is a growing campus ministry serving God on the campus of the University of Kentucky! We are all about being in community, in the Word, and serving others. All are welcome!

name: Mu Nu Gamma
summary: Mu Nu Gamma was established at the University of Kentucky to honor top performing mining engineering students. It is the first honor society solely devoted to the mining engineering profession.

name: College Mentors for Kids
description:
Mentoring transforms lives: We connect kids to college students through weekly on-campus activities that inspire growth, confidence, and brighter futures. Each week, College Mentors transport approximately 100 students from Lexington elementary schools to UK's campus, where they are paired with a college-aged mentor. Our mentor-buddy pairs meet on campus from 3-5 p.m. on Tuesday and Wednesday and 3:30-5:30 p.m. on Monday. During this time, we have guests speaker, a fun activity that relates to different college and career paths, and a skill-building personal component!

 

Our application is NOW OPEN!  If you are interested in joining, go to https://members.collegementors.org/application to apply!  We are limited on space for Fall 2025, so please APPLY NOW! We will have in-person meetings with our little buddies, and we are closely following regulations and recommendations at the national and local levels to ensure our activities are safe for everyone involved. If you have any questions about joining College Mentors, please reach out!
summary: College Mentors for Kids is a non-profit organization on UK's campus that mentors elementary students from local schools.  This is a great service opportunity for those looking to make a difference in a child's life.

name: ChefMed
description: ChefMed aims to promote knowledge and appreciation of cooking, food, HAES, and nutrition in the University of Kentucky College of Medicine.
summary: ChefMed aims to promote knowledge and appreciation of cooking, food, and nutrition in the University of Kentucky College of Medicine.

name: Phi Kappa Psi
description: Phi Kappa Psi at the University of Kentucky was chartered in 1988 as the Kentucky Beta chapter. Nationally we were founded on February 19th, 1852 at Jefferson College in Canonsburg, PA. Our national headquarters is located in Indianapolis, IN.
summary: Phi Kappa Psi at the University of Kentucky was chartered in 1988 as the Kentucky Beta chapter. Nationally we were founded on February 19th, 1852 at Jefferson College in Canonsburg, PA. Our national headquarters is located in Indianapolis, IN.

name: Women's Club Volleyball
description: Intercollegiate women's club volleyball team for University of Kentucky students that travels across the country. We fundraise throughout the season to cover travel expenses for three tournaments a semester and general team costs. At the end of each season we travel to a different location for a national tournament where all qualified teams in the country play for the championship title.
summary: Intercollegiate women's club volleyball team for University of Kentucky students that travels across the country. We fundraise throughout the season to cover travel expenses for three tournaments a semester and general team costs.

name: Wildcat Ambassador Student Organization
description: The Wildcat Ambassador Student Organization is a branch of the Visitor Center and the Office of Undergraduate Admission that is directed toward recruiting high school and transfer students to the University of Kentucky through meaningful relationships. The Wildcat Ambassadors will represent the University of Kentucky in a positive light at various recruitment events throughout the academic year, providing a valuable student to student connection that is vital to the college decision-making process. Ambassadors should be ready to share their stories regarding: -Why they chose UK -What makes them passionate about UK -Their major(s)/minor(s) -Their extracurricular activities and campus involvements
summary: The Wildcat Ambassador Student Organization is a branch of the Visitor Center and the Office of Undergraduate Admission that is directed toward recruiting high school and transfer students to the University of Kentucky.

name: CRU
description:
Cru is a family, captivated by the joy of knowing Christ, united under the call to make Him known.

  • Large Group Meeting: Thursdays at 8 PM
  • Ask us about small group and discipleship group opportunities!

Follow us on Instagram (@ukcru) and Tik Tok (@ukcru) for up to date info about small groups, social events, and more!
summary: We are a family, captivated by the joy of knowing Christ, united under the call to make Him known.

name: Christian Medical and Dental Association
description: The Christian Medical & Dental Association (CMDA) exists to motivate, educate, and equip Christian health professionals to glorify God. Students can become involved through joining weekly small-group Bible studies with fellow students, learning from Christian health professionals during talks on a variety of topics, or through participating in other fun fellowship activities put on by CMDA including outdoor picnics, board game nights, and monthly dinners in the homes of local physicians. CMDA is a great opportunity to become further involved in professional school and connect with other Christians from your classes and from the community. We are a group of students dedicated to using our studies and our gifts to glorify God and further His kingdom, and we would love to walk with you along the way.
summary: The Christian Medical & Dental Association (CMDA) exists to motivate, educate, and equip Christian health professionals to glorify God. All graduate health professions students are invited to attend weekly Bible studies and monthly meetings/social events

name: University of Kentucky Disc Golf Club
description:
Disc Golf plays like ball golf but with discs being thrown into baskets.  Our objective is to grow the sport and to compete in the College Disc Golf (CDG) scene! In addition to playing locally. Whether you want to play competitively or casually, this is the place for you! 

We do all of our communication through Discord.  Message us on Social Media or contact an officer if you want to join!
summary: UKDG is here to provide you with a way to play a fun, cheap, and safe sport.  Anyone is welcome to join who wants to play casually or be introduced into the sport!  Our more skilled players compete in intercollegiate in PDGA-sanctioned College events!

name: Beta Theta Pi
description: Beta Theta Pi is dedicated to building men of principle for a principled life. Our brotherhood aids the individual, builds the Fraternity and strengthens the host academic institution through lifelong devotion to intellectual excellence, high standards of moral conduct and responsible citizenship.
summary: Beta Theta Pi is dedicated to building men of principle for a principled life.

The MSA strives to uphold Islamic values through education, outreach, advocacy, and altruism.
summary: The Muslim Student Association aims towards fostering a diverse and inclusive community for both Muslims and non-Muslims through service and education.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description:
The National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) is the coordinating body for nine historically African-American fraternities and sororities.

The University of Kentucky NPHC is comprised of five fraternities: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc.; Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc.; Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc.; Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc.; Iota Phi Theta Fraternity, Inc.; and three sororities: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc.: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc., and Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. 

The council organizes educational programs, community service projects, and social events for its members, as well as for the greater University of Kentucky and Lexington community. Staple events hosted by UKY NPHC is the annual Homecoming Step Show, Minority Student Welcome, and NPHC Week for the Fall and Spring Semester. If you are interested in joining a NPHC organization, please connect with an active member of the organization and attend NPHC 101.
summary: The University of Kentucky National Pan-Hellenic Council is the coordinating body for nine historically African-American fraternities and sororities.

name: Alpha Gamma Rho
description: Alpha Gamma Rho (AGR) is a fraternity at the University of Kentucky, founded in 1920. Focused on promoting leadership and character development, AGR provides networking opportunities, academic support, and social activities for its members. Being the only social and professional fraternity at the University of Kentucky; the fraternity emphasizes a well-rounded college experience and community service, preparing members for successful careers both within the agriculture industry as well as other industries.
summary: Alpha Gamma Rho (AGR) is a fraternity at the University of Kentucky, founded in 1920. Focused on promoting leadership and character development, AGR provides networking opportunities, academic support, and social activities for its members.

name: Black Voices Gospel Choir
description:
As a gospel choir, Black Voices has a long and illustrious history at the University of Kentucky. Founded in 1970, the choir had its beginnings when a small group of African American students came together based on their mutual love of gospel music. After meeting over several months, the idea was proposed to formally organize as a gospel choir and to seek official recognition as a UK student organization. A constitution was developed and ratified, in addition to the choir gaining official University recognition later that year. The choir, directed by Mrs. Lutischa Coleman-Morton, was formally established in 1970, with the assistance of Minority Student Affairs Office which is now known as The African American Student Affairs Office. There have been many highlights in the long history of the choir.
The choir has performed in the National Collegiate Gospel Festival in Atlanta, Georgia, the Bobby Jones Gospel Explosion, the Gospel Praise Company Workshop, and were hosted by the Rev. Martin Luther King, Sr. at Ebenezer Baptist Church in Atlanta, Georgia. The Black Voices gospel choir at UK have two recordings to their credit, “God Is," which was recorded in 1974, and “Victory Shall Be Mine," recorded in 1975. More recently, the Black Voices at UK won the first “Battle of the Choirs, held in Lexington, Kentucky featuring Vanessa Bell Armstrong, and they were blessed to lead the worship service for the Mississippi Mass Choir (June 2000). In March 2000, Black Voices held its first campus-wide Gospel revival entitled “Even Me? Even You!" and were awarded the “Outstanding African American Student Organization (April 2000), and held their first campus-wide Holiday Program (December 2001).
From its origin to the present, Black Voices gospel choir at UK has continued to play a vital role in the recruitment of African American students to the UK campus and enriching their lives while attending the University of Kentucky. Since its 26th anniversary year, Black Voices at UK receives academic credit for class participation, while continuing to carry on a fine tradition of promoting goodwill through music. As Black Voices at the University of Kentucky continue their work and long tradition of excellence, it is with a spirit of optimism and enthusiasm to rededicate themselves to the mission of advancing the rich tradition of African American Spiritual and Gospel Music.
The purpose and mission of Black Voices Gospel Choir at University of Kentucky are to be an outlet for any student, at the University of Kentucky, to musically express themselves through gospel music and to positively act as a means of public relations toward recruitment for the University.

Psalm 118:23 “This is the Lord’s doing; it is marvelous in our eyes.
summary: Black Voices has pride itself in its roots in faith, family, and fellowship. Along with performances in the greater Lexington area, BV also does community service throughout the year, satisfying our mission to minister through song and service.

name: Alpha Chi Omega
description:
Alpha Chi Omega is a women’s organization dedicated to empowering real, strong women to reach their potential through a lifelong experience of sisterhood, service and leadership development. This is a place where real, strong women find connection, a community of support and opportunities to grow among those who encourage them to chase their passions and value them for who they really are— and want to become. Alpha Chi Omega, a member of the National Panhellenic Conference (NPC), has more than 140 collegiate chapters, 170 alumnae chapters and 300,000 sisters who have proudly worn the lyre badge since our founding in 1885. Alpha Chi Omega believes in fostering a deep sense of community where women can share diverse viewpoints and beliefs, excel academically and demonstrate thoughtful leadership and exploration. Our members are inspired to connect, lead, grow and serve.
 
Alpha Chi Omega is also known as a trailblazer in defining and advocating for the sorority experience. We’ve been a model for other sororities and were the first to adopt board governance, create programming on relationships and speak out about domestic violence on campuses nationwide. As our brand promise states, we are real, strong women working hard to change the conversation that too often stereotypes organizations like ours and the women who join them. Alpha Chi Omega is about being genuine. It’s about strength. It’s about taking on the issues and challenges that help our members grow into women who will change the world.
summary: Alpha Chi Omega is about empowering you to live as your true self, shaping the future through the powerful, transformative and everlasting connection to reach your potential and to be the real, strong woman you are meant to be.

name: Kentucky 4 Paws for Ability
description:
Thank you for your interest in 4 Paws for Ability at UK! If you are interested in collaborating with us on an event please email uk4pawsevent@gmail.com or if you would like more information/have questions or concerns please email uk4paws@gmail.com!

If you are interested in joining our club, please read all of the following information:

4 Paws for Ability is a non-profit organization based out of Xenia, Ohio that breeds, trains, and places service dogs to children and veterans with disabilities. These dogs begin training the day they are born, and we as college students are lucky enough to get to be a part of that training! We are considered volunteer trainers for the dogs. Our main job is to socialize the dogs as much as possible! Socialization is a huge part of the training process for service dogs. As trainers, we take our dogs everywhere with us so that they become familiar with all different people, surroundings, and environments. We also train our dogs in basic obedience and ensure that they always behave in a respectful manner. Our end goal as volunteer trainers is to produce confident, well-behaved pups who can handle any environment.

 

We have two different volunteer training positions that you can participate in. You can choose to be a primary handler, or a sitter. Primary handlers have full responsibility for the dog during the time they have with their dog. The dog lives with the primary handler and goes everywhere possible with them. 

However, as you might imagine, there are certain times when a primary handler cannot bring their dog with them wherever they are going. The primary handler may work, have a lab, be going out of town, or just need a break from the dog; that is where sitters come into play. Sitters are an extremely important aspect of our organization. A puppy sitter is someone who will watch a dog when a primary handler is unable to. There are daytime sitters and night time sitters. A daytime sitter is someone who only watches dogs for a few hours during the day. A night time sitter is someone who is approved to watch the dog overnight. To be a nighttime sitter or a primary handler, you must have your landlord sign a form verifying that they are aware you will be housing a service dog in training for a few days. This form will be sent to you once you complete the application. We strongly strongly suggest you become a sitter before applying to become a primary handler. Sitting allows you to get insight into the responsibilities that come with having a service dog in training. The job of caring for a service dog in training is often under-estimated; it takes patience, dedication, and a large time commitment.

 

As a student at UK, you have two options once you become an approved handler. You may choose to join the club, commonly referred to as “UK 4 Paws”, or you can choose to be a non-affiliated solo handler. All handlers have access to the Lexington Handlers Facebook page and the Lexington Handlers GroupMe chat as a way of communication to connect fosters and sitters. Club members at UK will also be added to the UK Handlers Facebook Group and UK Handlers GroupMe chats. These pages contain tons of advice and a close-knit community of student handlers. Club members also have access to the exec team for advice and guidance, as well as an invitation to all club events such as dorm programs, socialization outings, and club parties!

Club members do have requirements to meet in order to keep their status. As a new member, you are required to attend a Club New Member Meeting in the month you join. (Example: If you join October 6, you are required to go to the October Club New Member Meeting.) These are held by our Recruitment Coordinator. They go over everything about the club and 4 Paws in general. This is your time to get acclimated and ask questions! The Club New Member Meeting is only required once when you first join the club. Besides that meeting, all club members must attend a minimum of one club meeting per semester, these occur on the last Sunday of every month at 6pm. At club meetings, important information is passed along from 4 Paws headquarters to handlers at UK, and we discuss upcoming events and opportunities for the pups and their handlers. Primary handlers are required to get 4 "points", and secondary handlers are required to get 2 "points". “Points” may be acquired by attending meetings, fundraisers, events, parties, etc. Lastly, members are required to pay dues of $15 once a semester! To view more information about our club, review this presentation: https://www.canva.com/design/DAGo3SybT60/9VVG1fFaX8I7Zjs_zM_aiQ/edit?utm_content=DAGo3SybT60&utm_campaign=designshare&utm_medium=link2&utm_source=sharebutton

 

 

To begin the process of becoming a volunteer trainer, you must first fill out an application. The link to the application can be found by clicking this link: Apply to be a Volunteer Trainer. Please give plenty of time for your application to be reviewed; there has been a high volume of applications recently so the process is taking slightly longer. You will need to complete a housing verification form and attach it to your application if you live in an on campus dorm. You can download and complete that with this link: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1t7-OekbfRHF8qXaQJHVgzU7xdkhu9_0uAqx78B13_e0/edit?usp=sharing If you live in the dorms on UK campus, you can still be a handler! This form will just need to be signed by your Resident Advisor/Director and you can submit pictures of your dorm room. The only stipulation with living in the dorms is that any dog you bring into the dorms must be fully vaccinated. This usually happens around 16 weeks of age. Once your application has been approved, you must attend an orientation at the 4 Paws for Ability facility in Xenia, Ohio. It is about a 2.5 hour drive from Lexington, and you will be responsible for traveling to 4 Paws to complete the orientation. This orientation is mandatory, and typically lasts 2-3 hours. You will receive an email with details from a member of 4 Paws staff with a sign up for this orientation and other steps that must be completed as well.

If you choose to proceed with the application process and join the club for 4 Paws at UK, please fill out this form: htps://forms.gle/4QGzMPk1inCw3Xi9A upon completion of your orientation to be added to the appropriate Facebook Groups and GroupMe chats.
summary: The goal of 4 Paws at UK is to train and socialize service dogs in training while building connections with other students on campus.

name: Order of Omega
description: To recognize those fraternity men and women who have attained a high standard of leadership in interfraternity activities, to encourage them to continue along this line, and to inspire others to strive for similar conspicuous attainment; To unite outstanding fraternity men and women in an organization which will help to mold the sentiment of the institution on questions of local and intercollegiate fraternity affairs; To bring together members of the faculty, alumni, and student members of the institution’s fraternities and sororities on a basis of mutual interest, understanding and helpfulness; To help create an atmosphere where ideas and issues can be discussed openly across fraternity and sorority lines and to help work out solutions.
summary: To recognize those fraternity men and women who have attained a high standard of leadership in interfraternity activities, to encourage them to continue along this line, and to inspire others to strive for similar conspicuous attainment;To unite outsta...

name: University of Kentucky Solar Car Team
description: The University of Kentucky Solar Car team is a student-led organization that operates on the campus of the University of Kentucky in Lexington, KY. The team was formed in 1999 and is one the largest active student organizations on campus. With a large and active student body, the team works during the school year to finance, design and build solar powered electric vehicles which are used to compete in competitions organized and hosted by the Innovators Educational Foundation (IEF) such as the American Solar Challenge and the Formula Sun Grand Prix. Additionally the team participates in outreach events promoting higher education and sustainability.
summary: The University of Kentucky Solar Car Team finances, designs, builds, and races competitive solar-powered electric vehicles for the American Solar Challenge while promoting sustainability.

name: Every Nation Campus
description:
We are a multi-ethnic, church-based campus ministry that values devotion, diversity, and discipleship. We are Christ-centered, Spirit-empowered, and socially responsible. We seek to reach the UK campus, to reach the city of Lexington, and to touch the world. 

Check out our host church, Every Nation Lexington Church, at https://everynationlexington.org/.

For more information on Every Nation and its Campus Ministries, visit http://www.everynation.org.

Follow Every Nation Campus at UK (ENC-UK) on Instagram!: @ENC_LEXINGTON

Follow Every Nation Lexington Church on Instagram!: @EVERYNATIONLEX

Follow Every Nation Campus North America on Instagram!: @EVERYNATIONNA

Why Campus Ministry?

If we reach the campus, we reach future leaders.

Historically, the university campus has been a seedbed of culture shapers and world leaders—sometimes for good, sometimes for evil. If we reach them with the gospel now, they will one day change the world for Jesus.

If we reach the campus, we reach families.

Statistically, people are most open to the gospel during their time at university, and with every student reached, we see the potential to reach the entire family with the gospel.

If we reach the campus, we reach the world.

Culturally, university campuses are becoming more and more diverse, giving us a unique opportunity to reach the nations with the gospel by reaching out to international students in our cities.

For more information, please contact our president, Lillian Maxwell.
summary: We are a multi-ethnic, church-based campus ministry that values devotion, diversity, and discipleship. We seek to reach the UK campus, to reach the city of Lexington, and to touch the world.

name: Student Health Advisory Council
description:
Student Health Advisory Council, or SHAC, is an active group of students who work with the University Health Service to provide important consumer/student input.  SHAC makes recommendations to UHS and to the administration of the University relating to the Health Service budget, the student health insurance plan, recruitment of Health Services professional staff, health education, and any health related matter of interest to UK students.

To apply to join the Council, please click here and complete the application linked in the Google Form.
summary: SHAC is an active group of students who work with the University Health Service to provide important consumer/student input and makes recommendations to UHS and to the administration of the University.

name: Kappa Delta
description: Kappa Delta Sorority is made up of women dedicated to bettering their campus and community around them by offering their time to community service activities, serving as student leaders on campus, and excelling in their various academic programs. They have a strong sisterhood that holds their values that were set out long ago dear to them and strives to live out those values daily.
summary: Kappa Delta Sorority is made up of women dedicated to bettering their campus and community around them by offering their time to community service activities, serving as student leaders on campus, and excelling in their various academic programs.

name: Tau Beta Sigma
description: Tau Beta Sigma is a co-educational national honorary band sorority dedicated to serving college and band programs. The sorority operates primarily as a student service and leadership recognition society whose chief aim is to assist the Director of Bands in developing the leadership and enthusiasm that they require of their band. Our goals are not only to provide the band with organized and concentrated service activities, but to give our membership valid and wholesome experiences in organization, leadership, and social contacts.
summary: Tau Beta Sigma is an honorary band service sorority that strives to serve the University of Kentucky Bands and the local community.

Anyone interested in joining can contact our President, Hadley Hill by email hadley.hill@uky.edu.
summary: Agricultural Education Society is an organization for students of all majors who are passionate about sharing the story of agriculture, serving affiliated youth organizations, and developing as professionals.

Anyone interested in education or becoming a middle school teacher is welcome to join. BCMLA will have several meetings and speakers over the course of the year and everyone is welcome to attend.
summary: Bluegrass Collegiate Middle Level Association is a student organization at the University of Kentucky that helps future middle school teachers become amazing educators.

summary: AIChE is the American Institute of Chemical Engineers chapter for the University of Kentucky. We are a group of students and staff striving towards building a community specifically for Chemical Engineering students through professional development...

description: The purpose of the SBA is to institute and support programs from within the College of Law for the common benefit of SBA members, the College of Law, and others; to provide a common and collective voice of the student body in relations with faculty; and to maintain an atmosphere in all areas of College activity wherein the maximum respect is secured for students, the College, and the legal profession.
summary: The purpose of the SBA is to institute and support programs from within the College of Law for the common benefit of SBA members, the College of Law, and others; and to provide a common and collective voice of the student body in relations with faculty.

name: Phi Kappa Tau
description:
The brothers of the Kappa Chapter of Phi Kappa Tau at the University of Kentucky are especially known for our academic and philanthropic achievements within the UK Greek system. Our select group of gentlemen has held positions in various UK student organizations and influential positions in their respective communities. Our brotherhood has many honorable values through our generosity, mutual ambition to succeed, and a shared belief in fairness in all of our endeavors. We constantly strive to create admirable projects and achieve our goals while instilling the spirit of brotherhood in all that we accomplish. On average, our fraternity holds around 100 members annually. We value our size as it allows us to maintain a fraternity that values each of its members as an individual and is highly selective of its members. Each year we interview hundreds of men only to select the best gentlemen on campus. This has allowed our fraternity to include an admirable group of gentlemen.

Our associate members are allowed to quickly ascend to higher-ranking positions within the fraternity and on campus due to our size. Phi Tau values its scholarship program and its core brotherhood values above all. We instill these principles within each of our members, which in turn has created a successful and established fraternity. The mission of Phi Kappa Tau is to champion a lifelong commitment to brotherhood, learning, ethical leadership and exemplary character. The vision of Phi Kappa Tau is to be recognized as a leadership organization that binds men together and challenges them to improve their campuses and the world.
summary: The Kappa Chapter of Phi Kappa Tau brothers at the University of Kentucky are known for our academic and philanthropic achievements within the UK Greek system.

name: Panhellenic Association
description: The Panhellenic Association is the largest women's organization on campus with over four thousand members. The Panhellenic Council serves as the governing body for fifteen national sororities at the University of Kentucky. Our main goal is to unite sorority women together to focus on common goals and to create a network of support for chapters and members. We provide interactive programs and activities for sorority women and all University of Kentucky students. Every sorority woman belongs to the Panhellenic Association immediately upon pledging. Two delegates from each chapter serve on the Panhellenic Council. From this Council, Panhellenic elects officers who plan and support Panhellenic Association activities.
summary: The Panhellenic Association is the second largest organization on campus with over two thousand members. The Panhellenic Council serves as the governing body for fifteen national sororities at the University of Kentucky. Our main goal is to unite soror...

name: UK Clay Target Team
description: We are an entirely student-led organization, dedicated to the promotion of shotgun sports to people of all ages. Any student at UK are welcome to participate with the UK Clay Target Team, regardless of what your experience level is. In fact, many of our best shooters had never fired a gun before joining the team. We also have a varsity squad that travels to various competitions across the country every year. If you are interested in joining, reach out to our team's President, Mason Duran. (815) 953 4128
summary: We are an entirely student-led organization, dedicated to the promotion of shotgun sports to people of all ages. Any student at UK is welcome to participate with the UK Clay Target Team, regardless of their experience with competitive shooting.

name: Phi Lambda Sigma

summary: The mission of Phi Lambda Sigma is to support pharmacy leadership commitment by recognizing leaders and fostering leadership development.

summary: The BAE Student Branch at the University of Kentucky is an organization for the professional advancement of students enrolled in the Biosystems and Agricultural Engineering program at the University of Kentucky.

summary: The Dairy Club provides students with an interest in the dairy industry opportunities to interact with individuals in the field. We are open to all majors and you do not have to have a dairy background to join!

We work to better our member chapters as well as their individual members through our Pillars of Excellence. These are: Scholarship & Academics, Service & Involvement, Leadership & Development, Brotherhood & Community and Health & Wellbeing.
summary: The Kentucky Interfraternity Council (IFC) is the governing body for 20 recognized social fraternities at the University of Kentucky.

description: Combined AWWA/WEA student chapter branched under the Kentucky Tennessee sections of AWWA/WEA. Our organization is dedicated to helping students approach the water industry and promote the common professional interests of its members and to further the purposes of the Chapter through not-for-profit activities.
summary: Combined American Water Works Association and Water Environment Federation student chapter under the Kentucky Tennessee sections of AWWA/WEA. This organization is dedicated to helping students approach the water industry.

name: Phi Sigma Rho
description: Phi Sigma Rho is social sorority specifically for women and nonbinary people pursuing degrees in STEM-related fields, including agricultural & medical biotechnology, biochemistry, biology, chemistry, computer science, environmental & sustainability studies, mathematics, neuroscience, and physics. Phi Sigma Rho prides itself on teaching it's members how to find balance between social and professional aspects of life. We participate in social events throughout the semester including sisterhood retreats and formals. We also host our own philanthropy events and participate in those hosted by other Greek organizations. Through the emphasis of our core values of friendship, scholarship, and encouragement, we help our sisters to grow. If you have any questions, please visit our website or email an officer.
summary: Phi Sigma Rho is a social sorority specifically for women and nonbinary people pursuing degrees in STEM-related fields. We are a member of the United Greek Council.

name: National Association of Black Journalists
description: The National Association of Black Journalists at the University of Kentucky is a group that has been dedicated to the advancement of minorities in media on the UK campus and in the Lexington, KY community for more than 12 years. The UK chapter abides by the NABJ constitution as well as the NABJ bylaws and believes in its mission to: Strengthening ties among black journalists; Sensitizing all media to the importance of fairness in the workplace for black journalists; Expanding job opportunities and recruiting activities for veteran, young and aspiring black journalists, while providing continued professional development and training; Increasing the number of black journalists in management positions and encouraging black journalists to become entrepreneurs; Fostering an exemplary group of professionals that honors excellence and outstanding achievements by black journalists, and outstanding achievement in the media industry as a whole, particularly when it comes to providing balanced coverage of the black community and society at large; Working with high schools and colleges to identify and encourage black students to become journalists, and to diversify faculties and related curriculum; and Providing informational and training services to the general public. The National Association of Black Journalists (NABJ) is an organization of journalists, students, and media-related professionals that provides quality programs and services to and advocates on behalf of black journalists worldwide. Join us and be a part of a group dedicated to growing you and the professional media industry.
summary: The National Association of Black Journalists at the University of Kentucky is a group that has been dedicated to the advancement of minorities in media on the UK campus and in Lexington, KY community for more than 12 years.

name: CARES First-Generation
description: CARES First-Generation (Center for Academic Resources and Enrichment Services and First-Generation) provides an empowering community that guides and inspires all students to successfully complete their academic journey, cultivates their sense of belonging, and prepares them to become leaders on the global stage. In addition, we support William C. Parker Scholars and First-Generation students, serving as a key resource for their academic success, personal growth, and professional development.
summary: The Center for Academic Resources and Enrichment Services (CARES) First-Generation ​are committed to providing quality academic support services and programming with compassion and empathy for every student in a welcoming and supportive community.

name: Ultimate Frisbee Club Team
description: Kentucky's Premier Ultimate Frisbee Club. From a handful of dudes in 2008 to two competitive teams playing up and down the East Coast. We believe in athletic pursuit, competitive spirit, and fair play. We take people of all skill levels from never touching a disc to those that have been playing since freshman year of high school. If you are interested or want to know more; contact the team email, text one of the officers, or just show up to a practice. We look forward to seeing you.
summary: Kentucky's Mens Ultimate Club Team

name: Kinesiology Health Promotion Club
description: The purpose of this organization is to provide health, wellness, and physical fitness information to the UK community. The club also serves as an academic and networking resource for those interested in the fields of Kinesiology and Health Promotion. The club is open to all those who want to participate! Please join the club on BBNvolved before joining the GroupMe so that we can keep an accurate record of members! https://groupme.com/join_group/91378202/h3qpt8lq
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide health, wellness and physical fitness information and resources to the UK community.  The club also serves as an academic and networking resource for those interested in the field of Kinesiology.
