name: Alpha Phi Omega
description: Under the guiding principles of leadership, friendship, and service, Alpha Phi Omega provides service to the community by donating time and effort to various organizations and causes. We strive to help each individual member develop leadership skills, experience friendship on many levels, and provide service to others. Our mission is to prepare campus and community leaders through service.
summary: Alpha Phi Omega is a national co-ed service fraternity founded on the principles of Leadership, Friendship, and Service.

name: Prolife Wildcats
description:
Prolife Wildcats works both on and off campus to promote the pro-life cause in all its forms. Students for Life works to end all the practices of our society that threaten the sanctity of life and to support those facing crisis pregnancies and those suffering after an abortion. We work to spread an attitude that respects the dignity of human life from the moment of conception. We believe that who your parents, your location, your socioeconomic status, your race, your gender, or anything else about you don't determine how valuable you are. Visit Students for Life's website below to learn more about our mission to stop the evil of abortion!

Check out our promo video! 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3vBGR5ZsAsA&list=PL_BexxCdfyL2G_1kqEiJZ_3JewT6HCPs7&index=27
summary: Pro-Life Wildcats is a group of individuals who work both on and off campus to promote the Pro-Life cause in all its forms! We have volunteer, travel, and leadership opportunities. Join us in our mission to serve women and save babies!

name: Best Buddies
description: Best Buddies® is an international nonprofit organization dedicated to establishing a global volunteer movement that creates opportunities for one-to-one friendships, integrated employment, and leadership development for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD). Here at UK, we focus mainly on the friendship aspect of things, pairing up students with individuals with disabilities from the Lexington area. We get together on a monthly basis to hang out like a normal group of friends, facilitating genuine relationships and interaction. In the process, we build communication/social skills and improve the overall quality of life for our members both with and without disabilities. We participate in community outings, attend various UK sporting events, and collaborate and serve alongside other organizations on campus. We hope to create lifelong friendships and spread inclusion here at the University of Kentucky.
summary: Best Buddies® is an international nonprofit organization dedicated to establishing a global volunteer movement that creates opportunities for one-to-one friendships, integrated employment, and leadership development for people with intellectual and dev...

name: UK Connect
description: UK Connect is a collaboration between the Robinson Scholars Program, First Generation Initiatives, Student Support Services (SSS) and The Center for Academic Resources and Enrichment Services (CARES). This program is designed to increase the persistence of students who are seeking or may need additional academic support and enrichment opportunities.
summary: UK Connect is a collaboration between the Robinson Scholars Program, First Generation Initiatives, Student Support Services (SSS) and The Center for Academic Resources and Enrichment Services (CARES). This program is designed to increase the persistenc...

name: Kentucky Journal of Equine, Ag & Natural Resources Law
description: The Kentucky Journal of Equine, Agriculture, & Natural Resources Law is a multi-disciplinary journal of law, science, and policy published annually by the University of Kentucky College of Law. The Journal is edited entirely by students of the College of Law. As a forum for articles by practitioners, academicians, policy-makers, and other professionals throughout the United States and abroad, the Journal welcomes original manuscripts focusing on the legal, policy, and ethical issues related to the environment, natural resources, land use, and energy. Shorter discussion pieces, descriptions of creative solutions to persistent problems, and commentary on policy and politics are also suitable for publication in the Journal. Each issue also includes notes written by Journal staff members. KJEANRL was named one of the Top 100 Law Journals of 2010 by Washington & Lee University School of Law.
summary: The Kentucky Journal of Equine, Agriculture, & Natural Resources Law is a multi-disciplinary journal of law, science, and policy published annually by the University of Kentucky College of Law.

name: National Association for Music Education Collegiate Chapter
description: NAfME Collegiate exists to promote music education by providing learning opportunities for music education students, helping Kentucky music teachers, and facilitating professional networking.
summary: NAfME Collegiate exists to promote music education by providing learning opportunities for music education students, helping Kentucky music teachers, and facilitating professional networking.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description: Guided by five core values, Alpha Kappa Psi focuses on providing ethical leadership and professional development. Through educational programs, community service, and a hands-on application of formal education, Alpha Kappa Psi students use the business skills they are taught in the classroom to acquire life skills such as the art of compromise, consensus building, project management, interviewing, and networking. The fraternity has grown to become the oldest and largest professional business fraternity in the world. With nearly 240,000 initiated members and more than 200 chapters, it is among the 20 largest college fraternities. The men and women of AKPsi reflect the world’s diversity and are united by a common interest in business. That being said, the skills that Alpha Kappa Psi are applicable to anyone's career, and the fraternity welcomes students from any major. The UK chapter of Alpha Kappa Psi currently represents men and women from a huge array of academic backgrounds. While we provide our members with a number of intangible skills that will be useful in the workforce, we are also a tight knit group of friends and that brotherhood is as beneficial as the interviewing and networking skills that we gain. Alpha Kappa Psi is educational, but it is also a lot of fun! Feel free to contact us for more details at akpsi.president@gmail.com.
summary: Guided by five core values, Alpha Kappa Psi focuses on providing ethical leadership and professional development. Through educational programs, community service, and a hands-on application of formal education, Alpha Kappa Psi develops business leaders.

name: Graphite Creative Writing Association
description:
Graphite was founded in 2013 as a collaborative Gaines Center Jury project by UK students Katie Cross and Kendra Sanders with the help of the creative writing professor Julia Johnson, who became the organization's first faculty advisor. Graphite went through a brief recession after the emergence of COVID-19, but saw a massive revitalization in activity in 2024 after a fresh team of passionate writers took the club to new heights. Since then, Graphite has continually acted as a hub for students interested in creative writing on campus; hosting and sponsoring a variety of meetings, workshops, events, fundraisers, and publications to help advance Lexington's literary community.

Graphite is also the team behind the production of The Deposit, the new literary magazine at UK solely produced by undergraduate students with a heavy emphasis on the creation of physical copies as opposed to digital distribution. 

Graphite's meetings for the Spring semester of 2026 will occur every week on Thursdays at 6:00 PM in Room 1245 on the twelfth floor of the Patterson Office Tower.
summary: Graphite is the University of Kentucky's only student organization dedicated entirely to creative writing. It aims to provide an outlet to learn about and engage with creative writing for students on campus through meetings, events, and publications.

name: Beta Alpha Psi
description: Beta Alpha Psi represents scholastic achievement as well as dedication to building a strong foundation for a future career in Accounting and the Financial Services. We partner with firms across the financial services industry to provide exclusive networking events as well as professional development opportunities. We have a proud history of connecting our members with meaningful employment post-graduation. We strive to instill strong communication and professionalism skills while working to achieve the goals of the organization as well as each member's individual educational and career goals. We work to create a valuable network of connections that our members can draw upon throughout their careers.
summary: Beta Alpha Psi represents scholastic achievement as well as dedication to building a strong foundation for careers in Accounting & Financial Services. We partner with firms to provide exclusive networking events as well as professional development.

name: Graduate Appalachian Research Community
description: The Graduate Appalachian Research Community (GARC) is a student organization that promotes and supports interdisciplinary research about Appalachia. The group's mission is to foster a supportive community in which graduate students from across the university can collaborate, present research, discuss obstacles and successes, and more. We sponsor a yearly research symposium and arts showcase for student researchers and artists that typically takes place in February. The symposium/showcase is open to students of all disciplines and all academic levels. We also, in addition to this event, organize lectures, film screenings, social outings, and such throughout the year.
summary: The Graduate Appalachian Research Community (GARC) is a student organization that promotes and supports interdisciplinary research about Appalachia.

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics - Paducah
description: We are the University of Kentucky Paducah Chapter of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA). Our Chapter is comprised of a variety of undergraduate students all participating on an extracurricular basis. We focus primarily on the annual AIAA hosted Design/Build/Fly (DBF) competition, while participating in campus events along the way.
summary: We are a chapter of the AIAA organization located on the UK campus in Paducah, KY. We provide an outlet for those interested in the aerospace and aeronautics field by participating in events like Design/Build/Fly, research conferences, and STEM outreach.

name: Orthodox Christian Fellowship
description: We are a young adult group dedicated to growing closer to Christ and each other through our 4 core themes. These themes are fellowship, education, worship, and service. We strive to enact all these values in our meetings and lives each day.
summary: We are a ministry of the Orthodox Christian Church at the University of Kentucky, promoting fellowship among Orthodox and non-Orthodox students on campus.

name: Student Government Association
description:
The University of Kentucky Student Government Association (UKSGA) represents all undergraduate, graduate and professional students enrolled at the University. UKSGA exists to increase student influence over academic policy; to provide necessary student services; to protect and expand student substantive and procedural rights; and to better represent the student body in relations with the faculty, administration, Board of Trustees, and the Commonwealth of Kentucky. Membership for this page is restricted to SGA members for internal operations. To stay up to date on SGA events, initiatives, and programs please continue to check here, social media, or at uksga.org! 

Go Cats

description:
The Trew Friends Club at UK works to raise awareness of the importance of organ donation among individuals of all ages. Trew Friends seeks to increase the number of registered organ, eye, and tissue donors. Trew Friends supports education programs and medical research in the areas of transplantation, organ donation, and children's health. 

 

Join Group Me: https://groupme.com/join_group/111941870/n5IW2hIm

name: Pi Kappa Phi
description: The Eta Tau chapter of Pi Kappa Phi was rechartered at the University of Kentucky in 2024 and has set out to create leadership opportunities and lifelong brotherhood for our members. Our founders, alumni, and active members strive to exemplify leadership in the classroom, on the athletic field, in campus politics, and within the community. Founded at The College of Charleston in 1904, Pi Kappa Phi has set out to define fraternity as a lifelong brotherhood of leaders. Pi Kappa Phi is the only Greek organization to own and operate its own philanthropy, The Ability Experience, which serves to create shared experiences between brothers of Pi Kappa Phi and people with disabilities.
summary: The Eta Tau chapter of Pi Kappa Phi sets out to create leadership opportunities and lifelong brotherhood for our members. Rechartering at UK in April 2024, we have established ourselves at UK and have are a united brotherhood of 80 members.

summary: WIM serves as an organization to support women in the mining industry through professional connections, outreach opportunities, and social events. We welcome anyone who supports that mission.

description: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. was founded Monday, January 13, 1913 on the campus of Howard University by 22 college educated women. The organization consists of college educated women committed to constructive development of its members and to public service with a primary focus on the black community. The Most Excellent Mu Epsilon Chapter was chartered Friday, May 9, 1975 by 13 dynamic women and was the first African American sorority chartered on the University of Kentucky's campus.
summary: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. was founded Monday, January 13, 1913 on the campus of Howard University by 22 college educated women. The organization consists of college educated women committed to constructive development of its members and to publi...

name: Kernel Press Club
description: The Kernel Press Club includes student publications The Kentucky Kernel and KRNL Lifestyle and Fashion. The Kernel is the University of Kentucky's independent student newspaper that publishes online daily and in print once a month with special sections. KRNL is a once-a-semester publication that features lifestyle and fashion content.
summary: The Kentucky Kernel is the University of Kentucky's independent student newspaper.

name: Anesthesiology Interest Group
description: The aim of our organization is to allow participating medical students the opportunity to discover more about anesthesiology, establish contacts within the department, and gain exposure to procedural skills frequently utilized by anesthesiologists. We host regular meetings throughout the year during which speakers (typically anesthesiology faculty) give talks about various aspects of the field of anesthesia. We also organize workshops demonstrating intubation technique, airway management, central line placement, lumbar puncture, and other procedural skills important to the practice of anesthesiology. At the end of the year, we assemble a panel of students who matched into an anesthesia residency program to host a Q&A session for students planning to apply.
summary: The aim of our organization is to allow participating medical students the opportunity to discover more about anesthesiology, establish contacts within the department, and gain exposure to procedural skills frequently utilized by anesthesiologists.

name: Pre-Dental Society
description: The Pre-Dental Society facilitates networking among pre-dental students and the development of relationships that last through dental school and beyond. One of our most important ways of bringing students together is through community outreach events that further member involvement in the community and with each other in dental and non-dental settings.
summary: This organization is for students interested in the dental field.

name: UK Club Softball
description:
This is our 12th year as a team and our 11th official year in the NCSA (National Club Softball Association). In our 2013-2014 season we were able to achieve first in our conference, third at the regional tournament (for which we got an at-large bid for World Series) and we placed in the top 12 at the World Series tournament in Columbus, Georgia. In our 2014-15 season we were able to earn second in our conference and ended with a winning record of 13-4. In the 2015-2016 season our team accomplished first in conference, held on to our national ranking, and went on to compete in the regional tournament where we fell to OSU and JMU. In the 2018-2019 season, we were able to host our very first tournament in Georgetown, KY in the spring. Our team placed second at the 9 team tournament we hosted. We won our conference, which gave us a berth to the regional tournament. At Regionals we placed 3rd. In the 2019-2020 season we were on track to make it to the regional tournament until the COVID-19 Pandemic hit. The Pandemic also cancelled our 2020-2021 season. We’re looking forward to building back a strong and inclusive team for the future seasons! 

We are looking for pitchers for our 2025-26 season!!! Please contact us to let us know if you are interested!
summary: The purpose of the Club Softball Team is to allow students who were involved in high school and travel fastpitch softball to continue playing while at the University of Kentucky. UK Club Softball is apart of the National Club Softball Association (NCSA).

name: Black Graduate & Professional Student Association
description: The Black Graduate and Professional Student Association (BGPSA) is the primary student organization designed to address the needs of underrepresented minorities in graduate and/or professional programs at the University of Kentucky, specifically, Black students. BGPSA is the only interdisciplinary organization of its kind. Networking and sharing ideas and resources across disciplines is the key to true innovation. BGPSA seeks to serve as a resource by facilitating leadership training, professional development, mentoring opportunities, community outreach, socializing opportunities and more! BGPSA membership is open to all graduate and professional students, faculty, and staff who are interested in supporting students of African descent in the pursuit of graduate and professional educational excellence. The BGPSA does not discriminate or limit its membership to any individual or group based on race, creed, color, nationality, ability or sexual preference.
summary: The Black Graduate and Professional Student Association (BGPSA)  is the primary student organization designed to address the needs of underrepresented minorities in graduate and/or professional programs at the University of Kentucky.

name: Gender Sexuality Alliance
description:
We are a group of LGBTQ* students and allies who meet to have discussions, promote equality and above all have fun. We meet at the LGBTQ* Resource Suite of the Student Center (Room A250) on Thursdays at 7:00 PM. We discuss topics ranging from sexual and mental health, to what is considered positive and negative representation in the media. We also host fun events like hiking and ice skating. We are first and foremost a community. A place for LGBTQ* students and allies to meet and find a sense of belonging. The door is always open, and you will always be welcome here.

 

Come to the dark side. Our instagram (where you can find our events for that week) is @uk_gsa.

Join our official discord! https://discord.gg/ghYTcCwXx5
summary: We are a community of LGBTQ* students and allies who meet to have discussions, promote equality and above all have fun. We meet at the LGBTQ Resource Suite of the Student Center (Room A250) on Thursdays at 7:00 PM.

name: University of Kentucky Western Equestrian Team
description:
The UK Western Equestrian Team shows in association with the Intercollegiate Horse Show Association (IHSA). https://www.ihsainc.com/ 

The IHSA promotes competition for riders of all skill levels, who compete individually and as teams at regional, semi-final and national levels.

The association was founded on the principle that any college student should have the opportunity to participate in equestrian competition regardless of financial status, gender or riding level. The IHSA emphasizes learning and sportsmanship and fosters teamwork. IHSA welcomes riders in their first years of riding and students with show experience. IHSA eliminates the expense of owning and transporting horses and puts competition within financial reach of college and university students.
summary: The UKWET was founded on promoting the horse industry and to encourage riders of all levels to improve their horsemanship and reining skills through lessons and shows against other IHSA schools.

name: Global Brigades
description:
Global Brigades is the world’s largest student-led global health and sustainable development organization. Since 2004, Global Brigades has mobilized tens of thousands of university students and professionals through skill-based programs that partner with community members to improve quality of life in under-resourced regions while respecting local culture. 

Global Brigades is an international non-profit that uniquely implements a holistic model to meet a community’s health and economic goals. Our model systematically builds community ownership and collaboratively executes programs with the end goal of sustainably transitioning to a relationship of impact monitoring.

WATCH THE VIDEO 

Our mission is to empower volunteers and under-resourced communities to resolve global health and economic disparities and inspire all involved to collaboratively work towards an equal world. Our vision is to improve equality of life by igniting the largest student-led social responsibility movement on the planet.

Our Global Medical Chapter provides primary health care services to small, under-resourced, rural communities with limited to no access to medical care.  Physicians provide patient consults and prescribe treatment for acute illnesses. Students work in triage, shadow physicians, provide education workshops, and help to distribute medicine in the pharmacy.
summary: Global Brigades is the world’s largest student-led global health and sustainable development organization. It is an international movement of students and medical professionals working alongside local communities to implement sustainable health systems.

name: UK Women's Club Lacrosse
shortName: University of Kentucky Women's Club Lacrosse
description: The women's lacrosse program is a club sport at the University of Kentucky. Tryouts are held at the beginning of the fall semester each year, usually the first or second week of September. We typically play a couple of games against local teams, such as Transylvania and Asbury, during the Fall. Our primary season is in the Spring, where we play at home and away tournaments with many universities, including Tennessee, Ohio State University, the University of Illinois, Indiana University, etc. We hold practice each semester on Tuesdays and Thursdays at the club sports fields. Practice times may vary, but are typically 6:00 pm-8:00 pm.

To the campus community and the world, we are valued citizens deeply committed to making a difference where we live. "The purpose of this sorority shall be to unite its members in the bonds of sincere and lasting friendship, to stimulate one another in the pursuit of knowledge, to promote the moral and social culture of its members, and to develop plans for guidance and unity in action; objects worthy of the highest aim and purpose of associated effort. The purpose of this sorority shall be advanced through the National Convention, the National Council, and the college and alumnae chapters."

name: Kappa Alpha Theta
description: Founded in 1870 at Indiana Asbury (now DePauw University) in Greencastle, Indiana, Kappa Alpha Theta is the first Greek-letter Fraternity known among women. In creating Kappa Alpha Theta, our founders were leading the way for women’s groups and for women in higher education. The Gamma Iota chapter at the University of Kentucky embodies their founding principles and strives for their members to grow as individuals, aim for excellence, accept diversity, and create lasting friendships. Our colors are black and gold, and our symbol is the kite.
summary: Kappa Alpha Theta was founded in 1870 as the first Greek-letter fraternity for women. Since that day, Theta membership has challenged and supported generations of women, and we have inspired one another to be our best selves.

We create change by bringing young people together and creating safe spaces for students of all political and ideological stripes to have honest conversations about drugs and drug policy. Founded in 1998, SSDP comprises thousands of members at hundreds of campuses in countries around the globe.

name: Horse Racing Club
description: Our mission is to educate and expose members to the horse racing industry within Lexington, KY. Through educational meetings, guest speakers by industry professionals, and engaging social events, we aim to foster an environment where members can give their equine aspirations direction. Being a part of the Horse Racing Club, members are able to learn about the equine racing industry, develop interpersonal skills, and connect with potential employers. The Horse Racing Club is committed to creating memorable experiences, inspiring lifelong passion for horses, and promoting professional growth.
summary: The Horse Racing Club at UK is an entirely student run organization that is dedicated to the involvement of its members. We are committed to educating students of any major about the equine racing industry in Lexington, KY.

name: Society of Women Engineers
description: SWE is a professional organization dedicated to stimulating women to achieve their full potential in careers as engineers and leaders, expanding the image of the engineering profession as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and demonstrating the value of diversity.
summary: SWE is dedicated to promoting equality between men and women in engineering.  We aim to encourage women, girls, and other minorities to pursue a career in engineering and technology and to help women and minorities become leaders.

name: Interprofessional Healthcare Residential Community
description:
The IHRC is a high-impact, living-learning program designed for students of any major who are interested in pursuing a career in healthcare.

 

Open to new and returning students. Apply via the UK Housing application.

name: Christian Student Fellowship
description:
CSF is a non-denominational campus ministry at the University of Kentucky. CSF isn’t a place. CSF isn’t a program. CSF is everywhere on campus—although we do have a building where we sometimes hang out. It’s a million different places and a million different people. It’s downtown serving the homeless. Climbing at the Johnson Center. A conversation about Jesus ’til 2am. It’s wherever people are interacting with each other and with God.

 

CSF is mainly staffed by recent UK grads who take the first year or two post-graduation to give back to the UK community by working full time at CSF. A few long-term staff help guide and invest into these younger staff members who in turn pour into our student leaders as they reach out to campus with the good news and good life of Jesus.
summary:
Relationally Connecting the people of UK to a Relational God.
www.ukcsf.org

name: Collegiate 4-H
description:
Join the Movement with Collegiate 4-H!
Ready to lead, serve, and make a difference? Collegiate 4-H is your chance to dive into meaningful community involvement while building leadership skills and lifelong friendships. Whether you’ve grown up in 4-H or are just discovering it, we’d love to have you join our dynamic team!

We meet monthly for hands-on service projects, engaging workshops, and fun fellowship. As a member, you’ll also play a vital role in supporting 4-H events at the county, regional, and state levels helping current 4-H youth grow and thrive through mentorship, leadership, and volunteerism.

Come be part of something bigger. Collegiate 4-H is more than a club it’s a community with purpose!
summary: Collegiate 4-H is an extension of the 4-H organization and is a continuation of 4-H membership and service through college years. The club welcomes everyone from past members to any new interested students. We focus on leadership and service activities.

description: Sigma Alpha Epsilon's eight founding fathers started Sigma Alpha Epsilon in 1856 at the University of Alabama. Our mission is to  advance the highest standards of friendship, scholarship, leadership and service for our members throughout life. The Kentucky Epsilon chapter at the University of Kentucky was chartered in 1900 and since our inception, have initiated over 2,500 brothers. Our chapter has won 49 Intramural championships and have many prominent alumni including William T. Young, Joe Craft, and Chris Sullivan.
summary: Our Eight Founding Fathers started Sigma Alpha Epsilon in 1856 at the University of Alabama with the mission of advancing the highest standards of friendship, scholarship, leadership and service for our members throughout life.

Any student who has expressed interest in elementary education as a major is eligible to become a member.

description: The SAC is a free clinic run by medical students from the University of Kentucky. Our mission is to assume a leadership role in improving the health and well-being of the uninsured adult population in Lexington by providing free medical services and increasing awareness of local healthcare resources. • All clinic staff—triagers, floor managers, interviewers, and physicians—are volunteers. • We see 8 adult patients per clinic night. • We have three patient exam rooms, and a pharmacy. • Free group smoking cessation classes are offered every Thursday beginning at 6:30pm.
summary: The SACUK is a free clinic run by medical students from the University of Kentucky.  Our mission is to assume a leadership role in improving the health and well-being of the uninsured adult population in Lexington by providing free medical services and

name: Delta Sigma Phi
description:
Delta Sigma Phi is a national, not-for-profit Fraternity that’s helped young men become better for more than a century. Our great Fraternity helps and encourages our members to become better students, better leaders, better citizens, better professionals…better men.

Delta Sig was founded on December 10, 1899, at the City College of New York (CCNY), by Meyer Boskey and Charles A. Tonser Jr., who we identify as our primary founders. Our two founders brought a group of men together from various religious backgrounds which were unheard of.
The fraternity's mission is to "empower and encourage our members to become Better Men - men of courage, men of action, and men of excellence."

Delta Sigma Phi is a values-based membership organization that seeks to enrich the lives of our members and all mankind. We believe in—and built Vision 2025 around—our three historical values:

  • Culture | Encourage the pursuit of knowledge and intellectual development in our chapters and members.
  • Harmony | Commit to high standards of moral conduct and bettering ourselves and the world around us.
  • Friendship | Form lifelong bonds based on the values of our ritual and secured by honesty, integrity and care.

Delta Sigma Phi was the first fraternity to be founded on the basis of religious and ethnic acceptance. We continued being a fraternity of firsts when we:

1. Published our new member manual, The Gordian Knot in 1925 which was one of the first of its kind in the fraternity/sorority world.
2. Banned "Hell Week" in 1938 
3. First fraternity to include statements on the respect for human dignity and a policy regarding acceptance of members with HIV/AIDS in our governing documents. 
4. First fraternity to provide the fraternity experience to deaf students when we started a chapter at Cal State Northridge in 1984
5. First fraternity to ban Little Sister organizations, in recognizing the importance of women's equality and the value of women's groups as stand-alone organizations.
summary: Delta Sigma Phi is a national, not-for-profit Fraternity that's helped young men become better for more than a century. Founded on December 10, 1899.

name: University Christian Fellowship
description:
UCF is a non-denominational Christian campus ministry on the University of Kentucky campus. We seek to know Jesus and learn as his students and disciples, and to help others know him and the way, the truth, and the life in him too. Since 1974 UK students connected with the local Lexington Christian Fellowship, Trinity Christian Fellowship, and Emmanuel Christian Fellowship churches have generation after generation reached out on campus with the gospel of Jesus Christ our Lord and met together with one desire to know Jesus more and learn as his disciples and put his teachings into practice. 

 

     This year we invite all students from all backgrounds, and especially those who do not yet know Jesus, to a weekly Bible study on campus. We gather to open his Word, hear his teachings and the promises of God as his eager students, and study them and open our hearts to the power and infallible truth of the gospel of God. We are excited to meet and share the gospel of Jesus with those who do not yet know him, that they might believe in him and have life in his name, and are excited to meet those in his Kingdom who already know him and want to know him more and study him and his teachings together in joyous fellowship with fellow brethren.  

 

     For more information, contact samuel.hahn@uky.edu, or visit our Instagram page at https://www.instagram.com/_ucfky/.
summary: UCF is a non-denominational Christian campus ministry on the University of Kentucky campus. We seek to know Jesus and learn as his students and disciples, and to help others know him and the way, the truth, and the life in him too.

name: Patterson Journal of International Affairs
description:
The Patterson Journal of International Affairs (PJIA) is an online and print publication run by Patterson School students and graduates. As a registered student organization, PJIA strives to integrate with our UK and Lexington communities through meaningful analysis that informs local and global debates. Lexington is a small city with an international outlook. As such, there is a strong demand for local insights into international issues, and that is a niche in both the local market and the local intellectual community PJIA fills. Covering topics from international development to commerce to security and diplomacy, the PJIA  is the place to get prudent analysis on all of today's hot button issues.

To see their latest updates on the world or for submission guidelines, check out their website here: https://pattersonjournal.com/
summary: The Patterson Journal of International Affairs (PJIA) is an online and print publication run by Patterson School students and graduates that focuses on covering international occurrences and lending student insights to propel dialogue.

We meet once every month during each semester and have guest speakers including faculty from Kentucky's physical therapy program, Dr. Patrick Pabian and Dr. Nathan Johnson, as well as physical therapists in the community, rehab science professors, admission administrators and current physical therapy students. Our goal is to guide our members to become competitive candidates for PT school. We prepare our members by organizing meetings regarding school applications, resumes, GRE test prep, community service, internship opportunities and more. Be sure to follow us on our Instagram page for more information about meeting dates and times!
summary: We are a student association with open membership to any student interested in physical therapy. We meet once every month during each semester and have guest speakers including the director of the physical therapy program, Dr. Tony English, as well as ...

description: Chi Omega is a sisterhood that provides a network of friends and lifelong development for collegiate and alumnae members. Chi Omega is committed to sisterhood, scholarship, community service, campus involvement, career and personal development, and social activities. We host a yearly philanthropy event called Greek Sing in which all the proceeds go to the Make-A-Wish Foundation. So far, UK Chi Omega has raised over $1.96 million for the Make-A-Wish Foundation.  In 2019 we celebrated our 105 years of being on the University of Kentucky's campus, and in 2020 we celebrated 125 years of Chi Omega nationally!
summary: Chi Omega is a sisterhood that provides a network of friends and lifelong development for collegiate and alumnae members. Chi Omega is committed to, scholarship, community service, campus involvement, career & personal development, and social activities.

name: University of Kentucky Rugby Club
description: The University of Kentucky Men's Rugby Club is a highly competitive and dedicated team that has found significant success in recent years. The team's top squad competes for national championships, winning the 2023 and 2024 National Collegiate Rugby (NCR) D1AA National Championship and consistently ranking among the top teams in the country. The program is known for its discipline and strategic play, blending seasoned leadership with young talent to maintain its elite status. The club also has a "B" side, which offers younger or less experienced players a chance to develop their skills and compete in a structured environment. This provides a pathway for athletes to grow within the program. While most of the players on in the squad are recruited from across the country for rugby, the team welcomes athletes with an open mind and a willingness to work hard, fostering a community that values personal development alongside on-field success. 2024 National Champioship https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JCsSWOxzrPY 2023 National Championship https://youtu.be/aG66RPHzDyE?si=gx48qKXI4f7h3SOG Recruitment Video https://youtu.be/aG66RPHzDyE?si=gx48qKXI4f7h3SOG
summary: We practice 3 times a week (Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday) and have matches on the weekends. We travel and play against some of the best competition in the country. We are back-to-back D1AA National Champions (2023, 2024).

description:

The UK Student Chapter of AWM consist of any and all members of the University of Kentucky community who are interested in promoting and supporting women in mathematics. Our group hosts a number of events each semester. name: UK Club Gymnastics
description:
UK Club Gymnastics Info

General Info:

We are an intercollegiate and recreational gymnastics club that offer gymnastics to both the inexperienced and experienced. We practice from 4 to 6 hours a week in the gym, as well as weightlifting once a week. Although you do not have to come to every single practice it is highly recommended to gain new skills or to get old ones back and/or to compete. We practice at Excel Gymnastics.
Address: 700 S Keeneland Dr., Richmond, KY 40475
 
Competitions:
We compete only in the spring semester but competing is completely optional. You can choose to compete in one meet, all of the meets, or none at all. It's completely up to you! A list of potential meets will be sent out. We compete through NAIGC competition rules which can be found at https://naigc.org/ 
The club will provide team leotards to those who wish to compete. Merchandise such as T-shirts will be sold, as well as team jackets! Fundraising will be done and members are expected to help to be able to pay for carpooling, hotels, and meet fees. Competing is completely relaxed and super fun, whether you’ve competed for years or if it’s your first competition. There is very little pressure, and the goal is to have the best time possible!
 
 
Club Requirements:
The requirements to be in the club are short. You must be a UK student (undergrad or graduate), sign liability forms through Excel Gymnastics and the UK Club requirements, and pay the semester fee. This fee changes depending on how many people join the club, but it helps us rent the gym for the semester and is divided amongst the entire team. For example, with 30 people, everyone would only have to pay about $75. The more people, the cheaper it is!
 
Carpooling:
We do carpool for those who do not have a car on campus. If you need a ride, it isn’t a problem, just be sure to text in the GroupMe so someone can pick you up. If you are able to drive, parking at Excel is free.
 
Coaching:
While we do not have an official coach, many of the team members have coached before. We keep everyone safe by setting up drills for progressions and spotting when needed. However, the structure of practice is very individualized, and we expect everyone to only attempt skills they are capable of doing.
summary: We are an intercollegiate gymnastics team that welcomes anyone, whether you have 10 years of gymnastics experience or none! We travel to 3-4 competitions a year & competing is optional. Our goal is to create an inclusive environment while having fun!

name: Delta Gamma
description: Delta Gamma is an international women’s fraternity that continues to place a strong dedication on personal values and standards, academic excellence, leadership and service. Delta Gamma encourages each member to strive for excellence and offers the programming and support to help each member reach that commitment. Delta Gamma offers its members the experience of sharing the strong bonds of sisterhood for a lifetime.    Delta Gammas membership has grown to more than 142,000 women worldwide with 260 collegiate chapters and more than 250 alumnae groups across the United States and Canada. Delta Beta got started on UK's campus in the fall of 1962 when the existing sororities on campus were unable to accommodate the number of women wishing to pledge. Delta Gamma was selected as the chapter to be brought to UK's campus. Delta Gamma actives and alumnae from other Delta Gamma chapters came and assisted with recruitment. Delta Beta of Delta Gamma pledged its first group of outstanding women the following semester. Work began on our current house almost immediately and our current home at 450 Pennsylvania Avenue opened in 1963. The anchor can still be seen sitting in our front yard today. Since our founding on UK's campus we've received numerous awards such as the Deans cup, homecoming queen, and have had girls participate in student government as well as the equestrian team, track team, dance team, and cheerleading. Since our existence on this chapter we have been a part of nearly every student organization on campus.
summary: Delta Gamma is an international women’s fraternity that places a strong dedication on personal values & standards, academic excellence, leadership and service. DG offers its members the experience of sharing the strong bonds of sisterhood for a lifetime.

Interested in joining or want to learn more? Contact us at axs.uky@gmail.com

name: Phi Sigma Kappa
description: Being a part of the Greek community at the University of Kentucky since 1926 Phi Sigma has continuously worked to promote brotherhood, stimulate scholarship, and develop character. We are a lifelong brotherhood dedicated to the betterment of the individual, the university community, and our country. This is done by by giving our members opportunities to develop leadership skills, participate in service to others, achieve academic excellence, experience cultural diversity, and practice personal integrity. Phi Sig sets an example of true brotherhood not only in our relations with each other, but in our association with people everywhere. Complementing the mission of higher education, we seek to help our members to combine formal and informal learning experience; to more fully appreciate the importance of both theoretical and practical knowledge; and, by broadening their understanding of human relationships, to produce men of wisdom who will be better prepared to make positive contributions to society and all mankind. We continue to strive to develop men of honor that which inspire others by his example and thus win admiration and respect for himself and for Phi Sigma Kappa. Damn Proud.
summary: Being a part of the Greek community at the University of Kentucky since 1926 Phi Sigma has continuously worked to promote brotherhood, stimulate scholarship, and develop character.We are a lifelong brotherhood dedicated to the betterment of the individ..

name: Sigma Delta Pi
description: The Sigma Delta Pi Honor Society is composed of students who are interested in furthering their knowledge of the Hispanic language and culture, and who meet the academic criteria specified below. The University of Kentucky has been a member of this National Society since 1969 and continues to initiate new members each year using the traditional initiation ceremony. In addition, Sigma Delta Pi members will also be invited to departmental receptions for speakers, cultural events, and other activities which may be suggested by the members. The National Honor Society offers various scholarships and benefits to its members, and UK students are encouraged to take advantage of these opportunities.
summary: The Sigma Delta Pi Honor Society is composed of students who are interested in furthering their knowledge of the Hispanic language and culture, and who meet the academic criteria specified below. The University of Kentucky has been a member of this Nat...

name: Campus Outreach
description: 1. Campus Outreach is founded by Christians who agree in their belief that man's only access to God is through Jesus Christ and faith in His deity, His death as full payment for man's sins, and His resurrection. While this is the foundation of Campus Outreach, those holding other beliefs are welcome to get involved. Campus Outreach does not discriminate on any basis of race, creed, religion, color, age, sexual orientation, disability, veteran status, marital status or national origin. There are, however, no "members" in the sense of meeting requirements or being accepted into membership.
summary: Campus Outreach is open to all college students and full-time staff members at the University of Kentucky, who are interested in attending weekly Bible studies, meetings, and events, which are led by Campus Outreach staff and/or older student leaders.

name: Mock Trial
description:
Mock Trial is a competitive team that combines speech, debate, and theater to compete in trial simulations. Each year, The American Mock Trial Association (AMTA) publishes a criminal or civil case. The 600+ mock trial teams in the nation then prepare the prosecution/plaintiff and defense sides of this case in order to compete in trial simulations. The Mock Trial teams have recently competed in the 2022 Opening Round Championship Series in Atlanta, GA, and the 2017 National Championship in Los Angeles, CA. 

The Mock Trial team will host informational sessions and auditions in September.

If you would like to contact us concerning a tournament, please email us at ukymocktrial000@gmail.com

If you are interested in joining Mock Trial at University of Kentucky, please fill out the following interest survey: https://forms.gle/WrGoPB6aXhhD5oHu8
summary: Mock Trial fields competitive teams that attend trial simulation tournaments with AMTA, combining speech, debate, and theater. The program was founded to help students develop communication skills, enhance analytic thinking, and advocate justice.

name: Japanese Culture in Kentucky Society
description:
Welcome to the Japanese Culture in Kentucky Society (JCiKS) Club! 

Here at JCiKS, we promote international awareness between Japanese and Kentucky/American cultures. At first glance, they share little in common, but the truth is much is shared between the two! Our club serves as a dynamic hub for students dedicated to fostering a deeper understanding and appreciation of Japan's rich heritage right here in the heart of Kentucky!
 

What We Do:

- Hai! Kaiwa (はい！会話): Whether you're a beginner or advanced learner, our club offers opportunities to improve your Japanese language skills through Hai! Kaiwa! 

We have two tables: an English table and a Japanese table. This provides an opportunity to practice speaking Japanese and English! Don't know any Japanese? That's completely fine! Feel free to join us at the English table!

- Cultural Events: Every month, we hold an event related to Japanese culture! From making Japanese dishes to celebrating holidays by throwing beans at each other, there is an event for everyone to enjoy here at JCiKS! In the past, we have done many events, like Setsubun, Onigiri night, Origimai, Calligraphy, Halloween Parties, and Ikebana (Flower Decorating)!

Membership is open to any interested person affiliated with the University of Kentucky regardless of major! Non-affiliated persons are able to have honorary memberships, so don't be shy! Everyone is welcome.

We are an officially sanctioned club of the University of Kentucky.  We are sponsored by the Japan Studies program under the Department of Modern and Classical Language, Literature, and Culture in the College of Arts & Sciences.  We also work together with the English as a Second Language (ESL) Program.
summary: The Japanese Culture in Kentucky Society aims to promote cross-cultural exchange and friendship through international awareness between Japanese and Kentucky cultures. Anyone is welcome to attend our meetings, regardless of Japanese language experience!

name: Student National Dental Association
description: "In its first 25 years of existence, the SNDA has demonstrated its vitality and viability. It has a legacy of activism. It has become an integral minority student support system and the bridge between students and minority dental health practitioners. The ultimate goal is to improve the health status of the underserved and to increase the manpower distribution among the minority populations. SNDA national meetings are held annually to continue to advance the mission of the SNDA and to refine the collective efforts of the organization." - https://sndaonline.net/
summary: SNDA is a committed to promoting, aiding and supporting the academic and social environment of minority students in the field of dentistry. This organization aims to establish a sense of community for all dental students.

Guided by their Purpose, Alpha Gam’s strive to attain a higher standard, thereby improving their lives, the lives of those around them, and the communities in which they live. Together, they continually work to: Inspire the Woman. Impact the World.

name: Kentucky Wilderness Medicine
description: The fundamental purpose of the Wilderness Medicine Interest Group (WMIG) is to advance interest, education, and basic practical skills related to wilderness medicine. The WMIG aims to provide an organized mechanism by which medical students can learn the body of wilderness medicine knowledge and its application in a high-quality and evidence-based fashion. Finally, the WMIG aspires to combine scientific, humanistic and environmentally conscious values in relation to wilderness medicine. The WMIG will organize a monthly lecture series on various topics of wilderness medicine, host hands-on workshops with emergency medicine residents, prepare students for MedWARS (a wilderness medicine simulation race/competition), engage with the community to educate on wilderness safety, and organize group outings (hikes, etc.). When possible, presenters will be from the most pertinent field of study (e.g. Field Water Treatment presented by an Infectious Disease physician) and workshops will be in simulated low-resource environments
summary: The fundamental purpose of the Wilderness Medicine Interest Group (WMIG) is to advance interest, education, and basic practical skills related to wilderness medicine. The WMIG aims to provide an organized mechanism by which medical students can learn t...

Founded on the core beliefs of love, honor, and truth, and chartered to UK's campus in 1902, Gamma Iota takes pride in its commitment to UK and the Lexington community. Our active brothers are engaged members of the campus who carry themselves with respect and punctuality every day.

name: Lambda Kappa Sigma
description: Lambda Kappa Sigma (LKS) is an international professional pharmacy fraternity open to undergraduate and graduate pharmacy students and practicing pharmacists. The Core Values of LKS are: Sisterhood, Leadership, Service, Scholarship and, underlying all Core Values, Integrity. LKS fosters personal growth through the support, encouragement and camaraderie among members, challenging each to be the best human being, pharmacist and sister they can be. Through active participation in campus activities and fraternity programs, LKS members cultivate valuable leadership skills and first-hand experience with group dynamics. With an emphasis on women's health issues, LKS educational programs enhance professional development in the field of pharmacy by giving members the understanding necessary to serve patients knowledgeably and with confidence. Founded in 1913, LKS has more than 24,000 members, with 44 campus chapters and 36 alumni groups chartered internationally.
summary: Lambda Kappa Sigma (LKS) is an international professional pharmacy fraternity open to undergraduate and graduate pharmacy students and practicing pharmacists. The Core Values of LKS are: Sisterhood, Leadership, Service, Scholarship and, underlying all ...

description: TAB is devoted to fostering and honing the skills of future trial lawyers within the UK College of Law. From that body, students can also be chosen to compete on competitive teams that represent the the school at the state, regional, and national level. TAB is also involved with 1L students who will be the future of the organization by putting on an intramural tournament in their spring semester. TAB hosts speakers and CLE programs at the College of Law to bring awareness to the issues and complexities of litigation work, as well as provide networking opportunities for students with practitioners in the field.

name: University of Kentucky Running Club
description: This is a club running team intended to accommodate those that wish to run competitively or recreationally. There is a cross country season in the fall and track & field in the spring through the National Intercollegiate Club Running Association (NIRCA). Competitions take place at local universities/colleges in Kentucky and surrounding states. Not feeling competitive? Club running offers a community of runners at all levels as well as many local/community race options. We invite everyone to participate whether you have a lot of running experience or haven’t ran once in your life! Instagram: @ukclubrunning (Ask to join GroupMe to be apart of team and know more about practices!)
summary: This is a club running team intended to accommodate those that wish to run competitively or recreationally. We meet various times throughout the week to train and compete regularly throughout the year for cross country and track & field.

name: Formula Kentucky
description:
Come build a race car with us! 

Our process starts with design, then we manufacture, then we test! Our team competed at an international competition in May 2024, where we placed in multiple categories. We are building on this success to make our newest car, FK07, the best competition vehicle yet in 2025!

In addition to engineering, we have a large business aspect. With this team we manage the budget and finances, plan team events, create marketing and social material, interact with the community, and correspond with companies nation wide. 

We are a large team, with a full onboarding for new members called FK101. We invite you to join us for any of these and hope to see you at one of our events!
summary: We are a team of engineers and business-people that designs, builds, and races a Formula-style racing vehicle from the ground up. We analyze and manages costs, budgets, and performance, and develop relationships with sponsors and industry professionals.

name: Latino Law Student Association
description: The Latino Law Student Association at the University of Kentucky promotes and encourages a more diverse learning environment at the University of Kentucky College of Law. LLSA also provides support for all students, especially those of Hispanic background. This includes encouraging participation in national organizations, such as the National Latino Law Student Association and the Hispanic National Bar Association, as well as attendance at conferences across the nation. LLSA will continue to strive to encourage other Hispanic students at all levels of education as they continue their journey to law school and beyond.
summary: The Latino Law Student Association at the University of Kentucky promotes and encourages a more diverse learning environment at the University of Kentucky College of Law.

name: OUTLaw
description:
OUTLaw is the LGBTQ+ affinity organization at the University of Kentucky J. David Rosenberg College of Law, dedicated to fostering a strong sense of community, support, and inclusion for students of all sexual orientations, gender identities, and romantic expressions. Our primary goal is to create a welcoming and affirming safe space where LGBTQ+ law students can find solidarity, advocacy, and meaningful connection throughout their legal education.

As an organization, OUTLaw is committed to promoting equity, visibility, and justice both within the law school and across the broader legal profession. Membership is open to all, as OUTLaw proudly welcomes allies who share our values and vision for a more inclusive and equitable legal environment. In addition to providing a safe place for those already in the LGBTQ+ community, OUTLaw is committed to providing a safe space for people to explore their identity and connection to the LGBTQ+ community as well. No one will be asked to disclose their sexual orientation, gender identity, or romantic preferences to participate in OUTLaw activities or membership.
summary: OUTLaw is a student-led affinity organization at the University of Kentucky Rosenberg College of Law that is committed to fostering a safe, inclusive, and empowering environment for LGBTQ+ students, allies, and members of the greater legal community.

name: Psychology Student Association
description:
The Psychology Student Association is an all-inclusive organization for psychology students. If you are a psychology major or minor and are interested in meeting others with the same interests, this is the club for you! We are here to help guide students through their college careers involving psychology. We offer networking opportunities, help with classes, and graduate school-related services will be offered!  Social events are just as important to us as academic ones and will be featured occasionally as the club popularity grows! Students of all grade levels are permitted. If psychology interests you, become a member today!

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/99303205/mZBSoBQQ
summary: The Psychology Student Association is an all-inclusive organization for psychology students. If you are a psychology major or minor and are interested in meeting others with the same interests, this is the club for you! We are here to help you!

summary: The Medical Laboratory Science Club is for students interested in becoming a medical laboratory scientist and promoting the profession.

name: College of Health Sciences Discovery Healthcare Program Ambassadors
description:
The mission of the Discovery Healthcare Program is to diversify healthcare professions by partnering with high school students who possess diverse life experiences and perspectives. We are dedicated to empowering these students through support, mentorship, and enrichment opportunities. Our goal is to assist them in their transition from high school to college and, ultimately, into healthcare professions. 

“We believe that everyone deserves a chance to explore their potential in the healthcare field,” said Randa Remer, Executive Director of the Discovery Healthcare Program. “Our new name embodies our mission to uncover and nurture the talents of aspiring healthcare professionals from all backgrounds.” 

The Discovery Healthcare Program will continue to provide the same impactful services, including mentorship programs, workshops, and resources tailored to meet the unique needs of our students. We are committed to building a diverse and equitable healthcare workforce that reflects the communities we serve. 

We invite educators, students, and community members to join us in this exciting new chapter as we continue our mission to discover and empower the next generation of healthcare leaders.
summary: The Discovery Healthcare Program is a nonprofit organization dedicated to increasing diversity in healthcare professions. We partner with high school students to provide mentorship, support, and resources as they navigate their educational journeys.

name: The Society of Sigma Gamma Epsilon
description: The Society of Sigma Gamma Epsilon is a non-profit, non-political, autonomous, student-led, and student-governed organization at the University of Kentucky (UK). The primary objective of SGE is the academic and scientific advancement of its members at UK and the extension of the relations of friendship and assistance among colleges, universities, and scientific schools with recognized standing devoted to the advancement of the Earth Sciences.
summary: The Society of Sigma Gamma Epsilon was established to recognize scholarship and professionalism in the Earth Sciences. It has for its objectives the scholastic, scientific, and professional advancement of its members.

name: The Moot Court Board at the UK Rosenberg College of Law
shortName: Moot Court Board at UK RCOL
description: The Moot Court Board at the UK Rosenberg College of Law is made up of law students who desire to practice and develop appellate skills for later law practice. The Moot Court Board provides a means of doing so, while also providing access and funding for its members to engage in different moot court competitions across the nation.
summary: The Moot Court Board at the UKRCOL is made up of law students who desire to practice and develop appellate skills for later law practice.

name: Student Affiliates in School Psychology
description:
The Student Affiliates in School Psychology (SASP) at the University of Kentucky is a student-led organization dedicated to advancing the field of school psychology and spreading awareness of issues in education.

While SASP is primarily composed of graduate students in the School Psychology program, we also welcome undergraduate and graduate students from across the College of Education and the broader UK community who share our passion for supporting children, families, and schools.

Our mission is to empower members through leadership, advocacy, service, and professional development opportunities that prepare them to become effective science-practitioners and change agents in education. SASP fosters self-reflection and growth through monthly meetings, volunteer events, workshops, and social gatherings. 

From hot cocoa bars and movie nights to guest speakers, panels, and community service, our events bring people together, spark great conversations, and create lasting connections.

Through partnerships with national and state organizations such as NASP and KAPS, SASP members gain access to valuable resources, mentorship, and advocacy opportunities. Together, we’re focused on building a community where curiosity, compassion, and collaboration lead to purposeful changes.
summary: Join SASP at the University of Kentucky! We are a community of future school psychologists and supporters passionate about helping students, building connections, and making a difference in education!

description: We are Taiwanese Student Association! We welcome anyone who wants are interested in Taiwanese cultures, and people who join celebrating Taiwanese holidays with us! We help each other out, such as helping incoming students to find resources that they need for their studies at UK. We would love to learn more about other cultures, too! If you are interested in joining, please contact us, and we will get you in the group. Welcome!

name: Natural Resources and Environmental Science Club
description:
The Natural Resources and Environmental Science Club provides opportunities for others to learn about and engage in natural resources and environmental science through the following objectives:

I. Organize diverse volunteer and professional development (service-oriented) opportunities for students at local and state levels.
II. Provide educational opportunities for the current and prospective student community through workshops, seminars, and other activities geared toward environmental sustainability.
III. Provide social opportunities for like-minded students and other community members to share interests, hobbies, and appreciation of natural resources.
IV. Collaborate with local organizations, universities, and other UK clubs to expand our mission’s reach and maintain our ability to provide opportunities to students.
summary: To give students the opportunity to learn and engage in natural resources and environmental sciences by providing volunteer, professional development, educational, and social opportunities for like-minded students.

name: Agribusiness Club
description: The Agribusiness Club hosts regular meetings with local agribusiness managers, hosts an in-state trip to agribusiness industries such as Chaney's Dairy, James Beam Distillery, and TC Highlands in the fall, and an out-of-state trip in the spring to learn about different industries in other regions such as cotton, peanut, John Deere, BASF, CGB, and Jack Daniels. Recent trips include Texas, Georgia, Tennessee, and North Carolina. Membership is open to all undergraduates interested in the agriculture industry.
summary: The Agribusiness Club provides an opportunity to network and learn about the different aspects of the agriculture industry in and around Kentucky. We gather once a month with leaders from agribusinesses to discuss current issues facing our industry.

description: NCPA is one of three organizations that make up the Kentucky Alliance of Pharmacy Students (KAPS). Independent community pharmacists continue to be one of the most accessible and most trusted health care professionals for patients today. As student pharmacists, we are committed to continuing this trend along with advancing the profession of pharmacy.
summary: NCPA is one of three organizations that make up the Kentucky Alliance of Pharmacy Students (KAPS). Independent community pharmacists continue to be one of the most accessible and most trusted health care professionals for patients today. As student pha...

name: Kesem
description: Kesem is a child's friend through and beyond a parent's cancer. Throughout the school year, our Kesem team will fundraise money to send children ages 6-18 who are affected by a parent's cancer to a free week-long summer camp. This is a student-run organization that welcomes members from all backgrounds. We are in search of passionate students to serve as general body members or who are interested in volunteering as counselors at our one-week camp in the summer. Join our GroupMe here for more information or email kentucky@kesem.org with any questions!
summary: Kesem is a child's friend through and beyond a parent's cancer. During the school year, Kesem student leaders plan and fundraise for a week-long summer camp where children impacted by a parent's cancer can have fun & bond over their shared experiences.

name: Sigma Tau Delta
description:
The Delta Epsilon Upsilon chapter of Sigma Tau Delta was originally organized at the University of Kentucky in 1984. Our parent organization is a national honors society that was founded in 1924 at Dakota Wesleyan University. Its purposes are (a) to confer distinction for high achievement in English language and literature studies in undergraduate, graduate, and professional work, (b) to promote interest in literature and the English language on local campuses and their surrounding communities, and (c) to foster the discipline of English in all its aspects, including creative and critical writing. 

     UK students who join the international chapter are eligible to apply for Sigma Tau Delta scholarships, participate in international conventions, and submit original written work for publication in one of Sigma's two literary magazines. Membership provides a prestigious addition to any graduate school application or job resume, and organization contacts have led our graduates to careers in publishing, e-design, writing, teaching, and business.

     As a chapter dedicated to literacy outreach, we not only strive to build a strong literary network, but also to find innovative ways to serve our campus and Lexington community. We regularly host literature and English-related events across campus, such as Blind Date with a Book sales, poetry giveaways, graduate school panels, and Banned Books Week celebrations. In collaboration with the English Peer Mentor Program, we also facilitate events designed to help freshmen get accustomed to college life and classes. We maintain partnerships with Student Center Cinema, the Carnegie Center, and local schools to bring students events and service opportunities with a literary connection.
summary: We are the Delta Epsilon Upsilon chapter of Sigma Tau Delta, the International English Honor Society. Our goal is to connect students from all majors who are passionate about literature and want to build a community dedicated to literacy outreach.

name: Healthcare Administration Student Association
description:
The mission of the Healthcare Administration Student Association (HASA) is all about development. Not developing as just as a professional, but as a young adult preparing to enter the "real-world." HASA's goal is to be a launchpad for students looking to break into the industry (any, not just healthcare!). HASA will not be just another a club to attend, but a resource that actively helps students move forward in their professional careers. This means hands-on development, not just listening to guest speakers, but actually working on your resume, networking strategy, communication and negotiation skills. Workshops where students can walk away with something that they can immediately apply to advance their career. Any and all things about the workforce, from office politics to company loyalty, as well as Q&As, etc. will all happen this year!

Follow us on Instagram: @ukyhasa

Email us to join our Flare group chat!
summary: The purpose of this organization is to assist in the development of future healthcare administrators through leadership, service and education outside of the classroom.

name: Women's Law Caucus
description: WLC is open to all law students. WLC focuses on building connections with women in the legal community and working to educate our members about the unique issues that women face in the legal field.
summary: WLC is open to all law students. WLC focuses on building connections with women in the legal community and working to educate our members about the unique issues that women face in the legal field.

description:
In this club students will engage themselves with the classical languages of Latin and Greek, along with the cultures and mythology associated with them.

Join our Discord server:

https://discord.gg/uVb7CFHX3Z
summary: In this club students will engage themselves with the classical languages of Latin and Greek, along with the cultures and Mythology associated with them.

name: Material Advantage
description: Material Advantage is the campus organization umbrella for The Minerals, Metals and Materials Society (TMS), Association for Iron & Steel Technology (AIST), the American Society of Materials (ASM), and American Ceramic Society (ACerS). Our aim is to promote among its members a self-sought increasing knowledge of metallurgy, ceramics, materials science and engineering and all its branches and to instill a professional pride in their chosen life work. We hope to get engineering students more deeply involved in the field of materials science and engineering by providing opportunities on and off campus to network at, learn from, and participate in our events.
summary: Material Advantage at UK is an organization that focuses on all things materials science! We aim to foster a community of Materials students and provide opportunities for our members to explore the field of materials engineering including industry tours.

name: Academy of Managed Care Pharmacy
description: The Academy of Managed Care Pharmacy (AMCP) is a national professional association of pharmacists, health care practitioners and others who develop and provide clinical, educational and business management services on behalf of more than 200 million Americans covered by a managed pharmacy benefit. AMCP members are committed to a simple goal: providing the best available pharmaceutical care for all patients.
summary: The Academy of Managed Care Pharmacy (AMCP) is a national professional association of pharmacists, health care practitioners and others who develop and provide clinical, educational and business management services on behalf of more than 200 million Am...

name: Tabletop and Roleplaying Games Club
description:
We are a Social Club dedicated to the encouragement of creativity, critical thinking and team skills in a fun and relaxing manner. If you enjoy Board Games, Role Playing Games, Card Games, or other Tabletop games, this is the club for you! If you enjoy acting, writing, or game design, this is the club for you too! The club is open to all members of the UK Community, join us for weekly game night or visit one of our workshops to learn more about creating, running or playing in a variety of games and systems! Interested, but not sure you want to actually join? Just need to have a night of fun before an exam? No need to join the club, just join us for a night of gaming! All our game nights are open to all students, member or no, and we have several game nights each semester specifically for non-club members to come and check us out! Hope to see you all soon! Have fun and keep playing!

 

See what we're up to by checking out our Discord Server (https://discord.gg/8maKSAkbsE) or contacting ukytabletoprpgclub@gmail.com to get emails!
summary: We are a Social Club for students that enjoy Tabletop gaming, including board games, card games, roleplaying games, and wargames!  If any of these interest you, join us for one of our weekly game nights or any of our other events throughout the year.

name: University of Kentucky Men's Lacrosse Team
description: The University of Kentucky Men's Lacrosse team is a member of the MCLA. The team plays in Division 1 of the Atlantic Lacrosse Conference. If you have any interests please fill out the player interest form on our website  https://www.kentuckylax.com/
summary: The University of Kentucky Men's Lacrosse Team is in the National Collegiate Lacrosse League (NCLL) Midwest South Conference.

description: The RIG provides medical students with information regarding radiology as a career, informs students on how to apply and match into radiology residencies, and offers a means for interested students to meet the UK Radiology department.
