name: Social Work Association
description:
Get Involved With SWA: Social Work Association at the University of Kentucky is a student-led organization that aims to promote social justice while providing community service, opportunities for continuing education, and developing students as professional social workers.

MAKE FRIENDS! MAKE A DIFFERENCE! BECOME A VOLUNTEER! All those interested in social work and service are encouraged to join SWA. We welcome both undergraduate and graduate students as well as non-social work majors. 

EVERYONE IS WELCOME TO JOIN!
summary: SWA promotes social work values, especially social justice, while increasing our development of community awareness and action!  All undergraduate and graduate students are welcome!  Volunteer Work, Advocacy, and Student Engagement.

name: Wesley Foundation
description: Our mission at the Wesley Foundation is to lead students to God through Jesus Christ, nurture their growth in that relationship, prepare students to advance the Kingdom of God and develop Christian leaders in a safe Christian residential living environment.
summary: Our mission at the Wesley is to lead students to God through Jesus Christ, nurture their growth in that relationship, prepare students to advance the Kingdom of God, and develop Christian leaders in a safe Christian residential living environment.

name: The Federalist Society
description:
The Federalist Society for Law and Public Policy Studies is a non-partisan organization “dedicated to reforming legal order.” The purpose of the organization is to provide a forum whereby members have the opportunity to explore, evaluate, discuss, question, and debate political philosophy, particularly as applied to the judiciary, both within the organization and with the greater community.

 

The Society is founded on the principles that the State exists to preserve freedom, that the separation of governmental powers is central to our Constitution, and that it is the duty of the judiciary to state what the law is, not what it should be. The main purpose is to sponsor fair, serious, and open debate.

 

The Federalist Society at the University of Kentucky seeks to contribute political balance to legal education by providing a forum for legal experts to interact with law students and share legal concepts absent from most traditional law school curriculums.
summary: The Federalist Society is a non-partisan organization “dedicated to reforming legal order.” The purpose of the organization is to explore, evaluate, discuss, question, and debate conservative and libertarian political philosophy.

name: Biomedical Engineering Society
description: BMES is an academic interest organization striving to bring together graduate and undergraduate students who are interested in biomedical engineering and related topics in an educational and service-oriented environment. Our chapter participates in many academic events including seminars on current research in BME and similar fields including prosthetic development, biological mechanics, cell and tissue engineering, medical devices and processes, and regenerative medicine. Members are provided opportunities to grow professionally with workshops and networking events. We participate in community service activities with local schools and nonprofit organizations as well as social activities with other UK organizations and BMES members.
summary: BMES is an academic and service-based organization striving to provide a community for those interested in biomedical engineering and other related topics. We work to provide students with networking, volunteer, professional, and learning opportunities.

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America
description: We are here to provide a voice for democratic socialism on UK's campus through education and service. Democratic socialism emphasizes the link between social and economic inequality. We are affiliated with the Democratic Socialists of America and not with any political party.

name: Kappa Kappa Psi
description: Kappa Kappa Psi is an honorary band fraternity that promotes and recognizes service, musicianship, leadership, and brotherhood within the University of Kentucky Bands. Our Brothers exemplify these qualities, and are always striving for the highest as musicians, leaders, Brothers, and members of the band.
summary: KKPsi is an organization that believes in service, musicianship, leadership, and brotherhood for the University of Kentucky Bands. We recognize and promote each of these qualities within the bands and Brothers, always striving for the highest.

name: National Marrow Donor Program on Campus
description: • The National Marrow Donor Program (formerly known as Be The Match) is the largest nation-wide registry that connects patients in need of blood stem cell and bone marrow transplant to willing donors. Our campus chapter works to educate members of the UK community about the importance of growing and diversifying the NMDP registry. Though various on-campus club activities, we aim to recruit members of the UK community to join the registry and raise money for NMDP.
summary: The National Marrow Donor Program is the largest nation-wide registry that connects patients in need of blood stem cell and bone marrow transplant to willing donors. Our campus chapter works to educate and recruit members of the UK community.

name: Gamma Upsilon Chapter of Phi Sigma Pi
description: Phi Sigma Pi is a gender-inclusive honor fraternity dedicated to improving humanity with honor. We accomplish this by promoting the ideas of Scholarship, Leadership, and Fellowship to our members. All potential new members must have a cumulative GPA of 3.0 or better as well as at least one more semester of college remaining. For more information, check out our website, social media pages, or contact any of our members!
summary: Phi Sigma Pi is a gender-inclusive fraternity dedicated to improving humanity with honor. We look to accomplish this by promoting our tripod of ideas: Scholarship, Leadership, and Fellowship. For more information, check out our website or Instagram page.

description: We are a chapter of the Society of Women Engineers (SWE) located at the University of Kentucky extension campus in Paducah, KY. SWE provides professional growth and development in the engineering field, along with community involvement and outreach opportunities through volunteering. Some of our events include attending national and regional conferences, hosting "Death by Chocolate" and our numerous volunteering and bonding activities. SWE is a great way to meet new people and make lifelong friends in engineering! We are continuing to grow our club with great ideas each year and welcome you to join in on the fun!
summary: We are a chapter of the Society of Women Engineers (SWE) located at the University of Kentucky extension campus in Paducah, KY. SWE provides professional growth development in the engineering field, community involvement and outreach through volunteeri...

name: American Association of Pharmaceutical Scientists
description:
The AAPS chapter at the University of Kentucky is focused on four main goals:
1. Working towards achieving the broad goals of AAPS of extending and enriching the scope and domain of pharmaceutical sciences.
2. Bringing professionals from academia, industry, and government regulatory agencies to the university to share their expert knowledge.
3. Organizing pharmaceutical conferences to exchange knowledge and boost scientific cooperation.
4. Providing graduate students with an opportunity to network with others.
Our chapter is continuously working to engage and educate our community and members, so they become aware of what individuals in the pharmaceutical sciences do and hopefully inspire people to develop/pursue careers in the pharmaceutical sciences.
summary: Here at AAPS, we strive to provide members with opportunities to enhance their education, broaden their network, engage with the community, and launch their careers in the pharmaceutical sciences.

name: Chess Club
description:
Chess Club was formed to provide the University of Kentucky an organization that connects students by virtue of chess. We are here to provide our community a place to learn, teach, and play chess while also connecting students with a common interest.

Join the Group Me to stay informed!! https://groupme.com/join_group/95526484/Zrpx5W1C

Join our chess.com club: https://www.chess.com/club/uky-chess-club/join

name: Sports and Entertainment Law Society
description: The Sports and Entertainment Law Society (SELS) is dedicated to exploring the intersection of law and the dynamic fields of sports and entertainment. Our organization provides a platform for students passionate about these industries to engage with legal issues that shape them. Through guest speakers, panel discussions, and networking events,  SELS fosters a community where members can develop knowledge in areas such as contract negotiation, intellectual property rights, dispute resolution, and regulatory compliance. SELS aims to offer invaluable opportunities to learn, connect, and grow in exciting areas at the crossroads of sports/entertainment and law.
summary: The Sports and Entertainment Law Society was created to give students an outlet to connect with individuals currently working in the industry, as well as giving them to opportunity to learn more about what a J.D. can do for you in these fields.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
description:
The student chapter of the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) at the University of Kentucky offers students an excellent opportunity to learn more about the civil engineering profession, gain valuable contacts with engineering employers, and get to know other students in the department.

Our weekly meetings feature speakers from a variety of civil engineering fields such as transportation, structures, water resources, environmental, and construction.

The ASCE student chapter at UK organizes many activities throughout the year such as community service events, socials, and field trips.  The chapter also participates in various competitions at the annual regional symposium. Attend our next weekly meeting to learn what civil engineering is really all about!

Click here to join our GroupMe
summary: ASCE at Uk connects civil engineering students with employers, peers, and professionals through weekly meetings, guest speakers, community service, socials, field trips, and symposium competitions. Come see what civil engineering is all about!

name: Counseling Center
description:
The University of Kentucky Counseling Center (UKCC), provides free, confidential psychological counseling and mental health services to currently enrolled UK students. We also provide consultation to students, UK faculty and staff, and caregivers.

 

We know that university life can be demanding, and that academics, work, athletics, finances, family, health, and relationships can be stressors in students' lives. We also know that many of our students face forms of oppression and discrimination that impact their mental health, interpersonal relationships, and academic progress.

 

Staff at the UKCC strive to create a welcoming, inclusive environment for all UK students to promote exploration of any personal problems or concerns.

 

We offer confidential, short-term group, individual, and relationship therapy to support students' growth and to assist students with mental health, academic, or personal concerns that may interfere with academic performance or a sense of personal well-being while at UK. We also offer workshops and technology-based wellbeing services.

 

Visit our website for more info: https://studentsuccess.uky.edu/counseling-center
summary: The University of Kentucky Counseling Center: Consultation and Psychological Services (UKCC), provides confidential psychological counseling to currently enrolled UK students and consultation services to currently enrolled UK students, UK faculty and s...

name: Biology Graduate Student Association
description: Mission Statement: The BGSA’s mission is to provide an entity for representation and organization of graduate students and post doctoral fellows in the Department of Biology at the University of Kentucky. We uphold professionalism in all our events and strive to establish and preserve integrative collaborations within the department. Finally, we provide community involvement and outreach opportunities to our members. Who is a BGSA member? A BGSA member is any active graduate student (Post-Bach, Master’s and Ph.D.) or postdoctoral scholar, present, visiting or in absentia, in the Department of Biology at the University of Kentucky.
summary: Graduate and postdoctoral fellows advancing research and science literacy through community involvement

name: Society for Biomaterials
description: The University of Kentucky student chapter of the Society for Biomaterials (UK SFB) is an academic organization dedicated to uniting graduate and undergraduate students from diverse disciplines who share an interest in biomaterials. We foster an educational, service-oriented, and engaging community by offering social, academic, and professional enrichment opportunities. From collaborative potlucks with adjacent student organizations to hands-on workshops and research tool training, our events support both learning and networking. We encourage and support our members in attending regional and national SFB events, including Biomaterials Days and the SFB Annual Meeting. No matter your level of experience or expertise, we welcome all students to join our inclusive community, gain valuable skills, and explore opportunities in the dynamic field of biomaterials.
summary: The University of Kentucky student chapter of the Society for Biomaterials (UK SFB) is an academic organization dedicated to uniting graduate and undergraduate students from diverse disciplines who share an interest in biomaterials.

name: W.A.R. [Worship Anointing  & Revival] Campus Outreach Ministries
description: A christian organization, focused on empowering students, staff, faculty, and the surrounding community of the University of Kentucky to achieve spiritual actualization through Worship, Anointing and Revival. The purpose of this organization is anchored on the scripture found in Ephesians 6:12 “For we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this world, against spiritual wickedness in high places.” Pursuant to this text, we hope to remind students and others that the battles we face in encountering opposition to our dreams, callings and goals can be overcome with Spiritual weapons such as prayer, fasting corporate worship, Christian fellowship and Biblical study. This organization also intends to empower students to adhere to tenants of Holiness doctrine, while in pursuit of their educations. We hope that, by preaching the unadulterated gospel of Jesus Christ, we will empower students to be light and salt within their respective spheres of influence and transform the increasingly secularized culture of The University of Kentucky.

name: Pre-PA Student Association
description: The University of Kentucky Pre-Physician Assistant Student Association is a group open to students desiring to learn about a career as a Physician Assistant. Our goal is to better educate prospective students about the PA profession and acceptance into a PA program. Our emphasis is on the University of Kentucky’s Physician Assistant Studies program in the College of Health Sciences. The club has a variety of speakers ranging from current PA students, working PA-C's, admission representatives, and PA faculty. These speakers provide a lot of information about applying to and getting accepted to PA school, and they give insight about the profession as a whole. We also have group volunteering events and other networking events to help students in their pursuit toward the PA profession and meet others with the same career aspirations.
summary: Our club is for students who are interested in learning more about the PA (physician assistant) profession and PA school. We have different speakers and events geared toward preparing students for PA school.

description: The Martin Luther King Center empowers University of Kentucky students across identities to enhance their college experience by promoting student success, civic leadership, and community building—rooted in Dr. King’s vision of the Beloved Community. We support students through access to our multipurpose space, connection to full-time staff and advisory support, engaging programs, collaborative initiatives, and student organization support.

name: St. Augustine’s Chapel ― Lutheran-Episcopal Campus Ministry
description:
St. Augustine's is an inviting, lay-led, prayer-filled, and inclusive Christian community at the University of Kentucky. Since 1954, the Chapel has been a spiritual haven for students amidst the business of college life, a place to pray, sing, build community, and grow closer to God.
 
St. Augustine's is supported by our roots in the Episcopal and Lutheran traditions. We strive to be an accessible community for all who seek an open and honest relationship with God through Jesus Christ for personal and communal transformation and renewal. St. A's is open to all members of the University community regardless of race, age, gender, culture, ability, ethnicity, nation of origin, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, pregnancy, religious beliefs and affiliation, military status, socioeconomic status, or any other child of God you can think of. God loves you just the way you are.

Formerly Ignite - Lutheran Campus Ministry and Episcopal Campus Ministry, now united together in Christ to provide an inclusive ministry for any and all members of the UK community.
summary: We are a Lutheran and Episcopal Campus Ministry at the University of Kentucky affiliated with the Indiana-Kentucky Synod (ELCA) and the Episcopal Diocese of Lexington.

The organization is an affiliate with National DYA organization registered in the State of Illinois (or write Canada if applicable). All cultural activities of DYA are based on guidelines and inspiration from Tattwajnana Vidyapeeth (TVP) and Nirmal Niketan (NN) of Mumbai, India and Devotional Associates of Yogeshwar (DAY) of North America.

NSSLHA essentially lays the foundation for students' future roles in ASHA (American Speech-Language-Hearing Association) as speech-language pathologists, audiologists, or speech scientists. NSSLHA provides many opportunities for student and community involvement, including community service events, KSHA support, and it's very own scholarship fund.

summary: The Internal Medicine Interest Group provides medical students with the opportunity to explore the field of internal medicine and learning opportunities to develop skills to excel as a future physician in internal medicine.

name: The Horticulture Club
description:
The Horticulture Club at The University of Kentucky is a group of students and faculty members who work together to further community interest and education in Horticulture. We are a student-focused club. Members come from a wide array of experiences with plants! Through the club, our members get the opportunity to interact with fellow horticulture experts, houseplant enthusiasts, or even notorious succulent killers to work together and create something beautiful.

 

We host workshops, field trips, and workdays throughout the semester, so students can hone their skills and their love of nature. In the past, we have done natural dyeing with indigo leaves, created wreaths and bouquets for events, and visited local nature preserves to do native wildflower walks as a club! Additionally, the Horticulture Club specializes in native plant production, hosting sales for the UK community each semester. You can actually find the Horticulture Club’s plants in many of the school’s campus projects! The Native Plant Nursery is an integral part of the club’s funding, allowing students to offset the costs of going on club field trips, traveling to professional conferences, and even our summer trips abroad!

 

If you're interested in joining the club, come to a meeting and scope it out! We meet every Wednesday 5-6pm. Once you come to a meeting, we can get your membership set up. Feel free to reach out if you have any questions! We’d love to have you!!
summary: The Horticulture Club is a group of students and faculty that have a common passion for plants and nature! We host workshops, field trips, and workdays for our members every month. We also specialize in growing native plants and sell to the community!

name: Psychiatry Interest Group
description: This group exists for students interested in applying to a psychiatry residency or just interested in learning more about the field of psychiatry, to be connected with other like-minded students. This group also serves to connect students with faculty members for shadowing and research opportunities, facilitate volunteering opportunities with mental health organizations in the greater Kentucky area and to educate students on other opportunities available to them in the field. Finally, we aim to help students planning on applying into psychiatry by connecting them with faculty mentors to help strengthen their applications.
summary: An opportunity for medical students interested in psychiatry to broaden their understanding of and gain exposure to topics in psychiatry.

name: NABA: Minority Business Professionals
description:
A minority-based organization inclusive to all majors dedicated to helping students’ network with professionals, develop essential business skills, along with providing service to the Lexington community, and creating a sense of community within Gatton.

 

INSTAGRAM: UK_NABA 

Stay update with all events! Contact one of the E-board members to get more information.
summary: NABA is a minority based organization dedicated to helping its members experience success in the classroom and the transition into the workforce.  We accept all business majors and minors.

name: The National Society of Leadership and Success
description:
The NSLS is the largest and only accredited leadership honor society in the United States. Our local chapter is part of this national organization with more than 800 chapters and over 2 million existing members.

Students are selected based on exemplary academic achievement and leadership potential. Our chapter members will have access to:

  • Over $400,000 in annual scholarship opportunities (visit nsls.org/scholarships-and-awards to learn more)

  • An exclusive NSLS job bank

  • A personalized letter of recommendatio

    Our leadership program teaches interpersonal skills that give you an advantage in a competitive job market. If you become a member, you can also:

    • Learn from top global leaders like former US Presidents, CEOs, celebrities, and more with exclusive Speaker Broadcasts.

    • Meet new friends and make lifelong connections (on-campus and virtually).

    • Overcome challenges with success coaches, shortcutting your way to your goals.

    • Gain access to exclusive private networking events.

      Visit nsls.org to learn more and accept your nomination. Frequently Asked Questions:

      Some common questions we receive are listed below. If you have any other questions, visit nsls.org to chat with a team member or email them at support@nsls.org.

      What do I have to do to become inducted?

      The Steps to Induction take approximately 9-10 hours over the course of the semester and you can complete it at your own pace. To learn more, visit nsls.org/the-membership-experience.

      Is this recognized on campus?

      The NSLS is officially recognized on our campus and is led by Mark Samudre, Chapter Advisor, and Reagan Maddox, Chapter President. Student members in our chapter will join more than 2 million members nationwide.
summary: The National Society of Leadership and Success at the University of Kentucky.

name: UK International Center
description:
Join us for international-related and cross-cultural events and activities. The International Center includes International Student & Scholar Services, Education Abroad and more!

The UKIC supports University of Kentucky’s global vision by providing leadership, raising awareness, facilitating the pursuit of international education and encouraging global collaborations for the University community, the Commonwealth and beyond.

For more information on events visit our website!
summary: Join us for international-related and cross-cultural events and activities. The International Center includes International Student & Scholar Services, Education Abroad and more!

name: Russian Club
description: The Russian Club promotes the study of Russian language and culture through student activities.
summary: The Russian Club promotes the study of Russian language and culture through student activities.

name: Adventist Christian Fellowship Student Group
description: ACF is a religious club founded by followers of Jesus Christ. We believe in the Trinity, Jesus as our personal Savior, the Bible as holy and inspired writings, the standing of the 10 Commandments, and in the second coming of Jesus Christ. Our mission is to bring individuals to Jesus Christ by demonstrating His love for all people, studying the Holy Bible together, and uniting to serve the needs of students, faculty and other community members.
summary: ACF is a religious club founded by followers of Jesus Christ. We believe in the Trinity, Jesus as our personal Savior, the Bible as holy and inspired writings, the standing of the 10 Commandments, and in the second coming of Jesus Christ.

name: Arabic Club
description: The purpose of this organization is to collaborate and learn from other individuals within the club about Arabic culture and language while addressing and understanding cultural differences between members. Throughout the year we will host meetings and cultural events that will serve as educational opportunities focusing on giving people introductions to Arabic celebrations. Through this, we will work to break down the misconceptions about Arabic culture and create a fruitful environment for cultural diversity to flourish.
summary: Come join us and enhance your Arabic language skills, or enjoy in person events and the sharing of culture and language.

name: Baptist Campus Ministry
description: The Baptist Campus Ministry is a Christian Community on campus open to all students. The BCM is a safe place for students to explore faith, get involved in a community, and serve the campus. We seek to love God and love others in everything we do.
summary: The Baptist Campus Ministry is a Christian Community on campus open to all students. The BCM is a safe place for students to explore faith, get involved in a community, and serve the campus. We seek to love God and love others in everything we do.

name: Sigma Alpha Iota International Music Fraternity
description:
Sigma Alpha Iota is an international women's music fraternity that was created to "uphold the highest standards of music" and "to further the development of music in American and throughout the world."

Please email our President Ella at edla231@uky.edu if you are interested in joining!
summary: Sigma Alpha Iota is an international women's music fraternity that was created to "uphold the highest standards of music" and "to further the development of music in American and throughout the world."

name: Omicron Delta Kappa Nu Circle
description: Omicron Delta Kappa, the National Leadership Honor Society, recognizes and encourages superior leadership and exemplary character. O∆K encourages collaboration among members across the five pillars pf leadership celebrated by O∆K:  scholarship, athletics, service, communications, and arts. Founded in 1914 at Washington and Lee University, O∆K honors and develops leaders through scholarships, workshops, career opportunities, leadership resources, and a lifelong connection to other members. The Society also champions its leadership values of  collaboration, inclusivity, integrity, scholarship, and service.Our goal within the Nu Circle Chapter is to bring together a network of like minded individuals striving to lead positive impact on campus and the community, while enhancing professional and intellectual growth, and establishing long lasting relationships.
summary: Omicron Delta Kappa, the National Leadership Honor Society, recognizes and encourages superior leadership and exemplary character. O∆K follows the five pillars pf leadership celebrated by O∆K:  scholarship, athletics, service, communications, and arts.

name: Chi Epsilon Civil Engineering Honor Society
description: Chi Epsilon is the National Civil Engineering Honor Society in the United States. Most engineering disciplines have a recognized national honor society. For the civil engineering profession, Chi Epsilon fills this role. Dedicated to the purpose of maintaining and promoting the status of civil engineering as an ideal profession, Chi Epsilon was organized to recognize the characteristics of the individual civil engineer deemed to be fundamental to the successful pursuit of an engineering career, and to aid in the development of those characteristics in the civil engineering student. Students and professionals are selected to become members based on recognition of their scholarship, character, practicality and sociability, considered by Chi Epsilon to be the four primary traits of a successful engineer. For student members, scholarship is determined by being in the top third of their junior or senior class. Members of Chi Epsilon are considered top graduates and are highly sought by civil engineering employers.
summary: Chi Epsilon is the National Civil Engineering Honor Society in the United States. Most engineering disciplines have a recognized national honor society. For the civil engineering profession, Chi Epsilon fills this role. Dedicated to the purpose of main..

name: American Student Dental Association - Kentucky Chapter
description: The American Student Dental Association is a national student run organization which protects and advances the rights, interests, and welfare of students pursuing careers in dentistry. It represents students with a unified voice and provides information, education, advocacy, and services. The association introduces lifelong involvement in organized dentistry and promotes change for the betterment of the profession.
summary: ASDA is a national student run organization which protects and advances the rights, interests, and welfare of dental students.  It represents students with a unified voice and provides information regarding involvement in organized dentistry.

name: Integrated Plant and Soil Sciences Graduate Student Association
description: The IPSS GSA is for all graduate students pursuing a Masters or Doctoral Degree in the Integrated Plant and Soil Sciences program, which includes crop science, plant biology, molecular biology and biochemistry, horticulture, and soil science. Our organization strives to prepare students for life after graduate school through professional development events such as fall and spring tours with industry and our annual 3MT event. We encourage community within our department through monthly socials, events following symposium, and a new student mentoring program.
summary: An association for graduate students pursuing a Masters or Doctoral Degree in the Integrated Plant and Soil Sciences Graduate Program offered through the Department of Plant and Soil Sciences and the Department of Horticulture.

name: Latter-Day Saint Student Association
description: LDSSA serves to support the social and spiritual needs of students who are members of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints and those not of our faith who are are interested in our way of life our partnered with us through community building, civic involvement, and service.
summary: LDSSA supports the social and spiritual needs of students who are members of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints and those not of our faith who are interested in our way of life and partnered with us through community building.

name: Pi Tau Sigma
description:
Pi Tau Sigma, Pi Lambda chapter is the Mechanical Engineering Honors society at the University of Kentucky. Pi Tau Sigma strives to uphold the values of Integrity, Service, and Leadership in our engineering community.

PI TAU SIGMA (ΠΤΣ) is the International Honor Society for Mechanical Engineers. Pi Tau Sigma members are chosen on a basis of sound engineering ability, scholarship service, leadership, and integrity. The Pi Tau Sigma mission is to strive to create better engineers through commitment to academic excellence and dedication to service. Pi Tau Sigma prides itself in the core values: Integrity (soundness of character and moral conduct), Service (serving the mechanical engineering profession and the community), and Leadership (fostering initiative through example in a professional manner). Pi Tau Sigma is a member of the Association of College Honor Societies. There are presently 182 collegiate chapters at universities in the United States, Puerto Rico, Mexico, Qatar, and United Arab Emirates.
summary: Pi Tau Sigma, Pi Lambda chapter is the Mechanical Engineering Honors society at the University of Kentucky. Pi Tau Sigma strives to uphold the values of Integrity, Service, and Leadership in our engineering community.

name: Christian Pharmacists Fellowship International
description: CPFI is a Christian organization within the College of Pharmacy that brings together like-minded students and faculty. Our goals are fellowship, spiritual growth and service. We work to create a fellowship of students through Bible studies, volunteering within our community, and fellowship events throughout the semester. We have monthly chapter meetings where students can hear the testimonies of our professors and local pharmacists, and also encourage our fellow classmates.
summary: CPFI is a Christian organization within the College of Pharmacy that brings together like-minded students and faculty. Our goals are fellowship, spiritual growth and service.

name: Engineering Student Council
description: The ESC serves as the bridge between the Engineering student organizations, faculty, and the Engineering student body. ESC is in charge of making sure the Engineering organizations maintain ESC standing in order to gain funding from the college. The organization has many other responsibilities including helping coordinate engineering orientation, keeping organizations involved, and selling College of Engineering Merchandise.
summary: The ESC serves as the bridge between the Engineering student organizations, faculty, and the Engineering student body. ESC is in charge of making sure the Engineering organizations maintain ESC standing in order to gain funding from the college.

name: German Club
description: The German Club at UKY connects students interested in German language, culture, and history. Through fun events, cultural activities, and shared experiences, it bridges cross-cultural gaps and strengthens global understanding. The club celebrates German heritage, encourages language practice, and creates a welcoming space for students to learn, connect, and explore together.
summary: Welcome to the German Club at the University of Kentucky! If you are interested in German language, culture, people, or food we'd love to have you! We're here to enjoy, learn, and promote German on campus. All UKY students are welcome!

name: Health Law Society
description: The Health Law Society is here to foster a connection between law students and the growing field of health care. We offer opportunities to network with practicing attorneys and academics in Lexington and the surrounding areas, as well as lectures conducted by prominent professionals in the area of health care and law.
summary: The Health Law Society is here to foster a connection between law students and the growing field of health care.  We offer opportunities to network with practicing attorneys and academics in Lexington and the surrounding areas, as well as lectures cond..

name: Rock Climbing Club
description: The University of Kentucky Rock Climbing Club hopes to serve as a means of uniting rock climbing enthusiasts and complete beginners in promoting good climbing ethics, introducing new climbers to the sport, and developing our climbing skills together as a community.
summary: The University of Kentucky Rock Climbing Club hopes to serve as a means of uniting rock climbing enthusiasts, promoting good climbing ethics, introducing new climbers to the sport, and developing our climbing skills together as a community

name: Sigma Pi
description: Sigma Pi Fraternity was founded in 1897 at Vincennes University, in Vincennes, Indiana. Our Executive Office is located at the historic Mitchell House in Lebanon, Tennessee. The Fraternity is one of the top men’s collegiate organizations in North America, with more than 120 active chapters and colonies in the United States and Canada. With over 100,000 alumni and 5,700 undergraduates; our men strive for excellence by living our core values to promote fellowship, develop character and leadership, advance heightened moral awareness, enable academic achievement, and inspire service. Sigma Pi is the only Greek letter organization with an international philanthropic program, The ACE Project, specifically designed to give back to our host institutions.
summary: Sigma Pi Fraternity was founded in 1897 at Vincennes University, in Vincennes, Indiana. Our Executive Office is located at the historic Mitchell House in Lebanon, Tennessee. The Fraternity is one of the top men’s collegiate organizations in North America

name: Student Sustainability Council
description: The Student Sustainability Council was formed to supervise the distribution of the Environmental Stewardship Fee in order to responsibly advance the theory, practice and reality of Sustainability at the University of Kentucky. Any member of the University of Kentucky community can submit a proposal for funding support. If you have an idea for a project that will enhance the culture of sustainability on UK's campus, we encourage you to use our submission form to make us aware of your project.
summary: The Student Sustainability Council was formed to supervise the distribution of the Environmental Stewardship Fee in order to responsibly advance the theory, practice and reality of Sustainability at the University of Kentucky. Any member of the Univers...

name: National Society of Collegiate Scholars
description:
The National Society of Collegiate Scholars (NSCS) is an honors organization that recognizes and elevates high-achieving students. NSCS provides career and graduate school connections, leadership and service opportunities, and awards more than $ 1 million annually in scholarships, awards, and chapter funds. 

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/110477298/HzEkIhPM

Chapter Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/nscs.uky/

Nominate yourself to join: https://join.nscs.org/
summary: NSCS is a nonprofit dedicated to providing high-achieving collegiate students with the opportunity to network and obtain volunteer experience while building upon our three pillars: scholarship, leadership, and service.

name: Hispanic Studies Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of this organization is to address common professional concerns and promote unity amongst graduate students in order to facilitate their life in the department. HIGSA officers also serve on faculty and university-wide committees as representatives of the Hispanic Studies graduate students. HIGSA organizes a series of programmed events that serve the needs of students at all stages of their graduate career: Mentorship Program, Master's Reading List Club, Professional Development Workshops, Nuestro Rumbo, etc.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to  address common professional concerns and promote unity amongst graduate students in order to facilitate their life in the department. HIGSA officers also serve on faculty and university-wide committees as represe...

name: Iranian Student Association
description: ISAUK is a community of recreation and education open to faculty, graduate students, and undergraduate students. Our goals are to preserve the practice of Iranian traditions and cultures among our members, to reach out to the local Iranian community, and to cordially extend the knowledge of Iranian culture to individuals of other backgrounds.
summary: The Iranian Student Association UK (ISAUK) serves as a vibrant hub for faculty, graduate students, and undergraduate students, focusing on both educational and recreational activities. Our objectives include preserving Iranian traditions and culture.

name: Changing Health Attitudes + Actions to Recreate Girls
description: CHAARG aims to ignite a passion in college-aged girls for health + fitness. Too many college girls rely solely on an elliptical. CHAARG liberates girls from the elliptical + shows them that fitness can [+ SHOULD] be fun! Since every girl has a different preference of what fun is, we create opportunities for our CHAARG girls to “find their fit.” We do this by partnering with local studios once a week to lead a workout, whether it be cycling, jazzercise, yoga, or kickboxing — and everything in between. We also have socials and weekly small group workouts to build a community of accountability partners!
summary: CHAARG aims to ignite a passion in college-aged girls for health + fitness. Too many college girls rely solely on an elliptical. CHAARG liberates girls from the elliptical + shows them that fitness can and SHOULD be fun!

name: Eta Kappa Nu - Beta Upsilon
description: Eta Kappa Nu is the official honors society of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE). Founded in 1904 at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, the organization currently consists of over 260 chapters and 200,000 members internationally. Our three ideals are Character, Attitude, and Scholarship. The Beta Upsilon Chapter at the University of Kentucky is the premier honors society in the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering (ECE).
summary: Eta Kappa Nu is the official honors society of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE). The Beta Upsilon Chapter at the University of Kentucky is the premier honors society in the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering.

name: Department of Education Graduate Students
description: This organization is for Graduate Students in the Department of Education. This group will host and organize research writing circles, research symposiums, social nights, and meetings to increase awareness of graduate events happening on campus relevant to the education community.
summary: This organization is for Graduate Students in the Department of Education. This group will host and organize research writing circles, research symposiums, social nights, and meetings to increase awareness of graduate events happening on campus relevan...

name: Student Veterans Of America
description: Student Veterans of America is a leading organization for veterans in higher education. With a network of over 1,500 on-campus chapters across the United States and overseas, SVA connects student veterans, provides resources, and advocates for their success. SVA chapters, representing over three-quarters of a million student veterans, play a crucial role in empowering tomorrow’s leaders. By engaging with mature chapters supported by SVA programs, advocacy, and research, student veterans can surpass their assumed potential and participate in a transformative experience tailored to their unique circumstances and needs.
summary: The SVA is aimed at providing student veterans with a like-minded community. We are a growing chapter that wants to gather student veterans in a place where they feel like they can be themselves, network with other veteran, gain a sense of belonging.

name: Business Law Society
description: The Business Law Society is an organization that provides law school students with the opportunity to gain information regarding the legal aspects of the business world. The organization will provide law students the opportunity to gain a deeper understanding of the convergence between the legal and business realms. This goal will be achieved through lunch discussions and networking opportunities with local businesses and attorneys.
summary:
The Business Law Society is an organization that provides law school students the opportunity to gain information and networking opportunities in areas of the law that intersect with business. 
Discussions and panels will be held for law school students.

name: Violence Intervention and Prevention Center
description: The Violence Intervention and Prevention (VIP) Center works with students, staff, faculty, and community partners to support survivors and people who support survivors in the aftermath of interpersonal violence.
summary: The Violence Intervention and Prevention (VIP) Center's mission is to support and advocate for individuals impacted by interpersonal violence while engaging UK and the surrounding community in interpersonal violence education and prevention.

name: Chinese Students Sports Club
description: Hold Sports events for Chinese student and faculty members, connecting Chinese students, and welcome other international students and local students
summary: Hold Sports events for Chinese student and faculty members, connecting Chinese students, and welcome other international students and local students

name: Club Dodgeball
description: The official University of Kentucky Club Dodgeball team. As a co-ed club, we compete in the National College Dodgeball Association, which has East Coast and Mid-West teams all with similar clubs. Practices are open to all UK students, no matter their background. At UK Dodgeball, we strive to make an inclusive and fun environment for everyone interested.
summary: The official University of Kentucky Club Dodgeball team. We compete in the National College Dodgeball Association with other co-ed club teams throughout the East Coast and Mid-West.

name: Black Law Students Association
description: The Black Law Student Association (BLSA) is an organization that values professionalism, networking and mentorship. BLSA was officially chartered in 1996, but our presence dates to an earlier time. Beyond supporting our members through seminars focused on legal research, outlining and exam tips, we forge relationships with alumni from all over the state and interact with minority leaders in the surrounding areas Currently, our chapter has 23 members representing ten states. We are a part of the Midwest Region of the​ National Black Law Students Association. NBLSA is a registered Title 26 § 501(c)(3) non-profit organization.
summary: ﻿The Black Law Student Association (BLSA) is an organization that values professionalism, networking and mentorship. BLSA was officially chartered in 1996, but our presence dates to a much earlier time. Beyond supporting our members through semin..

name: Doane Fischer Society
description: The Doane Fischer Society, also known as the Pediatrics Interest Group, offers educational and service learning opportunities for medical students at the University of Kentucky College of Medicine who are interested in a career in Pediatrics. Monthly meetings are held for students to hear from current residents and attendings about their careers in pediatrics, and various community service opportunities are offered throughout the year- including the opportunity to participate in our Fun Run program with children in the UK BMI clinic. Officers are currently working on expanding the educational opportunities available for the group.
summary: The Doane Fischer Society, also known as the Pediatrics Interest Group, offers educational and service learning opportunities for medical students at UKCOM who are interested in a career in pediatrics.

name: The Finance Society
description:
Our mission as The Finance Society in the Gatton College of Business and Economics is to provide students with a comprehensive and enriching experience in finance. We believe that by engaging and empowering students, we can help them develop the skills and knowledge they need to succeed in their careers.

 

Our goal is to create a supportive community that fosters creativity, innovation, and leadership in all areas of finance. Through a variety of programs, events, and activities, we aim to engage the student body and provide opportunities for personal and professional growth. 

​

We place a strong emphasis on providing first-class service to our members. This means that we are dedicated to delivering high-quality programs and events that meet the needs and interests of our students. Whether it's through networking opportunities, professional development workshops, or hands-on projects, we strive to provide our members with the resources they need to succeed. 

​

By constantly striving to meet our values of engagement, empowerment, and excellent service, we aim to develop the next generation of financial leaders and help them achieve their professional goals. Our mission is to provide a comprehensive and enriching experience for students, preparing them for successful careers in finance.
summary: The Finance Society serves as an organization that engages and empowers fellow students within the Gatton College of Business and Economics. Join us in exploring the ever-changing world of Finance through all that we have to offer this semester!

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers - Paducah Campus
description: The object of this Society shall be to foster the advancement and dissemination of knowledge of the theory and practice of mechanical engineering, to present a proper perspective of the professional engineer’s work, to provide an opportunity to become acquainted with the personnel and activities of ASME, and to promote an overall attitude of professional awareness and fellowship.
summary: The object of this Society shall be to foster the advancement and dissemination of knowledge of the theory and practice of mechanical engineering, to present a proper perspective of the professional engineer’s work, and adapt to modern engineer standard.

name: Alpha Sigma Phi
description: Alpha Sigma Phi is a social fraternity with over 100 active chapters, founded at Yale University in 1845. The Sigma Chapter at the University of Kentucky was founded in 1917, and re-founded in the Spring of 2016. The Sigma Chapter Currently consist of members that dedicate themselves to being a social, service, philanthropic, and academic beacon at the university.
summary: Alpha Sigma Phi is a social fraternity with over 100 active chapters, founded at Yale University in 1845. The Sigma Chapter at the University of Kentucky was founded in 1917, and re-founded in the Spring of 2016.

name: American Pharmacists Association - Academy of Student Pharmacists
description: The mission of the APhA Academy of Student Pharmacists (APhA-ASP) is to be the collective voice of student pharmacists, to provide opportunities for professional growth, to improve patient care, and to envision and advance the future of pharmacy.
summary: The mission of the APhA Academy of Student Pharmacists (APhA-ASP) is to be the collective voice of student pharmacists, to provide opportunities for professional growth, to improve patient care, and to envision and advance the future of pharmacy.

name: Anthropology Student Association
description: The ASA intends to cultivate and maintain a student-led community within the Anthropology department at UK that emphasizes belonging, engagement, and prioritizes students' connections with each other, faculty, and staff. As a collective, we advocate for students' wants and/or needs by acting as a liaison to the department administration. To address the need for academic application, we organize experiential, advisory, and networking workshops. Through these efforts, we hope to bring campus-wide awareness to our field and its applications in society, cultural preservation, and broader academia.
summary: The Anthropology Student Association is a collection of undergraduates interested in anthropology who intend to foster community within the Anthropology department, advocate for students' wants and needs, and bring awareness to our field.

name: Family and Civic Engagement
description: The Office of Family and Civic Engagement provides support, education, and engagement opportunities to students and their families with the purpose of helping them to successfully navigate the University of Kentucky experience and give back to our community in meaningful ways.
summary: The Office of Family and Civic Engagement provides support, education, and engagement opportunities to students and their families with the purpose of helping them to successfully navigate the University of Kentucky experience..

name: UK Club Swim Team
description: This is a club swim team open to all abilities! Whether you swam on a summer league team, high school, or club, our team is for you! Our mission is to promote a healthy lifestyle and provide UK students with the opportunity to swim and compete while also developing a friendly, supportive, and inclusive environment. Check out our website here for more information and details on how to join and follow our Instagram for updates throughout the season!
summary: We are a club sports team at the University of Kentucky. We proudly represent UK in swimming competitions across the country. At the CCS National Championship Meet in Spring 2025, the UK Club Swim Team sent 8 swimmers to compete in Arizona!

name: Rodeo Team
description: The team's mission is to promote and develop the sport of rodeo at UK, providing students with the opportunity to further pursue this sport educationally and competitively at the intercollegiate level. One of the main goals of the team is to give its members the chance to participate in activities covering every aspect of the rodeo industry, from learning about rodeo in general to competing as part of the team. The teams is open to all students, with or without prior rodeo experience.
summary: The team's mission is to promote and develop the sport of rodeo at UK, providing students with the opportunity to further pursue this sport educationally and competitively at the intercollegiate level.  One of the main goals of the team is to give its ...

name: Transformative Learning
description:
Transformative Learning is a centralized student support unit on campus, a student’s one-stop shop for on-campus academic resources, including Academic Preparation and Placement, The Study, and Academic Coaching.  Transformative Learning partners with student service programs and academic units across the university to enhance student retention and progress to degree. 

The mission of Transformative Learning is to enhance students' academic experience as early and as often as possible. The leadership team strives to achieve this mission by working closely with students, faculty, and staff across campus in the development and enhancement of programs that will best serve the entire campus community.
summary: Transformative Learning is a centralized student support unit on campus, a student’s one-stop shop for on-campus academic resources, including Academic Preparation and Placement, The Study, and Academic Coaching.

name: Underground Perspective
description: Underground Perspective is a student ran organization based at the University of Kentucky founded in 2014. Described by our members simply as "a family," we've created a unique space on campus that allows everyone, including those who feel they don't fit in, to have their voices heard while maturing and growing together as students.
summary: UGP builds a social community with diversity. We want to bring different people on campus and have them discuss real life events or important topics in our cultures and communities. All Students are welcome!

name: United Greek Council
description: The United Greek Council serves as the governing body for special-interest and culturally based fraternities and sororities at the University of Kentucky. The mission of UGC is to support a diverse community by creating a culture of respect, inclusivity, and empowerment for its member organizations.
summary: The United Greek Council serves as the governing body for special-interest and culturally based fraternities and sororities at the University of Kentucky.

name: Emerging Leaders Program
description: The Emerging Leaders Program (ELP) is a leadership program that helps students discover their personal leadership style while giving them the opportunity to develop leadership skills and competencies necessary to facilitate positive change on campus.
summary: The Emerging Leaders Program is a semester-long leadership program offered by the Office of Student Organizations and Activities.

name: Animal and Food Sciences Graduate Association
description: Open opportunities for fellow graduates to meet, interact, and learn about research within the department. Provide opportunities for professional development. Organize social gatherings for graduate interaction and discussion. Help new members discover opportunities at the University.
summary: Open opportunities for fellow graduates to meet, interact, and learn about research within the department. Provide opportunities for professional development. Organize social gatherings for graduate interaction and discussion. Mentor new grad students.

name: Special Olympics College Club
description: The Special Olympics College Club (SOCC) is a student-led chapter under Special Olympics Kentucky. We interact with young adults with disabilities from the Lexington Community through social and athletic-centered events. As a member, you will play an essential role in the success of these events as you will be an equal partner to your peer with a disability.
summary: Special Olympics College Club is a student chapter of Special Olympics Kentucky. The purpose is to connect college students and individuals with intellectual disabilities through sport to build friendships and promote the purpose of Special Olympics.

name: Studio Season Board
description: The Theatre & Dance Studio Season allows students to generate their own work in the department's facilities with the department's available resources while benefiting from the advice and supervision of the department's faculty and staff. Faculty encourage students to use Studio Season as a laboratory for experimentation in a variety of theatrical forms. Students are challenged to think creatively.
summary: The Theatre & Dance Studio Season allows students to generate their own theatrical work in the department's facilities with the department's available resources while benefiting from the advice and supervision of the department's faculty and staff.

name: Student Organization for Art History
description: An undergraduate/graduate student group for Art History & Visual Studies majors and minors to discuss career, research, and internship opportunities.
summary: An undergraduate/graduate student group for Art History & Visual Studies majors and minors to discuss career, research, and internship opportunities.

name: Sigma Alpha Sorority - Beta Omicron Chapter
description: Sigma Alpha is a professional, national sorority that promotes its members in all facets of agriculture. The sisters of Sigma Alpha strive for achievement in scholarship, leadership, and service, and to further the development of excellence in women pursuing careers in agriculture. The Beta Omicron chapter was founded at the University of Kentucky on March 23, 2013 by thirty young women with a passion for agriculture and a desire for sisterhood. Since it's establishment, Beta Omicron has been one of the fastest growing chapters across the country. They strive to create a sisterhood that is unlike any other offered at the University of Kentucky, one that promotes its members professionally as well as providing a social environment for sisters to grow in together.
summary: Sigma Alpha is a professional, national sorority that promotes women in all facets of agriculture. The sisters of Sigma Alpha strive for professional development, community outreach, and building a sense of sisterhood in agriculture here at UK.

name: Latino Student Union
description: Latino Student Union’s purpose is to unite and strengthen the Hispanic/Latino community on campus, while fostering a bond between students (Hispanic/Latino, or of cultural interest), faculty, and the community we will one day serve. In addition, it is our mission to create an environment where all demographics are made welcome to express themselves socially, athletically, academically, and in all aspects of life- both collegiate and professional. Lastly, the organization will uphold the pillars of scholarship, service, FUN, and family. We will strive to create a home away from home, a real support system for our members and associates.
summary: Latino Student Union is the largest Hispanic/Latino serving organization at UK. All are welcome!!!!

name: Student League of Independent Potters
description: SLIP (Student League of Independent Potters) is a ceramic student organization that centers upon building a community of students passionate about ceramics. The student members meet monthy to plan activities and trips during the year. Pottery sales are organized during the SA/VS Open Studio event every December. Funds from the sale help fund students travel and attend the National Council on Education for the Ceramic Arts (NCECA) annual conferences. SLIP also participates in events that serve the pubic such as the Empty Bowls benefits that raise money for people in need.
summary: SLIP is a student led ceramics club in which we build a community of ceramics students passionate about clay and ceramics.  We hold monthly meetings as well as host different events.

name: American Society of Health-System Pharmacists Student Chapter
description: ASHP represents pharmacists who serve as patient care providers in acute and ambulatory settings. The organization’s more than 40,000 members include pharmacists, student pharmacists and pharmacy technicians. For over 70 years, ASHP has been on the forefront of efforts to improve medication use and enhance patient safety.
summary: ASHP represents pharmacists who serve as patient care providers in acute and ambulatory settings.  The organization’s more than 40,000 members include pharmacists, student pharmacists and pharmacy technicians.

name: Grehan Associates
description:
Grehan Associates is a full-service student-run communications firm for students at the University of Kentucky studying related majors. The purpose of Grehan Associates is to provide clients with exceptional strategic communications services including, but not limited to, public relations, marketing and design. While doing so, Grehan Associates is developing top candidates for future employment by providing students with essential real-world experience.

If you are interested in becoming a member of Grehan Associates, please apply here.
summary: Grehan Associates is a full-service, student-run communications firm for students at the University of Kentucky studying related majors. The purpose of Grehan Associates is to provide clients with exceptional strategic communications services.

name: Student College of Clinical Pharmacy
description: The American College of Clinical Pharmacy (ACCP) is a professional and scientific society that provides leadership, education, advocacy, and resources enabling clinical pharmacists to achieve excellence in practice, research, and education.
summary: The American College of Clinical Pharmacy (ACCP) is a professional and scientific society that provides leadership, education, advocacy, and resources enabling clinical pharmacists to achieve excellence in practice and research.

name: Undergraduate Historical Society
description:
The purpose of the Undergraduate Historical Society is to unite and inform all those who are interested in the study of history and to extend opportunities within the field of history at the University of Kentucky. Finally, the organization hopes to provide a social yet academic setting for the study and discussion of relevant historical events. We welcome students of all academic interests to join!

You can join our GroupMe here: https://groupme.com/join_group/49074627/KWlhrSqD
summary: The purpose of the Undergraduate Historical Society is to unite and inform all those who are interested in the study of history and to extend opportunities within the field of history at the University of Kentucky.

name: Alpha Lambda Delta
description: Founded in 1924, Alpha Lambda Delta (ALD) is a United States national society that honors the academic excellence of freshman students. ALD provides a community of scholars, a platform to develop leadership skills, and financial support through scholarships and educational partners. ALD has been guiding students to academic and career success for 95 years. Accept membership and start your journey!
summary: Founded in 1924, Alpha Lambda Delta is a United States National Honor Society that honors the academic excellence of freshman students for their first-year successes.

name: Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation Interest Group
description: Established in the late 1940s, the field of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation (PM&R) was born out of need to meet the unique needs of individuals facing the aftermath of severe injury and disease. These are patients returning from war, patients recovering from stroke, patients altered by life-changing events. As an interest group, we are devoted to learning about PM&R and promoting it's core belief: To improve the functional well-being of patients despite physical impairment and limitations. This is a relatively new and growing discipline, and our group invites your participation and your energy to help fuel the next generations of PM&R healthcare specialists. Please join us in this great cause!
summary: The Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation Interest Group (PMRIG) was born out of need to educate medical students about the PM&R specialty.

name: Cardiology and CT Surgery Interest Group
description: The CCIG is a group for medical students which focuses on cardiology and cardiothoracic (CT) surgery. We meet each month and have speakers or workshops that deal with the different aspects of cardiology and/or CT surgery.
summary: The CCIG is a group for medical students which focuses on cardiology and cardiothoracic (CT) surgery. We meet each month and have speakers or workshops that deal with the different aspects of cardiology and/or CT surgery.

name: Society of Postdoctoral Scholars
description: The Society of Postdoctoral Scholars is a group for those interested in post-doctoral studies at the University of Kentucky. It is not limited to current postdocs, but also to interested graduate students and faculty mentors.
summary: The Society of Postdoctoral Scholars is a group for those interested in post-doctoral studies at the University of Kentucky. It is not limited to current postdocs, but also to interested graduate students and faculty mentors.

name: ChemCats
description:
ChemCats is a branch of a national organization for any students of any major interested in chemistry. By joining ChemCats, you are associating yourself with the American Chemical Society (ACS), the largest scientific society in the world and one of the oldest professional societies in the United States. ChemCats can provide you with many opportunities to serve your community, to engage socially with other people interested in chemistry, and to learn about the amazing research projects professors are working on right here at UK. All majors are welcome, not just chemistry!

 

Join our GroupMe! https://groupme.com/join_group/61980377/NMlT03LQ
summary: ChemCats is a branch of a national organization for any students of any major interested in chemistry.

name: Kappa Beta Gamma
description:
Kappa Beta Gamma is a social sorority under the United Greek Council dedicated to promoting sisterhood for anyone navigating college life. Our chapter creates meaningful connections among our members through fun events, involvement with our campus community, and volunteering efforts with the Special Olympics and Hope Lodge. We value diversity, equity, and inclusion in everything we do. 

                                                                                       We can't wait to meet you!

                                                                                                                        Love in Beta

name: Student Public Health Association
description:
Any student at the University of the Kentucky who is interested in public health issues can join regardless of their year in school or specific college they attend. Please contact any of the officers to obtain more information or email us at kentuckyspha@gmail.com.

 

The University of Kentucky Student Public Health Association (SPHA) is a student affiliate of the Kentucky Public Health Association (KPHA). We are a nonprofit organization for students of the University of Kentucky College of Public Health and other individuals interested in public health. The chapter was founded on September 21, 2000. The student chapter consists of an executive board of elected officials and an appointed Community Service Chair.

 

UKSPHA is a student-run organization and basic governing unit of the University of Kentucky College of Public Health. UKSPHA’s role is to act as a liaison between students and faculty, promote student involvement to develop healthy communities, and represent the needs of students of the College of Public Health.

 

Follow our social media! 

Twitter: @UKySPHA
Instagram: ukyspha

Facebook: UK Student Public Health Association
summary: USPHA is an official student group whose purpose is to integrate and unify students across the multiple graduate and undergraduate departments and programs at the UK College of Public Health and to create a consolidated voice to address student concerns.

name: Sigma Lambda Beta International Fraternity, Inc.
description:
To nurture and further a dynamic, value-based environment, that utilizes our historically Latino-based Fraternity as a catalyst to better serve the needs and wants of all people.

Multicultural, Historically Latino based Fraternity
summary: To nurture and further a dynamic, value-based environment, that utilizes our historically Latino-based Fraternity as a catalyst to better serve the needs and wants of all people.

name: Family Medicine Interest Group
