name: Food Science Club
description: The Food Science Club is for all students interested in studying food science and learning about the food industry. Food science is the study of applied sciences towards food production, processing, and preservation. We're a club that welcomes curious students to learn together, go on field trips, and interact with alumni and professors!
summary: The Food Science Club welcomes students from all disciplines to explore the world of food science!

name: Kappa Psi Pharmaceutical Fraternity
description: Kappa Psi is the largest professional pharmaceutical fraternity, targeted towards not only providing service to our community but also fostering the professional development of our members.
summary: Kappa Psi is the largest professional pharmaceutical fraternity, targeted towards not only providing service to our community but also fostering the professional development of our members.

name: Master of Health Administration Student Association
description: The Future Health Executives Society (FHES) is dedicated to enhancing the graduate experience for students in the University of Kentucky Master of Health Administration (MHA) program through collaboration, engagement, and leadership. The organization fosters a strong sense of community by coordinating service, networking, and social events that connect current students with peers, alumni, and healthcare professionals. FHES values the development of meaningful professional relationships and leverages these connections to enrich student learning and career growth. Additionally, FHES serves as the representative voice of MHA students, advocating on their behalf and strengthening communication with faculty, program administration, and the Board of Executives.
summary: We are the Future Health Executives Society (FHES), a student-led organization committed to building community, advancing professional growth, and amplifying the voice of MHA students at the University of Kentucky!

name: Philosophy Club
description:
A university is a conversation of ideas. The Philosophy Club at the University of Kentucky seeks to stimulate among its members, and the student body, a lively interest in activities and disciplines of an intellectual nature–whether that be science, philosophy, literature, art, etc. Philosophy club also provides an opportunity to partake in the free discussion of a wide range of philosophical topics. We aspire to foster fruitful encounter, a coming-together in open discussion, of the various concerns and interests of the very many departments and disciplines of the University of Kentucky! 

 

Join us for our weekly meetings from 1:00-3:00pm every Wednesday on campus at either POT 1455 OR POT 1443! This is a student-led discussion where undergraduate and graduate students have the opportunity to flourish in conversation within an intellectually diverse environment. Follow our social media below and stop by any week; let us get to know you!

 

ALL ARE WELCOME! Undergraduates, graduate students, staff members, and faculty are all encouraged to join!

Instagram: @uky_phiclub

Officer Contact Information: 

President– Michael Yeager: (270) 319 - 9060
summary: Philosophy is supposed to be fun (and it is)! Join us to explore the vast interconnectedness of various intellectual disciplines and delve into the nature of this world and our experiences with/within it. Philosophy is all around us; let's talk about it!

name: Material and Chemical Engineering Graduate Student Association
description: The Materials and Chemical Engineering – Graduate Student Association (MACE) was formed as a conduit for graduate students to interact through meaningful collaborations, social and cultural activities, exchange of intellectual property, access to physical assets, as well as interact together on a department level with faculty and staff. MACE was formed in the Fall of 2013 and has full standing and acknowledgement from multiple campus-wide organizations, including the Student Government Association, Graduate Student Congress, and the Engineering Student Council. MACE focuses its efforts on bringing together graduate students within the department in a variety of social and professional events throughout the academic year.
summary: The Materials and Chemical Engineering Graduate Student Association (MACE) serves as a conduit for graduate students to interact through meaningful collaborations and interactive social and cultural activities for personal and academic growth.

name: Medicine, Education, & Development for Low Income Families Everywhere
description: MEDLIFE is a service organization that works to give equal access to healthcare, education, and safer homes to those in need in South America and Africa. Medicine, education, and development are the three pillars on which MEDLIFE is based. MEDLIFE is currently serving communities in Ecuador, Nicaragua, Peru, Costa Rica, and Tanzania.
summary: MEDLIFE is a service organization that works to give equal access to healthcare, education, and safer homes to those in need in South America and Africa. Medicine, education, and development are the three pillars on which MEDLIFE is based.

name: Digital Resource Library
description:
This is a place for all student organization officers to come for resources, guidance, training, and support.

 

Student Organization Essentials

Student Organization Registration: This training covers the process, required materials and benefits of becoming a Registered Student Organization

Registered Student Organization Essentials: This training covers the essential skills, tools, and policies necessary for Registered Student Organization leaders 

Hosting Student Organization Events: This training covers the policies and procedures for hosting student organization events for registered student organization leaders 

Event Planning Basics: Whether organizing a small meeting or orchestrating a large social event, event planning is a huge task! Every event, no matter how simple or complex, requires detailed planning and organization. This workshop focuses on training and preparing student organization leaders to, understand applicable policies and procedures, grow as leaders, and execute their missions through events, competitions, and more

BBNvolved Management: This training provides the basics of managing organization BBNvolved pages for student leaders 

Maximizing BBNvolved Use: Level up your organization through BBNvolved! Did you know, BBNvolved allows you to market your events, recruit new members, and manage internal business? Join Student Organizations and Activities at this BBNvolved workshop to learn how your organization can maximize your BBNvolved usage and level us this academic year!

Office of Student Conduct Training for Student Organizations: Presented by the Office of Student Conduct, this training includes an overview of the student conduct process, important policies, and how to report violations of the Student Code of Conduct for Student Organizations 

Leadership & Skill Development

Telling Your Organization's Story:

Telling Your Organization's Story
There is no doubt that your organization does wonderful work, but how do you communicate that out to important stakeholders? This workshop will help you brainstorm what information your org has at the ready and how to turn that into a narrative for others to understand the impact you are having on campus and in the community! 

 

Time Management for Student Leaders:

Time Management for Student Leaders   
This workshop focus on example methods of time management, as well as what to do when you aren't sure what to prioritize in the first place! This is a beginner/intermediate level workshop best aimed toward those who don't know where to start with time management or who have difficulty prioritizing.


Transitions & Onboarding for Student Leaders:

Transitions & Onboarding for Student Leaders
Whether your organization is transitioning leaders this semester, or making a plan for next, join the Office of Student Organizations and Activities for a workshop to learn tips for strong officer transitions!

 

Delegating without Dropping - Student leaders face a delegation dilemma: "We can't do it all, but it all has to get done!" Come talk with an SOA staff member about what good delegation looks like. This nuanced discussion will deal with the common pitfalls and gray areas of effective delegation: How do you assess what is appropriate to delegate in the first place? How do you check in with your team along the way? When should you adjust?

 

Determining Success - Good vs Perfect:

Determining Success - Good vs Perfect
This workshops seeks to empower both students and their organizations to better navigate the challenges of perfection while embracing the resilience of 'good enough' in your personal and leadership journey.

 

Recognition, Appreciation, & Gratitude in Student Organizations: 

Recognition, Appreciation, & Gratitude in Student Organizations 
Learn how a culture of recognition within student organizations can lift up and motivate people within your organization. Explore how acts of recognition can have a ripple effect, influencing the overall attitude and well-being of those in your organization and beyond.

 

Sharing Involvement Experiences on LinkedIn:

Sharing Involvement Experiences on LinkedIn
This workshop will provide insights on sharing your involvement experiences as a student leader on LinkedIn.

 

Social Media - How to be Most Impactful: 

Social Media - How to be Most Impactful 
This workshop will provide you and your student organization with information and tools for social media best practices, application, and how to achieve the greatest impact when it comes to marketing, outreach, and promotion.


Membership Development - You've recruited new members for your organization; now what do you do to help them thrive? Join the Office of Student Organizations and Activities for a workshop on developing new and existing members.

How to Have Hard Conversations: 

How to Have Hard Conversations 
Hard conversations aren't always easy, but that does not mean they have to be scary! This workshop will address tips for preparing for difficult conversations, strategies to use while in the conversation, and advice for appropriate follow-up. Open to members and leaders of any organization on campus.

 

Networking & Collaboration - Networking comes in many different forms - from social media to in person connections. Connecting and creating a network with others is important for you and your organization’s growth. Join Student Organizations and Activities for this workshop to learn how to grow your network and effectively collaborate with others!

Motivating Others - It's important to know who your fellow org members are, and what helps them stay motivated to do great work. This workshop helps identify different personality assessments that get to the root of what individuals need to feel heard and contribute to the team using their strengths. It all starts with communication, and as folks can learn more about themselves they learn how they can work best with one another.

 

For Club Sports

 

Club Sports Risk Reduction Training: This training for Club Sports leaders covers best practices for risks like concussion, exertional heat stroke, first aid/ CPR, and weather related policies. 

Club Sports Organizational Training: This training for Club Sports leaders includes details on processes for Club Sports organizations include the assumption of risk waiver, FERPA release, and more

 

For Advisors 

RSO Advisor Workshop: Student Organization Registration:

RSO Advisor Workshop: Student Organization Registration
This Zoom recording details the RSO Registration process including the timelines, requirements and expectations for all student organizations seeking recognition. 

 

Resource Workshop for Registered and Sponsored Student Organization Advisors: This is a workshop about resources available to student organizations for UK faculty and staff advisors of Registered Student Organizations (RSO). This workshop will provide advisors with information on on-campus resources available to their RSO, an overview of applicable policy, and a review of the registration and event registration process.

Please contact studentorgs@uky.edu if you have additional training needs or with questions.
summary: This is a place for all student organization officers to come for resources, guidance, and support. Please visit getinvolved.uky.edu or contact studentorgs@uky.edu with questions.

name: Stuckert Career Center
description: The Stuckert Career Center is connecting passion with purpose through major and career exploration, job shadowing and internship opportunities, resume and cover letter critiques, practice interviews, graduate and professional school prep, and so much more. Participants will learn about majors and careers based on their personality type, how to become marketable to employers, and schedule appointments with our career advisors for any of our free services. We also host several career related events throughout the year for all UK students, faculty, and staff. We kick off our events with a Part-Time Job Fair, followed by Jobs and Internships Fairs, and Graduate and Professional School Fairs with employers and schools across the world.

These officers represent the graduate student body, acting as a bridge between students, faculty, and staff. ChemGSA addresses any questions and concerns from the students which may arise, and is involved in many departmental activities. The association also organizes social events which bring the students together for a break from their sometimes hectic schedules. It is the goal of ChemGSA to create an environment within the department which is friendly, and enriches the experience of the graduate students while completing their studies at the University of Kentucky.

description: We would like to welcome all new and returning Jewish students on campus! We provide a warm, welcoming, and affirming Jewish community that is open to all students. Come to make new friends and make some great memories. As a minority at the University of Kentucky, it is important for Jewish students to have a place where they can feel at home. Our goal is to ensure that students are able to explore their Jewish identity in whatever way they choose, and to empower students to create their own Jewish space at UK.
summary: We would like to welcome all new and returning Jewish students on campus! We fall under a religious organization but we try and focus on the social aspect of being part of a group as well. Come to make new friends and make some great memories.

name: Red Shoe Crew - Lexington
description:
The Red Shoe Crew is a national organization for young professionals partnered with the Ronald McDonald House Charities (RMHC) to provide support for ill children and their families as they receive care from some of the best hospitals in the country. The Ronald McDonald House in Lexington is located next to Kroger Field—just a short walk from campus! This makes it easy for our chapter to volunteer at the house by cooking dinner for the families, packing Lunches With Love, and partnering with the house to host large-scale philanthropy events/fundraisers for families. We have opportunities all throughout the semester, so you can be as involved as your schedule allows. This ensures you can be part of our low-commitment club while creating a big impact!

 

Interested in joining? Click here
summary: The mission of the Red Show Crew-Lexington is to empower University of Kentucky students to engage in community service and philanthropic activities, develop their leadership skills and social networks, and build awareness of the Ronald McDonald House.

name: NeuroCATS
description: NeuroCATS is an undergraduate student organization for Neuroscience majors, minors, and enthusiasts. The aim of our organization is to bring students together for social events, outreach opportunities, free peer tutoring, leadership opportunities, and networking events with faculty mentors. We actively participate in outreach activities in Lexington's elementary, middle, and high schools including tabling at science fairs and running after-school programs called NeuroKids. To join, please email us and ask to be added to our GroupMe (by also providing your phone number).
summary: NeuroCATS is an undergraduate student organization for Neuroscience majors and enthusiasts. The aim of our organization is to bring students together for social events, outreach opportunities, peer tutoring, and networking events with faculty mentors.

name: Quiz Bowl Team
description: The Quiz Bowl Team is an organization that competes in academic tournaments in the surrounding areas. Quiz Bowl is a trivia competition in which teams answer questions spanning across many different academic areas including Science, History, Literature, Fine Arts, and much more! For anyone familiar with the KAAC Governor's Cup competition, Quiz Bowl is very similar to Quick Recall, and for anyone else, it is somewhat similar to team-based Jeopardy! Quiz Bowl promotes the development of both a wide and deep knowledge base across whatever subjects you are interested in, and allows you to have fun at the same time! We practice on a weekly basis, attend collegiate tournaments of varying difficulties hosted in the surrounding states, and also host tournaments at the high school and collegiate levels. If you are interested in joining or have any questions, you can contact us at ukquizbowl@gmail.com.
summary: The Quiz Bowl Team is an organization that competes in academic tournaments in the surrounding areas. Quiz Bowl is a trivia competition in which teams answer questions spanning across many different academic categories.

The Student Media Depot @ The Hub is a student digital media space located in the Hub at William T. Young Library managed by Smart Campus. The Media Depot provides; access to recording equipment and space, editing stations with specialized multimedia software, and technical support for students’ development of their academic media projects.

The Media Depot aims to reduce any technological barriers for students in order to complete multimedia assignments that enhance learning. Faculty are encouraged to collaborate with the Media Depot for projects or assignments, allowing the Media Depot's trained staff to assist with software and equipment as needed.

Contact the Media Depot at 859-323-110

name: UK Men's Club Soccer
description:
Men's competitive club soccer travel team. This is an organized team with practice 2-3 times a week with games almost every weekend. We are a part of NIRSA region 2 and represent the University of Kentucky. We compete on a club collegiate level, playing against teams in our region in competitive play as well as surrounding colleges in scrimmaging play. Tournaments can be played throughout our scheduled season if we decide, as well as an end of the year regional tournament if we qualify which can lead to a national tournament if we advance. In the spring, the league is inactive but scrimmages with local schools are scheduled

Official tryouts are held at the beginning of the fall semester.
summary: Men's competitive club soccer travel team. This is an organized team with practices 2-3 times a week with games almost every weekend possible. We are a part of NIRSA Region 2 and represent the University of Kentucky.

name: Neurosurgery Interest Group
description:
The Neurosurgery Interest Group (NSIG) at the University of Kentucky serves as a hub for students passionate about neurosurgery, neurological sciences, and related fields. Our mission is to cultivate interest and provide students with the resources and opportunities to explore the intricacies of the brain, spinal cord, and nervous system from a surgical perspective.

Through a variety of hands-on workshops, including surgical simulation labs and anatomical dissections, members gain practical exposure to neurosurgical techniques and tools. We host guest lectures from leading neurosurgeons and neurologists who share their expertise, discuss cutting-edge research, and provide insights into the day-to-day life of a neurosurgeon. Members will also have the opportunity to engage in case discussions, attend research presentations, and participate in career development sessions, all designed to deepen their understanding of the field.

In addition to educational programming, NSIG offers robust mentorship opportunities. Our group connects students with faculty mentors and practicing neurosurgeons, creating pathways for shadowing experiences, research involvement, and guidance on navigating the medical school and residency application process.

Whether you’re a student just beginning to explore the possibility of a career in neurosurgery or someone already committed to the field, the Neurosurgery Interest Group provides a welcoming and supportive environment for growth and learning. We aim to inspire the next generation of neurosurgeons by fostering a community built on collaboration, curiosity, and professional development.
summary: The Neurosurgery Interest Group at UKCOM connects students with neurosurgery through events, mentorship, and research opportunities, fostering interest and professional development in this specialized field.

name: Kentucky Law Journal
description: The Kentucky Law Journal (KLJ) is one of the nation's oldest law reviews. The first issue was published in July 1881, and publication has been continuous since 1913. The Journal publishes four annual issues in print as well as online original articles and student notes. KLJ is edited by a student editorial board, with guidance from a faculty advisor. Each issue contains articles written by prominent national scholars and notes written by KLJ members that encompass a broad range of legal topics. KLJ's primary commitment is to publish scholarly articles of interest to the legal community. In this pursuit, KLJ strives to serve as an integral element in the process of education at the College of Law by providing staff members with legal research, writing, and editing experience and training. KLJ aspires to afford students an opportunity for collaboration, and by publishing student works, KLJ also functions as a forum for student ideas and activism. Beyond the College of Law, KLJ serves the legal and academic community as a medium for the publication of original scholarship and as an arena for vigorous debate about the law.
summary: The Kentucky Law Journal (KLJ) is one of the nation's oldest law reviews. The first issue was published in July 1881, and publication has been continuous since 1913.

name: Korean Language and Culture Club
description: The Korean Language and Culture Club (KLCC) is a student-run organization created in order to provide a space for University of Kentucky students interested in Korean to connect. The KLCC is devoted to promoting academic study of the Korean language and cultural exchange between Korean and American cultures. We are open to all those interested, regardless of whether your Korean level is native, beginner, or somewhere in between.
summary: The Korean Language and Culture Club (KLCC) is a student-run organization created in order to provide a space for University of Kentucky students interested in Korean to connect. The KLCC is devoted to promoting academic study of the Korean language.

Join our discord and follow our Twitter (@ukyesports) to stay up to date on club events and to connect with other students.

description: As Pi Kappa Alpha, we set the standard of integrity, intellect, and achievement for our members, host institution, and the community in which we live. PIKE is dedicated to developing men who are, above everything, Scholars, Leaders, Athletes, and Gentlemen.
summary: As Pi Kappa Alpha, we set the standard of integrity, intellect, and achievement for our members, host institution, and the community in which we live.PIKE is dedicated to developing men who are Scholars, Leaders, Athletes, and Gentleman

name: 57 Day Blood Club
description: The goal of this club is to promote blood donation by students as a way to give back to the community. We will work hand in hand with the Kentucky Blood Center and the Sigma Nu fraternity to host a blood drive every 57 days (minimum time to donate blood consecutively).
summary: The 57 Day Blood Club is committed to promoting blood donations among students to give back to the community.  We work in cooperation with the Kentucky Blood Center and the Sigma Nu Fraternity to host a blood drive every 57 days.

name: Collegiate Curls
description:
Our mission is: Uplift, empower, and support multicultural students as they embrace their natural beauty through healthy hair and skin care practices.

We want to be a voice for multicultural students. More importantly, we want to be a voice for multicultural students on the University of Kentucky’s campus. Through our various initiatives and programming, we hope that we can be an organization where students have a place to talk about current issues going on in their own lives as a minority student, help to guide them through their journey of being a student of color on UK’s campus, and to uplift and empower them through our various programming events.
summary: This organization was created to uplift, empower, and support multicultural students as they embrace their natural beauty through healthy hair and skin care practices.

name: Residence Life
description:
The Office of Residence Life’s mission is to intentionally create values-based living and learning communities that empower students and staff to engage in active learning, global citizenship, and self-discovery as they shape their Kentucky experience.

Living on campus is a great way to learn about campus traditions, get to know lots of people, and have new experiences. You will be close to classes, find your favorite place to eat, meet people from around the world, and develop friendships that will last a lifetime. You will find unique experiences in each community when you live on campus.

Living on campus has a positive impact on a student’s academic success. Students are more like to succeed and return for their sophomore year if they live on campus. We know that students are happier, more successful, and more involved when they live in residence halls. Our goal is to create a community where our students thrive and feel at home. Living on campus is a smart decision for not just your first year, but throughout your college career.
summary: Residence Life creates a home-away-from-home in the residence halls for our students. Through the efforts of all of our staff members, living on campus is an inclusive, safe, and fun living and learning environment for our residents.

name: Chellgren Hall
description: Opened in 2015, Chellgren Hall is located on the site of Cooperstown (originally Cooper Village) which was named for Thomas P. Cooper, a former dean of the UK College of Agriculture. Cooper Village originated in 1945 as a “G.I. City” consisting of 324 prefabricated housing units for married veterans. The prefabricated units were eventually removed and replaced in 1956 by seven graduate and family apartment buildings.
summary: This page is for the residents of Chellgren Hall to keep up with what is going on within the community.

name: Ball Hall
description: Opened in 2015, Ball Hall, formerly known as Woodland Glen II, is centrally located on the site of Cooperstown (originally Cooper Village) which was named for Thomas P. Cooper, a former dean of the UK College of Agriculture.  Ball Hall is home to the STEMCats Living Learning Program.
summary: For the residents of Don and Mira Ball Hall to keep up on what is going on within the community and how to get involved.

name: Pigman Hall
description: Opened in 2015, Pigman Hall is centrally located on the site of Cooperstown (originally Cooper Village) which was named for Thomas P. Cooper, a former dean of the UK College of Agriculture.  Pigman Hall is also home to the Engineering Living Learning Program and named for Stanley and Karen Pigman.
summary: For the residents of Woodland Glen 3 to know what is going on within the community and how they can get involved.

name: Woodland Glen 4
description: Opened in 2015, Woodland Glen IV is located within central campus on the site of Cooperstown (originally Cooper Village) which was named for Thomas P. Cooper, a former dean of the UK College of Agriculture.  Woodland Glen IV is home to the CAFE Living Learning Program.
summary: For the residents of Woodland Glen 4 to know what is going on within the community and how they can get involved.

name: Woodland Glen 5
description: Opened in 2015, Woodland Glen V is located within central campus on the site of Cooperstown (originally Cooper Village), named for Thomas P. Cooper, a former dean of the UK College of Agriculture.  Woodland Glen V is home to the Interprofessional Healthcare Living Learning Program (IHRC).
summary: For the residents of Woodland Glen 5 to know what is going on within the community and how they can get involved.

name: SBI Complex
description:
SBI Complex is located on South Campus and consists of three Residence Halls that were opened in 2005

Smith Hall, is named after John T. Smith, the first African American to earn a doctor of philosophy degree from the University of Kentucky. After earning his Ph.D. in education in 1961, Smith became the first African American to serve as a vice president in a UK administration.

Baldwin Hall, is named after Dale E. Baldwin, a former UK cheerleader.  

Ingels Hall, is named after Margaret Ingels, the first woman in the United States to earn a graduate degree in mechanical engineering. Her early research at UK and her other posts, including Carrier Co., pioneered efforts in air conditioning.
summary: For the residents of The Smith, Baldwin, and Ingels Complex to know what is going on in their community and how they can get involved.

name: University Flats
description: Opened in 2017, University Flats has two wings. One is home to our graduate, professional and postdoctoral students, and the other for undergraduate students of sophomore standing or higher.  UFlats remains open during all academic recesses of the University to accommodate students who require housing during these periods.
summary: For the residents of University Flats to know what is going on in their community and how they can get involved.

name: Lewis Hall
description: Opened in 2017, Lewis Hall, named after Tom Lewis, a native Kentuckian and UK graduate, successful businessman and philanthropist whose gift to the university made possible the creation of the Lewis Honors College. Lewis Hall is located in Central Campus and is home to the Lewis Honors College.
summary: For the residents of Lewis Hall to know what is going on within the community and how they can get involved.

name: Donovan Johnson Hall
description: Both Halls opened in 2013. Donovan Hall is named for Herman Lee Donovan, the fourth president of the University of Kentucky. Johnson Hall was named after Lyman T. Johnson, the first African American student to attend the University of Kentucky.
summary: A spot for Donovan and Johnson students to meet and learn about events and stuff going on in the halls

name: Haggin Hall
description: Opened in 2014, Haggin Hall is named for Mrs. James B. Haggin and her husband, James Ben Ali Haggin, founder of Elmendorf Farm.  Haggin Hall is home to the following Living Learning Program: Community of Scholars.
summary: For the residents of Haggin Hall to know what is going on within the community and how they can get involved.

name: Boyd Hall
description: Opened in 2016, Boyd Hall is named after Cleona Belle Matthews Boyd, who taught Greek and Latin and was president of the UK Woman’s Club in the early 1900s.  Boyd remains open during all academic recesses of the University to accommodate students who require housing during these periods.
summary: This page is for residents of Boyd Hall to learn about upcoming events, meet their staff living in the building, and connect to others living within Boyd Hall.

name: Holmes Hall
description:
Opened in 2016, Holmes Hall is named after Sarah Bennett Holmes, UK’s Dean of Women from 1942-1957.  Holmes is home to the Creative Arts Residential College Living Learning Program.

With a maximum capacity of 645 residents, Holmes Hall is one of the largest residence halls on campus at UK. Located on the corner of Limestone and Avenue of Champions, Holmes Hall is home to various Creative Arts spaces that are available for residents.
summary: For the residents of Holmes Hall to know what is going on within the community and how they can get involved.

name: Jewell Hall
description: Opened in 2014, Jewell Hall was named for Frances Jewell McVey, former assistant professor of English at UK and former dean of women at UK from 1921-1932.  The following Living Learning Programs are housed in Jewell Hall: CI Connect Community.
summary: For the residents of Jewel Hall to know what is going on in their community and how they can get involved.

name: Blazer Hall
description: Opened in 2014, Blazer Hall is named for Georgia Monroe Blazer, who served continuously on the UK Board of Trustees from 1939 to 1961.  Blazer Hall has the two-bedroom suite and four-bedroom suite room types.
summary: For the residents of Blazer Hall to know what is going on within the community and how they can get involved.

name: Lewis Honors College
summary: The mission of the Lewis Honors College is to better the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the world by helping students to explore their purpose, develop intellectually, and lead with integrity.is Honors College

name: UK Women's Ultimate Frisbee
description: We are the official women's club ultimate frisbee team of the University of Kentucky, nicknamed Monarchy. We compete in USA Ultimate sanctioned tournaments as a part of the East Plains section of the Great Lakes region. Our preseason takes place in the fall semester and consists of 3-4 tournaments. Once our athletes return from winter break, our regular season begins, and we typically compete in 3-4 sanctioned tournaments around our region. Our first official year as a women's team was 2018. NO experience necessary and ALL are welcome!
summary: The University of Kentucky has a women's ultimate frisbee team! Whether you have never played or have played a lot, all are welcome! Please email us at kultladies@gmail.com for further details.

name: Office of LGBTQ* Resources
description:
Follow us on Instagram and Facebook @ukylgbtq. BBNvolved membership open to stay updated.

LGBTQ* Resources is University of Kentucky's central hub for accessing information, groups, and services related to diverse sexual orientations and gender identities. We are a campus-wide office that supports a welcoming, safe, and supportive place for students, faculty, staff and alumni. We also seek to connect and collaborate with community groups and friends across the City of Lexington and the Commonwealth of Kentucky.

Our mission focuses on the three core pillars of education, advocacy, and community building. We are actively engaged in supporting student organizations, offering professional development opportunities for campus groups, and advocating for lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, and queer individuals from many walks of life. Our ultimate goal is to highlight the wonderfully diverse experiences of LGBTQ* Wildcats while working to decrease marginalization in all forms.
summary: LGBTQ* Resources is University's central hub for accessing information, groups, and services related to diverse sexual orientations and gender identities. Our mission focuses on the three core pillars of education, advocacy, and community building.

name: Campus Recreation
description:
The mission of Campus Recreation is to provide quality recreation programs and services for the campus community that support student success and promote healthy lifestyles.

Learn more about our facilities, fitness, aquatics, intramurals, and our outdoor adventures programs here or on our website.
summary: The mission of Campus Recreation is to provide quality recreation programs and services for the campus community that support student success and promote healthy lifestyles.

name: UK Eventing Team
description: A student run club sports team made up of dedicated intercollegiate eventers. The team attracts students to University of Kentucky from all over the country with one common interest; eventing. The team turns an individual sport into a team effort, where student feel understood by their peers who are also combining school and horses. We do this through competing together, volunteer opportunities, team bonding, hosting events.
summary: A team of UK students with an interest in Three Day Eventing. We are made up of about 100 full time students who compete in USEA intercollegiate events.

name: Energy Club
description:
The Energy Club at University of Kentucky is a medium for UK students and faculty that share an interest in energy-related issues to network and learn about energy.

The specific goals of this club are: 

(1) Build a community of students and professionals who are interested in energy technologies and policy

(2) Educate members about current energy issues, the future of energy technology, energy challenges, the oil and gas industry, and renewable energy technologies.  This will be done through seminars, monthly meetings, company tours, conferences, and other events. 

(3) Provide professional development opportunities for members, namely opportunities to network with professionals in academia, professionals in industry, and each other

To join, please send a membership request on BBNvolved and attend one of our monthly meetings.

For further information, please visit our website (linked on this page under contact information), refer to our constitution (in documents section of our BBNvolved page), or contact us through BBNvolved.

Be sure to check us out on Instagram (@UK_EnergyClub).
summary: The Energy Club at University of Kentucky provides a medium for any student who is interested in energy-related issues to learn more about these issues through seminars, company tours, and conferences while networking with each other and professional

name: Kentucky Karana
description: Kentucky Karana is the University of Kentucky's premier nationally competitive Fusion dance team. We blend Eastern and Western dance forms into multicultural performances that showcase modern South Asian arts and culture. No prior dance experience is required to join, everyone is welcome!
summary: Kentucky Karana is the University of Kentucky's premier nationally competitive Bollywood-fusion dance team.

name: Joseph Hamburg Student Society
description: Provide framework in which to perform while in the Physician Assistant Studies Program. Ambition of the framework is to help formulate academic achievement and clinical excellence as a compassionate health professional.
summary: Physician Assistant Studies Class of 2026 Student Society

name: Creativity Club
description:
Creativity Club is not just a club for people who would like to create something artistic, but it is for anyone who would like to accomplish their dreams, physicalize their ideas, and make connections with the real world. If you have a dream, if you would like to achieve something that you've dreamt of, this might be the perfect club for you! 

Fill out the following FORM and we will get back to you ASAP!

We also have our GroupMe!
summary: Has anyone told you that your dreams are impossible? Has anyone inhibited you from imagining and dreaming freely, letting go of your creativity? Creativity Club is a club that pursues the impossible dreams to make them happen!

name: French and Francophone Club
description:
French Club's goal is to build community among UK French students and French speakers through shared appreciation of language and culture. All are welcome, no matter your experience level. Our events range from game nights, cultural presentations, conversation practice, to watching films in French! We hope to see you at our next meeting!

If you are interested in joining us at our next meeting, we recommend you follow our Instagram account or join our GroupMe for information on meeting times and location:

GroupMe:https://groupme.com/join_group/111090588/K8YdFJyA

Instagram: @frenchclubatuky

Any emails or inquiries can be addressed to: frenchclubatuky@gmail.com
summary: French Club welcomes you to participate in French and Francophone language and culture immersion. Our goal is to facilitate your French language studies no matter your experience level!

name: First-Generation Student Organization
description:
The FGSO is designed to support, educate, and promote the needs of students who are the first in their families to go to college and/or first generation Americans.

We are a group of students that help facilitate outreach to students via Canvas and student lead events.

Complete this form after applying to join! FGSO Interest Form

Join our GroupMe: GroupMe
summary: The FGSO is designed to support, educate, and promote the needs of students who are the first in their families to go to college.

name: Infectious Disease Interest Group
description: To promote an interest and explore the medical specialty of infectious diseases among UK medical students.
summary: To promote an interest and explore the medical specialty of infectious diseases among UK medical students.

name: Beta Beta Beta National Biological Honor Society
description:
Beta Beta Beta (Tri-Beta) is an honor society for students, particularly undergraduates, dedicated to improving the understanding and appreciation of biological study and extending the boundaries of human knowledge through scientific research.

Our Mu Lambda chapter, in particular, focuses on fostering relationships between biology students of diverse backgrounds. We know that biology classes can be quite large which makes it challenging to get to know our peers. Thus, we wish to provide every member with opportunities to get involved on campus and network so that each person feels part of a larger community.

Follow us on Instagram: @tribetauky
summary: Beta Beta Beta (Tri-Beta) is an honor society for students, particularly undergraduates, dedicated to improving the understanding and appreciation of biological study and extending the boundaries of human knowledge through scientific research.

name: Student Philanthropy Board
description:
The Student Philanthropy Board’s purpose is to support, promote, and facilitate the philanthropic efforts of UK students and faculty on campus, in Lexington, and around the world through the distribution of information, student engagement, and the fostering of relationships with community partners. We operate out of the Center for Student Philanthropy and Alumni Engagement in Gatton Student Center Room A260. 

Please submit the 2025-2026 New Member Application Form found below!
summary: The Student Philanthropy Board’s purpose is to support, promote, and facilitate the philanthropic efforts of UK students and faculty on campus, in Lexington, and around the world.

name: Ratio Christi
description: The mission is to equip university students and faculty to give historical, philosophical, and scientific reasons for following Jesus Christ. The purpose for which the Ratio Christi, Inc. (Ratio Christi) is organized is to encourage and strengthen the faith of Christian students at public and private institutions around the world, while sharing Christ's message and love with those who have not yet accepted Him. By situating apologetic clubs at universities nationally and internationally, Ratio Christi will take part in the battle for the mind by encouraging dialogue and stimulating discussion directed towards secularism and answering life’s pressing questions.
summary: The mission is to equip university students and faculty to give historical, philosophical, and scientific reasons for following Jesus Christ...

name: Indian Student Association
description: The organization wants to help Indian subcontinental students to connect with other university students and exchange in each others' diverse cultures. To enhance the students' experience at the university, the organization wants to help students get involved in cultural festivals and make life-long friendships with other students. Another focus of the organization is to help students achieve success in their academic careers through different activities hosted by the organization.
summary: The Indian Student Association is focused on the cultural indulgence of Indian subcontinental students, and creating a platform to improve college experience and the sense of unity.

name: Sigma Phi Lambda
description: Sigma Phi Lambda is a non-denominational Christian sorority that strives to glorify God by providing a source of fellowship for college women who sincerely seek to know His person, His will, and His ways.
summary: Sigma Phi Lambda (ΣΦΛ) is a non-denominational Christian sorority that strives to glorify God by providing a source of fellowship for college women who sincerely seek to know His person, His will, and His ways.

name: Model United Nations (MUN)
description:
Model United Nations at the University of Kentucky is a debate, diplomacy, and travel club all rolled into one. We simulate the United Nations, represent real countries, and tackle real world issues like climate change, security, and human rights. Along the way, members build skills in public speaking, negotiation, research, and writing in a supportive, low pressure environment.

No prior experience is required. We teach you how to write resolutions, speak like a diplomat, and navigate conference rules. Members can:

  • Attend regional and national Model UN conferences

  • Take part in on campus simulations and crisis scenarios

  • Join workshops on diplomacy, negotiation, and UN procedure

  • Meet people who care about global politics, law, and international affairs

If you’ve ever wanted to argue policy, change the world on paper, or just find “your people” who like international relations, Model UN is your place to start.

Join our Groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/96305745/TOLltJwt
summary: Model United Nations at the University of Kentucky is an undergraduate and graduate student organization where members represent countries, debate global issues, attend conferences, and build diplomatic skills. No experience is required.

name: STEMgiQueers
description: STEMgiQueers is the student org for LGBTQIA+ people and allies in STEM! We help our community connect with, support, and uplift each other through weekly meetings and occasional conferences. We welcome everyone, no matter your college or major!
summary: STEMgiQueers is the student org for LGBTQIA+ people and allies in STEM! We help our community connect with, support, and uplift each other through weekly meetings and occasional conferences. We welcome everyone, no matter your college or major!

name: CAFE
description: The Agriculture, Food and Environment (CAFE) Living Learning Program welcomes first-year students and supports their transition to college life. Through shared living in Woodland Glen, experienced peer mentoring, off-campus field trips, and community service projects - this exclusive community provides students with the framework they need to apply their knowledge, passion, and skills to local and global issues at the intersection of agriculture, food and the environment.
summary: Agriculture, Food and Environment Living Learning Program (CAFE LLP)

name: Audre Lorde Community
description: The Audre Lorde Social Justice Living Learning Program is for first-year students who are interested in social justice advocacy for identities like race, ethnicity, sexual orientation and gender identity. The rooms in the Audre Lorde SJ LLP are gender inclusive and are housed in Blazer Hall.
summary: The Audre Lorde Social Justice Living Learning Program is for first-year students who are interested in social justice advocacy for identities like race, ethnicity, sexual orientation and gender identity.

name: CI Connect Community
description:
For first-year students enrolled in the College of Communication and Information.

This community provides students who have declared a major or minor in the College of Communication and Information the opportunity to "connect" with other students, college faculty, and staff, all from the comfort of Jewell Hall.   This community encourages students to blur the lines between living and learning since program activities and monthly events will take place inside the residence hall.  The connected course is specifically designed for majors and minors in the college and will explore everything the college and UK have to offer.

Residence Hall: Jewell Hall
summary: This community provides students who have declared a major or minor in the College of Communication and Information the opportunity to "connect" with other students, college faculty, and staff, all from the comfort of Jewell Hall.

name: Creative Arts Residential College
description:
For students with a strong interest in the creative and/or performing arts.

The Creative Arts Living Learning Program is for students with a strong interest or major in the creative or performing arts—from dance, music, and theatre to visual arts, creative writing, design, and arts administration.  Holmes Hall will house solo and ensemble music practice rooms, a performance studio with projection capabilities for dance, music and theatre, an art studio and a writing studio for a truly immersive living-learning program, located within three blocks of the Fine Arts Building, the Singletary Center for the Arts, and School of Art and Visual Studies Building. First-year students will benefit from the support of Peer Mentors already engaged with the arts at UK. Faculty from the College of Fine Arts and the College of Arts and Sciences will teach connected courses that count toward the Arts and Creativity and/or Humanities requirements of the UK Core and organize programming uniquely customized for "creative types."

Residence Hall: Holmes Hall
summary: The Creative Arts Living Learning Program is for students with a strong interest or major in the creative or performing arts—from dance, music, and theatre to visual arts, creative writing, design, and arts administration.

name: EDLife Community
description:
For any College of Education student interested in teaching.

This community offers education-related programming, school visits, mentoring, and networking opportunities; regular engagement and support from faculty and upper-class Peer Mentors; opportunities to participate in hands-on instructional activities with students of all ages. Connected coursework provides students exposure to a variety of educational topics as well as an exploration of school settings, including the STEAM (science, technology engineering, arts, and mathematics) Academy high school and Districts of Innovation.

Residence Hall: Jewell Hall
summary: This community offers education-related programming, school visits, mentoring, and networking opportunities; regular engagement and support from faculty and upper-class Peer Mentors

name: Engineering Living Learning Program
description:
The Engineering Living Learning Program (ELLP) offers a unique opportunity to Pigman College of Engineering students who are passionate about pursuing a career in engineering or computer science to live together in a community focused on one goal – academic success. This residential experience complements classroom expectations by integrating students into the engineering community with programs centered on professional development and enhancement of the classroom experience. In addition, the ELLP provides students with opportunities to interact with Pigman College of Engineering deans, faculty, ELLP alumni representatives from the industry, advisors and other staff to assist students in their pursuit of a degree. Hand-selected peer mentors help students meet the academic expectations of the engineering program and excel in academic excellence, while also helping them transition into the University of Kentucky. Finally, each student will be assigned an ELLP, and will have the opportunity to participate in hands-on engineering classes and projects with other engineering students in a state-of-the-art classroom located within the ELLP on the first floor of Pigman Hall.

Residence Hall: Pigman Hall
summary: This community provides a supportive learning environment that increases student engagement and prepares participants for a successful engineering/computer science academic experience.

name: International Village
description:
For first-year students.

The International Village provides students with a meaningful international experience right on UK’s campus. Conveniently located in beautiful Blazer Hall, students in the International Village enjoy learning about the world from and with each other. Room assignments pair students from differing backgrounds, and dedicated programming offers opportunities to explore others’ cultures and develop international friendships.Through cultural and recreational programming as well as through connected coursework, students will have regular interactions with faculty, upper class peer mentors, and staff dedicated to supporting their academic success at the University of Kentucky.

Residence Hall: Blazer Hall
summary: The International Village provides students with a meaningful international experience right on UK’s campus.

name: KHP/Wellness Community
description:
*KHP & Wellness are now one Community

The Kinesiology and Health Promotion (KHP) and Wellness Community (KHPW LLC) is a residential experience designed for students who share a common interest in health and wellness, and/or interested in pursuing a career in the areas of exercise science, physical education, or health promotion. Students in this community are exposed to social and professional activities allowing for an exploration of the various avenues associated with kinesiology and health promotion. These include interaction with peers and faculty, connected courses, engagement with upper-class peer mentors, and programming dedicated to professional development, networking, and community-building. Participating students are also encouraged to develop and maintain a healthy lifestyle while leading and education others to do the same.

Residence Hall: Woodland Glen IV
summary: he KHP/Wellness Community is a residential experience designed for students who share a common interest in health and wellness, and/or interested in pursuing a career in the areas of exercise science, physical education, or health promotion.

name: Wildcat Coal Lodge
description: The Wildcat Coal Lodge sits next to Memorial Coliseum in North Campus and is the residence hall for the men’s basketball team. It opened in 2012, replacing the previous Joe B. Hall Wildcat Lodge.
summary: The Wildcat Coal Lodge sits next to Memorial Coliseum in North Campus and is the residence hall for the men’s basketball team. It opened in 2012, replacing the previous Joe B. Hall Wildcat Lodge.

name: UK Powerlifting Club
summary: This club is encouraging the physical well-being and empowerment of students through the promotion of strength-based training. Whether you are beginner, advanced, looking to compete, or to just have a group of people to workout with, come join us!

name: Student Dental Business Organization
description:
This group is for those passionate to know and understand more of the challenges and opportunities encompassing dental business.  It will be geared to all levels of knowledge so that anyone eager to learn can improve in a judgment free environment.  No minimum knowledge required.

Class will be lead by student officers and supervised by faculty.  Meeting will be designed on a lecture/discussion mixture and guest speakers will be invited to lecture over topics of past discussions; main focus being to help with a real life and more in-depth understanding of challenges and opportunities within dentistry.

Organization is designed upon a 3-4 year focus plan. This way each year foundational basics will be discussed with the hope of not repeating topics for 2nd and 3rd year students.
summary: Student Group passionate about learning more of the non-clinical aspects of dentistry.  Curriculum to be organized from lectures, books, and presentations. Guest speakers will also present in their respective fields.

name: If/When/How
description:
If/When/How is a national network of law students and legal professionals from all backgrounds who know that reproductive justice doesn’t just happen.

It takes thoughtful action and strategic activism: acknowledging the intersection of identities, collaborating across disciplines, and working toward a critical transformation of the current legal system. Because ensuring that all people can decide if, when, and how to create and sustain families depends entirely on if, when, and how hard we fight.

Our legal system should support and advance reproductive justice for all. But all too often, it functions as yet another vehicle of reproductive oppression – we exist to change that.
summary: If/When/How is a national network of law students and legal professionals from all backgrounds who know that reproductive justice doesn’t just happen.

name: The Apollo Society
description:
Our goal is to support and provide resources for students in the Lewis Honors College who are interested in healthcare. We are a service-oriented organization. We have many activities which allow students to partake in community service, build their resume, and be a part of a great community. We also have many mentoring and networking opportunities for people looking to better prepare themselves to apply to their pre-professional school. We have meetings twice a month for an hour. 

 

GroupMe Link: https://groupme.com/join_group/102562797/Qe4QhH5I
summary: This organization is for any student that is in the Lewis Honors College and is a Pre-Health major. This includes Pre-med, Pre-PT, Pre-PA, Pre-Pharm, Pre-Dent, etc.

name: Parents Attending Law School
description: Parents Attending Law School (PALS) is a student-led organization focused on supporting law students who are also parents and/or caregivers. Parents and caregivers of all ages, with dependents of all ages are welcome! Law school can be challenging for most, and being responsible as a primary caregiver complicates matters further. This is a group of parents and caregivers, designed for parents and caregivers to engage in meaningful fellowship and fun family activities, while providing support and information to incoming non-traditional law students, returning students, and the UKRCOL community as a whole.
summary: PALS aims to support law students who are also parents and/or caregivers. Law students off all ages, with dependents of all ages are welcome!

name: Create Your Positivity
summary: The purpose of CYP is to improve the overall wellbeing of all members of the College of Pharmacy

name: Tau Beta Pi - KY Alpha
description:
Tau Beta Pi is the only engineering honor society representing the entire engineering profession. It is the nation's second-oldest honor society, founded at Lehigh University in 1885 to mark, in a fitting manner, those who have conferred honor upon their Alma Mater by distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as students in engineering, or by their attainments as alumni in the field of engineering, and to foster a spirit of liberal culture in engineering colleges.

There are now collegiate chapters at 248 US colleges and universities, 44 active alumni chapters in 16 districts across the country, and a total initiated membership of approximately 594,000.

Our chapter is active in supporting the NewLife Day Center, God's Pantry, and Lego League Regional Competition. We also sponsor the College of Engineering Monte Carlo Night, TBP Lego Speed Bent Competition, TBP College of Engineering Awards Banquet, and the TBP Most Outstanding Graduating Senior, Teaching Professor, and Staff awards.
summary: Tau Beta Pi is the only engineering honor society representing the entire engineering profession. It is the nation's second-oldest honor society and is reserved for those who have exhibited distinguished scholarship and exemplary character.

name: ChemE Car
summary: ChemE Car is a car that starts and stops using a chemical reaction. The organization competes regionally at the AIChE Southern Regional Conference in the Spring. It is made up of multiple teams that each focus on one aspect of the car.

name: Squirrel Watching Club
description:
Do you like squirrels? The Squirrel Watching Club is a safe space for any student to enjoy campus wildlife! We take walks across campus each week to bond with each other and observe nature.

 

Interested? Join our GroupMe!
summary: Do you like squirrels? The Squirrel Watching Club is a safe space for any student to enjoy campus wildlife! We take walks across campus each week to bond with each other and observe nature.

name: Chinese Club
description:
The Chinese Club is open to all students interested in the Chinese language and culture. If you like Chinese and want to learn more about it, then you are definitely encouraged to join the Chinese Club!

Chinese Club allows for interactive ways to learn more about Chinese languages and culture through every meeting. 

Our mission is to foster interest in Chinese culture and allow students to engage in Chinese conversation and activities related to their daily life. All levels of Chinese students are welcome to join in the fun!

Join us on groupme: https://web.groupme.com/join_group/96207039/0XGuPuIk
summary: The Chinese Club is where everyone can experience a taste of the fascinating Chinese languages and culture through various activities alongside friends.

name: SpaceLex, Rocketry and Propulsion
description:
We are the University of Kentucky’s competitive rocketry and rocket propulsion team. As a skilled group from almost every discipline of engineering, we work to develop sounding rockets built to test the limits of modern spaceflight. Our vehicles are all designed and built from scratch by students, including the fiberglass airframe and fins, wiring for flight computers, machined engines, and our tested solid fuel. With every project we develop, we seek to be the first at it. Over summer 2024, we placed first at our first ever national competition with Meridian, our first competition rocket. We hope to continue to succeed at future competitions and push the boundaries of rocketry.

Our mission is to push the amazing state of Kentucky further into the aerospace industry. We want “Kentucky” and “Aerospace” to be nearly synonymous as we continue to display what engineers from our state can do. We accomplish this not only through our in-depth community outreach program and being “Kentucky Local”, but also through our incredibly innovative projects that push the boundaries of what defines aerospace. We believe space is for everyone, therefore all students regardless of major that are passionate about aerospace can join our team and get valuable hands-on experience in this field.
summary: We are the University of Kentucky’s first ever rocketry and rocket propulsion team. As a skilled group from almost every discipline of engineering, we work to develop sounding rockets built to test the limits of modern spaceflight.

name: Catholic Medical Association
summary: As a chapter of the largest association of Catholics in healthcare, we support healthcare professional and pre-professional students through fellowship, leadership and service opportunities.

name: Sawbones Orthopaedic Interest Group
description: We welcome all medical students interested in orthopaedic surgery to join our student-led interest group at the University of Kentucky College of Medicine.
summary: We welcome all medical students interested in orthopaedic surgery to join our student-led interest group at the University of Kentucky College of Medicine.

name: Engineers Without Borders
description: Engineers Without Borders USA-UKY is the University of Kentucky chapter of a wider national organization. Our purpose is to partner with underserved communities at home and internationally to build a more sustainable world. These community-driven partnerships address essential needs with climate conscious infrastructure while cultivating engineering and leadership skills in our partners and volunteers. We are always in need of new students dedicated to making an impact in their community through engineering and volunteering.
summary: Engineers Without Borders USA builds a better world through engineering projects that empower communities to meet their basic human needs. Our highly skilled volunteers work with communities to find appropriate solutions for their infrastructure needs.

name: Gatton Student Center
description: An energetic and vibrant hub of the University of Kentucky Campus since 1938. From the wide range of dining options to our entertainment and educational opportunities there’s so much more in between the lines: UK Bookstore, UK Federal Credit Union, PNC Bank, free wireless internet access and free laptop use, meeting/conference rooms, two theaters with state of the art media technology, WRFL radio, The Cats Den recreational and entertainment facility as well as the Center For Student Involvement. Additionally, students will find numerous student services, programs and organizations all within the Center. We invite to you explore and enjoy!
summary: Gatton Student Center

name: The Impact Movement
description: The Impact Movement exists to provide a place where students of all backgrounds can learn about the gospel and fellowship with other students. A major focus of our group is community. Our goal is to create a rich community that stretches all across campus. It is our hope that this community leads to genuine friendships that extend past Bible study and into real life. Whether you have been in the church all your life or you're just interested in seeing what Bible study is all about, come and fellowship with us! We would love to have you. All are welcome.
summary: The Impact Movement challenges and encourages students to embrace how their identity is integral to the ministry of the Gospel on campus, in the community, and throughout the world.

name: UK Libraries Outreach
description: As the premier research library in the commonwealth, UK Libraries provides ever-expanding access to quality information resources, teaching and learning programs and services, and excellent learning spaces. UK Libraries plays an essential role in the University’s goal to elevate the quality of life and enhance the intellectual and economic capital within Kentucky.
summary: UK Libraries is integral to teaching and learning, research, and outreach.  We strive to advance the information literacy skills of students through active classroom instruction, online tutorials, and course-specific web pages.

University Health Service (UHS) is your student health clinic at UK.

As a full time student, the student health fee is included in your tuition which allows you to be seen in our clinic for primary care, gynecology, behavioral health, & health and wellness services!

You can make an appointment through your MyChart Account or by calling the appointment line.

Primary and GYN/GU Care: 859-323-2778

Behavioral Health: 859-323-5511

We offer an abundance of health and wellness programming and also have student groups including Peer Health Educators, Sexperts, and the Student Health Advisory Council (SHAC)!

description: Bangladeshi Student Association is a non-profit, non-political, autonomous, student-led, and student-governed organization.The goal of this organization is to promote and highlight the rich and diverse culture, history, and tradition of Bangladesh by organizing multifarious events throughout the year. It also aims to provide services, representation, and welfare support to its members.
summary: Bangladeshi Student Association is a non-profit, non-political, autonomous, student-led, and student-governed organization that represents the culture, history and tradition of Bangladesh.

name: Nepali Student Organization
description:
The purpose of NSO shall be to Promote understanding, friendship, integrity, and cooperation amongst NSO members at University of Kentucky(UK).

  • Sustain, promote and exchange Nepalese language, literature, culture, and arts through various activities.
  • Promote discussions on topics of national and international areas of interest amongst its members as well as members of other organizations.
  • Provide peer counseling and advising to Nepalese students at UK.
  • Plan and coordinate social and cultural activities with other organizations.
  • Participate in community activities outside of NSO.
  • Arrange special functions to commemorate various Nepalese holidays and festivals.
  • Provide information and assistance to new and prospective Nepalese students.

Our members want to create a community for Asian Pacific Islander Desi Americans in the UKY and Lexington community. We seek to promote cultural awareness and diversity, create a strong sxsterhood, and serve the community.

We welcome those who want to advocate Asian awareness to join our Everlasting Sisterhood®
summary: Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. is a founder of the National APIDA Panhellenic Association (NAPA). We are Dedicated Female Leaders® that emphasize Loyalty, Honesty, Respect, Dedication, Integrity, Discipline, and Academic Excellence.

name: Christian Legal Society
description:
We are a group of law students who meet weekly to study God's Word and fellowship with the goal of equipping future Christian lawyers, both individually and in community, to proclaim, love, and serve Jesus Christ and our community. 

"And what does the LORD require of you? To act justly and to love mercy and to walk humbly with your God." -Micah 6:8
summary: Group of law students who meet weekly to study God's Word and fellowship with the goal of equipping future Christian lawyers, both individually and in community, to proclaim, love, and serve Jesus Christ and our community in fulfillment of Micah 6:8.

name: Graduate Society of Women Engineers
description: GradSWE is a student organization at the University of Kentucky affiliated with the Society of Women Engineers. While we have many of the same goals as the SWE section already established on campus, we strive to meet needs of graduate students not currently met by the primarily undergraduate SWE section. Primarily, we serve as a support system for hard-working graduate students, and are committed to their academic and professional development. We are gender-inclusive, and welcome faculty members to join meetings as well!
summary: GradSWE is a student organization at the University of Kentucky affiliated with the Society of Women Engineers. We are committed to the academic and professional development of graduate students in engineering and related fields.

name: Student Service Organization
description:
The Student Service Organization is dedicated to volunteering, participating in philanthropic activities, and fostering friendships among members. Some of the philanthropic activities we aim to pursue include, but are not limited to:
  • Writing letters to patients at local hospitals to uplift their spirits
  • Creating cards for residents of nursing homes
  • Organizing restaurant nights with proceeds donated to selected organizations
  • Hygiene and toiletry drives for the Hope Center 
  • Providing students with volunteer opportunities within the community, both as a group and individually
summary: An organization to get together and volunteer with other organizations around Lexington with similar missions of service. We do activities as a group to bring people together sharing the same interest in helping the community.

name: College of Fine Arts
summary: College of Fine Arts

name: Kentucky Advocates for Science Policy and Research
description: We strive to educate interested persons in science advocacy and policy issues by 1) providing resources to learn about the field of science policy; 2) discuss past and current science policy issues; 3) teaching members how to appropriately respond to science-related policy decisions, including but not limited to press releases, position statements, letters to members, and forums; and 4) provide opportunities to engage in policy discussions with policy makers. We also provide opportunities for members to learn and practice science communication skills by 1) providing resources for members to learn and practice communication skills and 2) provide opportunities to perform outreach activities.
summary: The Kentucky Advocates for Science Policy and Research (KASPR) is a group that aims to educate interested persons in science policy and advocacy and to provide opportunities for members to learn and practice science communication.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Clay Chapter
description:
Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International is a professional law fraternity advancing the integrity, compassion, and courage through service to the student, the school, the profession, and the community. Phi Alpha Delta is the preeminent law fraternity promoting the bonds of fraternalism and we are the leader in the development and advancement of professional ideals. 

One out of every five attorneys is a member of Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, including prominent lawyers, judges, U.S. Presidents, and five current U.S. Supreme Court Justices. Leadership opportunities within the Clay Chapter, the University of Kentucky College of Law chapter of Phi Alpha Delta, are abundant and accessible to all members and attendance at district and national conferences provides excellent networking opportunities.

Phi Alpha Delta is committed to providing academic, professional, and social development opportunities for all of its members. Our most notable events and offerings include our members-only outline and supplement library, cocktail hour lectures with professors and alumni, and our widely popular semi-annual Keeneland Tailgate. We look forward to improving our programming every year with the goal of fostering an open and supportive academic environment as well as building strong bonds and fellowship between our student members, professors, and the community at large.
summary: College of Law Phi Alpha Delta is a professional law fraternity advancing the integrity, compassion, and courage through service to the student, the school, the profession, and the community.  This chapter is meant for current law school students.

name: Alpha Alpha Alpha Honor Society
description:
Alpha Alpha Alpha (Tri-Alpha) Honors Society promotes academic excellence and provides opportunities for personal growth, leadership development, and campus and community service for first-generation college students. 


The Society encourages and rewards academic excellence among first-generation college students pursuing a bachelor’s degree in accredited institutions of higher learning.
summary: Alpha Alpha Alpha (Tri-Alpha) Honors Society promotes academic excellence and provides opportunities for personal growth, leadership development, and campus and community service for first-generation college students.

name: Geoscience Graduate Group
description: The Geoscience Graduate Group is the overarching representative, governing body for all graduate students in the Department of Earth and Environmental Sciences. Its membership is restricted to students in the master's and PhD programs. G3 acts as the main body of communication between the graduate representative to the faculty and the department representative in the Graduate Student Congress. This organization strives to be an inclusive organization for all EES graduate students.
summary: The G3 is the official graduate student organization for the Department of Earth and Environmental Sciences (EES). All graduate students enrolled in a graduate degree program in EES are automatically members of the organization.

name: Kentucky Science Olympians
description:
The Science Olympians are the student body behind the Kentucky Science Olympiad. Background in STEM is not necessary! See details below.

 

What is the Science Olympiad?

As of 2018, the University has become home to the Kentucky Science Olympiad. The Science Olympiad is a national science competition that engages middle and high school students. The students become engaged in STEM fields as they form teams to compete in a variety of events. Events vary; students may be asked to take a rigorous test, design a robot, or create an airplane!

In addition to hosting the state tournament every year, the Kentucky Science Olympiad State Office, here at UK, is responsible for the support of students, coaches, and directors across the state.

It is the mission of the Science Olympiad to engage students in science and to encourage them to commit their lives to STEM education and research.

Who are the Science Olympians?

The Science Olympians are the student backbone of the Kentucky Science Olympiad. They support the state office in the execution of its mission to foster student involvement in STEM and lead the way in getting the campus community involved in this special event.

Why Join?

There are many benefit to being a Science Olympian, such as:

  • Connect to other students with similar interests, hobbies, and passions.
  • See leadership and involvement opportunities.
  • Resume building and diversification.
  • Involvement in a nationally recognized institution sponsored by industry leaders such as NASA, Lockheed Martin, the Center for Disease Control, and many more.
  • Accrue volunteer hours.
  • Take on an important role in the educational development of our state and nation's youth.
summary: Kentucky Science Olympians unites Science Olympiad alumni, STEM majors, and anyone passionate about science education. We build community, support Kentucky Science Olympiad State Tournament, and inspire STEM engagement across the Commonwealth.

name: Future Professionals in Global Health
summary: We work to expose healthcare students to the importance of culturally competent care through meetings, service projects, and guest speakers. We encompass all professions and cultures, and no language knowledge is required to join!

name: National Organization of Minority Architecture Students
description: The National Organization of Minority Architecture Students (NOMAS) mission is to champion diversity within design professions by promoting academic excellence, community engagement, and professional development of its members.
summary: The National Organization of Minority Architecture Students (NOMAS) mission is to champion diversity within the design professions by promoting the excellence, community engagement, and professional development of its members.

name: Joint Council Operations Team
