summary: Council officers and Fraternity & Sorority Life staff.

name: First-Generation Scholars LLC
description:
The First-Generation Living Learning Community is a residential experience designed to support first-generation college students and assist them with their transition from high school into college life at UK. Academic success and enhanced sense of belonging are promoted through a connected course with their peers, increased faculty and staff interaction, as well as intentional programming efforts that include cultural enrichment experiences, social interactions and community service involvement.

First-Generation LLC residents will have access to Peer Mentors who are students that have either previously lived in our community or are passionate about helping students navigate their Wildcat experience. As a member of the First-Generation LLC, you will also have connections to programming, staff, and faculty that provide additional support to help guide you through your first semesters at UK.

All students in First-Generation LLC students have a connected course during the fall semester: UK 101 (or college equivalent course) for first-year students.
summary: First-Generation Living Learning Commuinity

name: First-Generation Faculty & Staff Advocates
summary: First-Generation Faculty & Staff Advocates

name: Fellowship of Women in Dentistry
description: Meetings will include guest speakers in a wide variety of specialties, fellowship activities, dental school info sessions, as well as community service! Come join us to learn more about the field of dentistry and network with fellow pre-dental students!
summary: This organization aims to provide a community for women interested in dentistry and to obtain experience in the field of dentistry and community service.

name: Creator Cats
description:
Creator Cats is a student organization that invites students interested in film production to connect and collaborate with peers who have similar passions. Creator Cats provides a space for students to create collaborative works in areas such as screenwriting, producing, directing, editing, and more. We are dedicated to assisting and encouraging the artistic growth of students at any experience or interest level. Come create with us!

FOR UPDATES CHECK OUR INSTAGRAM @creatorcats
summary: Creator Cats is an organization for students interested in the filmmaking process. We provide tools and community that will help students pursue their creative passions. With a focus on directing, editing, screenwriting, and more, creativity is key!

name: Vitals
description:
To bridge the gap between medicine and the humanities, to create a forum where medical students, physicians, and the administration of UKCOM feel comfortable sharing their stories and embracing their creativity. 

What is vital in your life? Is it your family, your career, your art? How do you process the world and your experiences? Vitals is a creative blog started by medical students at the University of Kentucky College of Medicine (UKCOM) to bridge the gap between medicine and the humanities. We invite submissions of poetry, prose, photography, video, art in all of its forms from students, residents, physicians, faculty, and administration of UKCOM.
summary: To bridge the gap between medicine and the humanities, to create a forum where medical students, physicians, and the administration of UKCOM feel comfortable sharing their stories and embracing their creativity.

name: Fiber Arts Club
description: The Fiber Arts Club is oriented to help teach the practice of various fabric arts and to facilitate a community of crafters. We encourage any and all fiber arts (such as crochet, knitting, cross-stitch, etc.), and we teach beginners! Join us on GroupMe to stay updated on club activities: https://groupme.com/join_group/109195720/XzYRrEDP
summary: The Fiber Arts Club (formerly Knitting Club) is oriented to help teach the practice of various fabric arts (such as crochet, knitting, cross-stitch, etc.) and to facilitate a community of crafters.

description:
The purpose of the Linguistic Graduate Student Association (henceforth LGSA) is to enhance the
personal and professional experiences of graduate students in linguistics and the language
sciences. This includes but is not limited to: facilitation of service and mentoring opportunities;
collaboration with Linguistics department faculty; advocacy for relevant campus issues at the
University of Kentucky.
summary: We are a graduate student organization dedicated to the spread of linguistic knowledge and awareness on the UK campus and beyond. The club is welcome to both graduate and undergraduate students. although undergraduate students will be non-voting members.

name: Asian/Asian American Association
description:
AAA is an inclusive organization that serves to not only bring AAPI individuals together, but also to welcome anyone interested in AAPI cultures/issues while creating an open, safe space for everyone. We have celebrated festivities from various AAPI cultures and hosted discussion-based events that spark interesting questions regarding social issues that AAPI individuals face. AAA has also collaborated with other organizations on campus to host major cultural events.

We only hope to continue contributing to the diversity of UK and encouraging more AAPI representation on campus, and we welcome you to join our journey :)
summary: AAA is open to anyone interested in AAPI cultures! Our goal is to harbor a safe space for members of the AAPI community and to advocate for the interests of AAPI culture on campus. We also emphasize the importance of representation and education.

name: Turning Point USA
description:
Turning Point USA at the University of Kentucky is the fastest-growing conservative student organization on campus! Our mission is to...

Educate students on the importance of fiscal responsibility, free markets, and limited government. 

Identify student activists on campus who believe in limited government and individual liberty.

Empower young activists to get involved in the fight for free markets and limited government, through building a strong campus network.

Organize young activists in chapters and networks across the country. TPUSA believes there is strength in organization.

Mobilize students who believe in free markets. TPUSA mobilizes its networks to get involved in activism, issue advocacy, public policy education, and grassroots organization.

Register students to vote.
summary: Our mission is to identify, educate, train, & organize students on the importance of fiscal responsibility, free markets, and limited government.

name: Analytics Club
description: Analytics club exposes students to applications of Business Analytics and potential career paths, provide training in Analytics tools, methodologies and software, and promote the Business Analytics Minor and other related programs at the University. summary: Analytics club exposes students to applications of Business Analytics and potential career paths, provide training in Analytics tools, methodologies and software, and promote the Business Analytics Minor and other related programs at the University.

Criteria for scholarships vary but generally include academic performance, financial need, community affiliations, and unique elements important to the sponsoring organization.

description:
The Center for Graduate and Professional Services (CGPS) has a mission to develop a community of leaders in the academy, industry, and society.

 
 
 
 

Learn more about our office and resources here!
summary: Center for Graduate and Professional Services

name: A Caring Place
description: Our goal is to involve the youth in the effort to serve those in our community affected by loneliness, a disease that 40% of our local community faces per the AARP. Through online and in-person service, research, and advocacy, we provide opportunities for our members to get involved in the movement against this ever-growing disease. During the school year, we will hold hybrid meetings to discuss and explore opportunities.
summary: Promoting understanding of loneliness among youth and young adults by providing opportunities to help and serve those affected by the disease in our community.

name: Martin School of Public Policy & Administration
description:
The James W. Martin School of Public Policy and Administration is an academic, research, and service unit of the University of Kentucky Graduate School. It was named in honor of Dr. James W. Martin, a scholar, public servant, and teacher whose accomplishments have created a lasting legacy of scholarship and service.

Starting in 1976 with one program - the Master of Public Administration (MPA) - the Martin School now has seven programs: 

-Bachelor of Arts in Public Policy

-Master of Public Administration

-Master of Public Policy

-Master of Public Financial Management

-Graduate Certificate in Public Financial Management

-Graduate Certificate in Nonprofit Management

-PhD program in Public Policy and Administration

Find out more about our programs here: martin.uky.edu 

Make a Difference. Start Here.
summary: Martin School of Public Policy & Administration

name: Association of Data and Computation
description: All students of the Computer Science, Computer Engineering, and Data Science departments at the University of Kentucky are eligible to be members of ADC. The ADC will not discriminate in membership based on race, color, creed, religion, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, gender, sexual orientation, age, or disability. The ADC will arrange monthly general meetings for the members, organize social and professional beneficial events, create a communication bridge between faculty and graduate students, and help graduate students build a strong network among themselves.
summary: ADC fosters closer relationships among students of Computer Science, Computer Engineering, and Data Science. It provides social activities, outreach programs, and professional development experiences for graduate students.

name: College of Education Student Success
description: College of Education Student Success
summary: College of Education Student Success

name: Cru Grads
description: Cru Grads is a Christian ministry for graduate students at the University of Kentucky. Cru strives to create a family, captivated by the joy of knowing Christ, united under the call to make Him known.
summary: Cru Grads is a Christian ministry for graduate students at the University of Kentucky. Cru strives to create a family, captivated by the joy of knowing Christ, united under the call to make Him known.

name: Association of Women Surgeons
description: AWS is a not-for-profit educational and professional organization whose mission is to inspire, encourage, and enable women surgeons to realize their professional and personal goals.
summary: AWS is a not-for-profit educational and professional organization whose mission is to inspire, encourage, and enable women surgeons to realize their professional and personal goals.

name: DanceBlue Committee
description:
DanceBlue is a University of Kentucky student-run organization that fundraises year-round and culminates into a 24-hour no-sitting, no-sleeping dance marathon. Money raised through DanceBlue is donated to the Golden Matrix Fund, established to support the kids of the DanceBlue Kentucky Children’s Hospital Hematology/Oncology Clinic both today and well into the future through an endowment. DanceBlue also helps to provide funds to support research at the UK Markey Cancer Center. Through over 20 years of DanceBlue, more than $25 million has been raised to support this cause.

This page serves as a communication source to our 190 student committee. Please refer to DanceBlue: The University of Kentucky Dance Marathon page for updates, forms, and events.
summary: DanceBlue Committee

name: Alternative Service Breaks
description:
The University of Kentucky’s Alternative Service Breaks (ASB) program connects students to service-learning opportunities across Kentucky and the U.S. Through weekend and spring break trips, ASB offers hands-on service experiences focused on a variety of social issues such as disaster relief, education, and community development. Whether traveling as close as Appalachia or to another region of the country, students gain new skills, friendships, and a deeper understanding of community impact through service.

Participation in ASB extends beyond the trip itself. Students take part in orientation sessions, educational programs, service projects, and reflection activities throughout the academic year. Opportunities to get involved include applying for a trip, joining the ASB student organization, or serving as a site leader or on the executive team. ASB encourages students to use their experience as a starting point for continued community engagement and leadership.
summary: University of Kentucky (UK) Alternative Service Breaks (ASB) is a program run by students, for students. We go beyond the borders of Lexington and Kentucky and offer you an alternative way to spend your academic breaks!

name: Leadership and Service Community
description:
This living learning program is open to any residential student who has a desire to develop and enhance their leadership skills while becoming active citizens in their local, regional, and global communities. Students will form relationships with others who share these interests and will learn from others student leadership experiences and develop a great awareness of diverse perspectives. 

The community is rooted in strengths-based leadership, with all members of the community utilizing the Gallup CliftonStrengths program to better understand strengths related to personal persistence and leadership opportunities. All community members will take the Gallup CliftonStrengths assessment at the beginning of their first-year in the community, and the focus on strengths, along with the active citizenship continuum, will be the foundational philosophies for this community. 

Residence Hall: Holmes Hall
summary: This living learning program is open to any residential student who has a desire to develop and enhance their leadership skills while becoming active citizens in their local, regional, and global communities.

description:
The purpose of Lewis House is to provide a space for members of the

University of Kentucky community to reflect on the relationship between faith and

thinking. It invites learning, reading, and discussion around the biggest questions

of life, including history, philosophy, theology, ethics, and politics. The primary

emphasis is upon a rigorous engagement of perennial concerns, utilizing the

intellectual resources of the Christian tradition.
summary:
The purpose of Lewis House is to provide a space for members of the
University of Kentucky community to reflect on the relationship between faith and
thinking.

name: Wildcats First
description: This program would serve as a campus-wide collaboration dedicated to addressing the academic success, belonging and engagement, health and wellness, and financial stability of first-generation college students. Enrichment opportunities will help students gain the knowledge, skills, and experiences that contribute to their academic and personal success.
summary: Wildcats First

name: College of Social Work
description: For over 80 years, the College of Social Work (CoSW) has been a leader in social work education. Our mission is clear: Through rigorous research, excellence in instruction, and steadfast service, the CoSW works to improve the human condition. Always, in all ways. Students in our Council on Social Work Education (CSWE) accredited BASW program interact with world-class faculty, staff, and a diverse cohort of students. Students gain practice and research experience in a variety of settings, including clinical social work, schools, child welfare, gerontology, community organizing, mental health, and international practice, among others. Graduates from our program are uniquely poised to enter a competitive job market and make the most of forecasted growth within the social work profession.
summary: For over 80 years, the College of Social Work (CoSW) has been a leader in social work education.

summary: Student Transitions and Family Programs

• To increase the exposure and knowledge of dental students about oral and maxillofacial surgeries and procedures.
  • To provide an opportunity for dental students to collaborate with their fellow peers and utilize their talents to create a professional environment conducive to advance knowledge in OMFS.
  • To be a resource to students interested in pursuing a residency program in oral and maxillofacial surgery.
  • To stipulate a platform to learn from experienced oral surgeons and dental providers from the University of Kentucky and abroad.
summary: To increase the exposure and knowledge of dental students about oral and maxillofacial surgeries and procedures. We plan to accomplish this by having guest speakers, case presentations, study support, and opportunities to explore surgical techniques.

summary: SMILE Club exists to promote random acts of kindness, to dismantle the stigma associated with mental health, and create a supportive community on our campus. SMILE Club works in conjunction with the Rae of Sunshine Foundation.

Aside from our academic programs, we also host career readiness, networking, wellness and social events to help you make the most out of your time in CI and prepare you for a successful career beyond UK. Our College also houses several student organizations that CI students may get involved with to explore their passions, including:

name: Circolo Italiano

name: College Libertarians
description:
The purposes of the College Libertarians at the University of Kentucky are as follows:

  • To build a libertarian order, in which all individuals are sovereign to pursue virtue in their own lives with the use of their private property and with the willful cooperation of others
  • To promote respect for individual rights as an essential precondition for a free and prosperous world — all while balancing this with the value of family, community, and national ties
  • To defend each person’s right to engage in any activity that is peaceful and honest — with a view towards a world in which individuals are free to pursue their dreams out of their own volition without interference by the state
  • To educate the citizens of tomorrow as to the promises of liberty through discussion of articles, speeches, debates, treatises, and current events 
  • To support the campaigns of Libertarian candidates for political office
summary: Supporters of the Libertarian Party, the third-largest political party in the United States, committed to seeking a world of liberty.

Our focus is to share the diversity of Latin American culture, music, and food through practicing and speaking Spanish at our meetings. We are open to all people interested in the Spanish language—both native and non-native speakers. We aim to foster an open, comfortable environment where members can improve their conversational skills.

Follow us on Instagram to stay up to date with us!! @hayquehablaruk
summary: Hay Que Hablar is a Spanish speaking club within the University of Kentucky and proudly Latino led. Our focus is to share the diversity of Latin American culture, music, & food through practicing and speaking Spanish at our meetings. Open to all!

name: Wildcat Involvement and Leadership Development
description:
Wildcat Involvement and Leadership Development is a 6 week long program that is focused on supporting your involvement at UK!

 

As a group, we will learn more about our strengths and values, identify communities to get involved in, and create an action plan to continue to engage in the UK community. 

Here's some things we hope you take away from this program:

  • Reflecting on your previous and current experiences with belonging and involvement
  • Learn more and be able to describe your values and Strengths
  • Be able to identify communities and areas for involvement at UK
  • Developing a connection with at least one other participant

name: Neurodiverse Educational Social Initiative
description:
• The Neurodiverse Educational Social Initiative (NESI) is a program by the Disability Resource Center. With the support of staff across the University of Kentucky, it is designed to supplement existing services offered across the University specifically in creating a sense of belonging and educational opportunities for neurodivergent students. NESI is open to all students, regardless of formal documentation of disability. All that is required is a desire to learn about neurodiversity and find a space to belong at UK. NESI is offered at no additional cost to students
  • NESI is designed to be staff led and student directed. On a weekly basis, students will meet to participate in activities including board games and snacks or other events. These meetings will include discussions of social topics and life skills, which may include guest speakers that are knowledgeable on the topic. The schedule of these discussions is determined based on student input and interest. Students can also join our GroupMe to connect with students outside of meeting hours. Students will also be referred to other resources at UK that partner with NESI to work with neurodiverse students.
  • NESI is not a counseling program and does not replace therapy or other psychological treatment options.
  • For more information or to sign up, please contact Arty Fitzpatrick, Disability Accommodation Consultant at Arty.Fitzpatrick@uky.edu
  • If you would like to schedule a meeting with Arty to learn more about NESI, please fill out this Google Form (clicking the link will open a new window). Please allow 2-3 business days for a response. Note that NESI follows the same hours as the DRC, which is not open on the weekends.
summary: NESI is a program by the Disability Resource Center, designed to create a sense of community, belonging, and educational opportunities for neurodivergent students, including autistic and socially anxious students

name: Presentation U
description: We are the University of Kentucky’s centralized tutoring center for speaking, writing, visuals, and all other forms of communication. We support UK’s efforts to graduate confident communicators by providing services to undergraduate students, graduate students, staff, and faculty. We currently offer both live and asynchronous online tutoring. Students can receive free tutoring for communication projects in any form for any course, including research papers, essays, lab reports, presentations, infographics, ePortfolios, and iPad related projects. Tutoring can be delivered face-to-face online or on written assignments submitted online.

name: Real Estate Club
description:
We are a student-led organization that aims to empower and equip fellow students at the Gatton College of Business & Economics with the tools they need to succeed in the competitive world of real estate. Our focus is simple yet effective: The Real Estate Club serves as a gateway to career opportunities, industry insights, and a robust network that would otherwise be hard to come by in college.

Through a series of carefully curated events, the Real Estate Club brings the world of real estate to campus, providing our members with access to the industry's top players. We partner with firms such as Keller Williams, SVN, and more to provide exclusive opportunities to our members, resulting in successful internships and job placements.

Whether you are seeking a summer internship, want to get more involved on campus, or are passionate about the industry, the Real Estate Club is the great starting point for your professional development journey. Reach out to realestateclubuky@gmail.com to learn more or join us in the upcoming 2026 Spring Semester on our BBNvolved page!
summary: The Real Estate Club at the Gatton College of Business & Economics is a student-led organization that aims to provide its members with access to career opportunities, industry insights, and a robust network in the world of real estate.

Members have the opportunity to enhance their understanding of neuroscience concepts and foster a sense of camaraderie among peers. WIN is committed to giving back through community service projects. Whether it's organizing educational workshops or volunteering at local schools, our members actively engage in initiatives that make a difference in the lives of others. We are always eager to welcome new members.
summary: WIN is an organization dedicated to supporting and empowering women in the field of neuroscience. Through outreach and mentorship, we strive to provide women with the opportunity to connect, learn, and grow together in the field of neuroscience.

name: NKY Pediatric Interest Group
description: As interest in pediatric medicine increases with the increasing student population at NKY, there has become a need to support the interest of the students here at UKCOM-NKY. This can be done by using the NKY Pediatric Interest Group as a homebase for students with an interest in pediatrics to find all of the resources and information that they require. This organization will provide an avenue for pediatric students to meet locally and be able to work as a close knit group to work on service projects that are important to them as well allowing local students to voice their opinions and questions about their future pediatric careers in a more personal environment. Much of the support that students require needs local attention such as finding local mentors, research opportunities, and pediatric based community service activities that are accessible to students. Creating the NKY Pediatric Interest Group as a local organization provides students with a close knit local support system from creating a strong future in pediatric medicine here at NKY.
summary: The NKY Pediatric Interest Group will serve as a place for students who are interested in pediatrics to network, find related service opportunities, and learn about the skills and mindset associated with becoming a pediatrician.

name: Unbridled
description:
What is Unbridled?

We are a TEDx-style talk series and a student organization at University of Kentucky College of Medicine. We host events for speakers to share their stories of humanity in medicine. This is a wonderful avenue to connect medical students with residents, physicians, and administrators on a human level, removing the name tags of "student" and "faculty" and just becoming people relating to each other.

 

What do we do?

We bring together stories and ideas from medical students, residents, and attendings. Sometimes this is done with a theme featuring a series of 3 speakers. Our phenomenal speakers speak vulnerably and authentically, sharing in their advice and life experiences. Everyone has a story to tell and this is our way of broadcasting those stories. 

 

Additionally, we will have workshops/"master classes", interviews, and Q&As. The "master classes" will be led by attendings and residents with particular passions who can give "pro tips" to rising clinical students on how to do any number of things in clinical medicine. 

Please note that this organization is meant for medical student membership only. Thank you.
summary: We are a TEDx-style talk series. We host events for speakers to share their stories of humanity in medicine. Medical students, residents, and attendings share stories and ideas through talks, interviews, Q&A sessions, and master classes.

name: Zine Club
description:
What is Zine Club?

Zine Club is a relaxed and inclusive club where UK students and community members come to create art based on a different theme each meeting.
The work created is then collected and distributed as a collaborative zine!

Each member of the club will receive a copy of the previous meetings zine at the start of the next meeting.

What is a Zine?

Zines are self published magazines including art, writing, photography, and anything else you can print on a page. They are created as pieces of artwork, platforms to spread information, and as a space to come together as a community under a publication.
summary: Zine club is a space for any and all majors to learn the art of zines!

name: Circus Club
description:
• What is Circus Club? 
    Circus Club is a community that learns and shares knowledge about the circus and circus arts, often through first hand experience and practice of circus skills such as juggling, acro-balancing, and aerial arts.
  • I don't know any circus skills. Can I still come? 
    Absolutely! The circus club is for every level of those interested in circus, from beginner to more seasoned circuser. Everyone is welcome.
  • What should I wear? 
    Comfortable work-out clothing is recommended. Please wear clothing that allows a wide range of movement, isn't too loose or ill-fitting (baggy clothes can get caught on equipment), and preferably covers knees and elbows. Layers are a good idea. For many of the skills (aerial arts, acro-balancing), you will need to be barefoot (no shoes). 
  • I don't know how to juggle, and I have no coordination. (Or: I don't know aerial arts, and I have no upper body strength.) Can I still do it? 
    Absolutely! People engage in circus arts to acquire strength, flexibility, and to increase hand-eye coordination; it is not assumed that we have them before we start. Circus Club will have skill share days, lessons, and practices where you can work on basic skills to increase strength, flexibility, and coordination. With practice, anyone can learn to juggle, do aerial arts or acro-balancing.
  • Is it dangerous? 
    There is an assumption of risk with all physical activities on campus, including circus activities. Minimizing these risks is one of Circus Club's top priorities. To that end, we have taken precautions such as rigging regularly inspected by trained professionals, safety mats, spotters, and members who are trained in the skills we will be practicing. Skills will be level-appropriate and heavily spotted; no one will attempt activities beyond their abilities. 

    We adhere to the University of Kentucky's assumption of risk policy: "Participants understand that physical activities contain an element of inherent danger including serious injury or even death. Participation in all activities is on a voluntary basis at the individual’s own risk. All participants are strongly encouraged to undergo a physical examination and consult with their personal physician indicating fitness level appropriateness for strenuous activities and to carry medical insurance coverage‚ prior to participating in any activity."

    You can learn more about us here and here!

  • Check us out on instagram! @circuscatsuky
summary: Circus Club is a community that learns and shares knowledge about the circus and circus arts, often through first hand experience and practice of circus skills such as juggling, acro-balancing, unicycling, and aerial arts.

description: We are a social fraternity dedicated to changing fraternity culture for the better. We are the only non-hazing AND non-pledging fraternity on campus, meaning the day you're initiated you have the same rights and responsibilities as any other member. We have innovative payment plans for dues, meaning no matter your financial situation ZBT can be your home. Our primary goals for this year include chartering to become a fully-fledged chapter, 2 social events minimum per semester, and a year-over-year growth rate of 100%. name: Healthcare Muslim Student Association
description:
The Healthcare Muslim Student Association (HMSA) is a faith-based organization, for both Muslim and non-Muslim graduate healthcare students and faculty.

The HMSA will have different activities throughout the year. Some of these activities include the following:

-Monthly talks and forums regarding health and well-being, and how it aligns with our faith.

-Social events where UK healthcare students can attend and connect with one another.

-Opportunities for students to network with one another and with faculty to potentially enhance their learning experience.

-Opportunities for HMSA students to reach out for help to accommodate any religious needs or concerns that they may have.

-Opportunities for HMSA students to work with other organizations and faculty to enhance Muslim representation at UK and within the healthcare colleges
summary: The Healthcare Muslim Student Association (HMSA) is a faith-based organization, for both Muslim and non-Muslim graduate healthcare students and faculty.

name: HR Student Employment
description:
HR Student Employment is a service designed to help current UK students find flexible, student-friendly job opportunities off-campus and through on-campus third-party vendors.

Schedule an appointment with our office at: https://SelfScheduleFori9.as.me/HRStudentEmployment
summary: HR Student Employment is a service designed to help current UK students find flexible, student-friendly job opportunities off-campus and through on-campus third-party vendors.

name: Human Resource Management Club
description:
✨ Welcome to the Human Resource Management Club at the University of Kentucky! ✨

Are you interested in people, leadership, and how organizations succeed? The HR Management Club is back and ready to help students like YOU connect, learn, and grow.

 

What We’re About:
We’re a student-led organization open to all majors who want to explore human resources, leadership, and management. Whether you’re interested in psychology, business, accounting, or simply working with people, our club provides an inclusive space to build skills and expand your network.

 

What We Offer:

Educational Workshops – Explore real-world HR topics like talent acquisition, employee relations, and performance management.
Guest Speakers – Learn directly from HR professionals and industry leaders.
Networking Opportunities – Meet like-minded students, faculty, and employers.
Professional Development – Resume reviews, mock interviews, and career prep.
Community Engagement – Service projects that make a difference on campus and beyond.

 

Why Join?

  • Build your professional network early.

  • Gain skills that make you stand out to employers.

  • Connect with peers who share your interests.

  • Have fun while preparing for your future career!

📅 Join us at our first interest meeting!

(TBD!)
summary: Welcome to the Human Resource Management Club! Our club fosters skill development, networking, and collaboration among like-minded students. Unleash your potential as we connect you with HR professionals, guiding your journey towards a successful career.

name: Coffee Cats
description:
Coffee Cats is a place to foster community around the aspects of a coffee shop. Members will engage in conversations with fellow students, have a chance to experience Lexington outside of campus, and exchange study tips. Meetings will happen twice a month at various places around Lexington, and members must attend at least one event per semester to remain an "active member". Members should fill out the Google Form to be added to our roster and GroupMe to be kept up to date with events and other news.  

 

Fill out the form here

Join the GroupMe here
summary: Coffee Cats is an organization for students at the University of Kentucky to interact with local coffee shops and eateries. Members will meet at a new place each month to enjoy community, try new food/drink items, and work on homework.

name: Rose: A Gender Diverse Student Collective
description: Rose is an organization seeking to provide safe space for Trans & GNC students to engage with their community. This is an organization by Trans/GNC people for Trans/GNC people.
summary: Rose is an organization seeking to provide safe space for Trans & GNC students to engage with their community. This is an organization by Trans/GNC people for Trans/GNC people.

name: Forestry and Natural Resources Graduate Student Association
description: The UK Department of Forestry and Natural Resources Graduate Student Association (FNR GSA) is comprised of Forest and Natural Resources graduate students with the goal of enhancing graduate student professional, social, and personal development within the forestry department. The FNR GSA meets regularly to facilitate social, philanthropic, and networking events to engage with other students, faculty, and the Lexington community. Examples of previous events are the Licking River Cleanup and volunteering at the Arboretum. The Forestry GSA also acts as the voice of the Forestry and Natural Resources graduate student community by communicating with the department chair, faculty, and other constituencies.
summary: The Forestry and Natural Resources Graduate Student Association is an organization dedicated to the needs, goals, and interests of the FNR Department graduate students. We provides opportunities and services for our students, faculty, and community.

name: Pickleball Club
description: Welcome to Pickleball Club! Pickleball is a fun mix of tennis and ping-pong that’s played on a basketball or tennis court using paddles and wiffle balls. People of all skill levels are welcome to join and equipment will be provided. For more information, feel free to contact the president Tamm Omar at (502)758-3815 or feel free to join our group me at https://groupme.com/join_group/100355476/V1grwrUM
summary: Welcome to Pickleball Club! Pickleball is a fun mix of tennis and ping-pong that’s played on a basketball or tennis court surface using paddles and wiffle balls. People of all skill levels are welcome to join and equipment will be provided :)

name: Student Orthodontics Club
description: This organization is for those interested in learning more about orthodontics. It will focus on discussion and presentations by leaders in the field of orthodontics.
summary: An organization for dental students to learn more about orthodontics beyond the required curriculum.

name: American String Teachers Association (ASTA) Student Chapter
description:
We are a student chapter of the national American String Teachers Association (ASTA) national organization. The American String Teachers Association is an organization dedicated to supporting string players and teachers of all levels across the country. If you teach a private studio, want to teach orchestra, be conductor, or develop a performance career, then ASTA has resources to help. ASTA national membership provides networking, professional development, group insurance rates, access to string  research journals, and curriculum in the field of string-instrument education.

 

The purpose of our chapter is to organize social networking and professional development opportunity for students interested in music performance and education.
summary: An organization for students interested in string-instrument education and performance.

name: UK Kendo club
summary:
The goal of this club is to teach and improve Kendo. Anyone is welcome to join, no experience necessary. Practice time, TBD.  Please contact us through our Groupme or Email 
https://groupme.com/join_group/95698921/db2kRpXJ
cglo227@uky.edu

name: Office of Undergraduate Research
description: The Office of Undergraduate Research (OUR) is dedicated to providing coordination, leadership, and support for University of Kentucky undergraduates interested in pursuing mentored research and creative work across all majors and class levels.
summary: The Office of Undergraduate Research (OUR) is dedicated to providing coordination, leadership, and support for University of Kentucky undergraduates interested in pursuing mentored research and creative work across all majors and class levels.

name: American Civil Liberties Union at the University of Kentucky
summary: This organization is designed to discuss, better understand, and disseminate information about civil liberties and civil rights and when possible assist in the protection of civil rights and civil liberties in accordance with the ACLU and ACLU-KY.

name: Christian Faith in Medicine
description: The Christian Faith in Medicine group will serve as a place for students to explore their faith perspective while learning how Christians aim to integrate their faith into the workplace as healthcare providers. We hope to bring in local physicians for networking, join with other organizations in community service projects, and to provide opportunities for fellowship among students at the UK-NKU COM campus.
summary: The Christian Faith in Medicine group will serve as a place for students to explore their faith perspective while learning how Christians aim to integrate their faith into the workplace as healthcare providers.

name: Asian Women’s Alliance
description: Asian Women’s Alliance’s purpose is to unite and strengthen the Asian/Asian American women community on campus, while fostering a bond between all students, faculty, and the community at the University of Kentucky. It is our mission to create a sisterhood where all demographics are welcome to express themselves socially, academically, and professionally. Lastly, the organization strives to uphold the pillars of scholarship, service, FUN, and family while creating a sense of belonging among the University of Kentucky’s community.
summary: Asian Women’s Alliance is here to foster a community and sense of belonging among Asian and Asian American women on campus. Of course it is not limited to just Asian women, anyone is welcome to join!

name: Women in Pre-PA Club
description: The purpose of this club is to better prepare women for applying to a Physician Assistant Graduate program; give back to the community; foster an environment for women to support one another and create new friendships; empower women in their education, leadership, and personal growth; and promote an environment where women in a specified field can come together to learn about their profession under the specific scope of women.
summary: Women in Pre-PA Club: Preparing and guiding undergraduate women who intend to attend a Physician Assistant program.

name: Sustainability Future Leaders
description:
Join our club's GroupMe! 

Click here to join: https://groupme.com/join_group/71499633/OsJ6nMfs 

 

Sustainability Future Leaders is an organization centered around a community service project that collects unwanted plastic bags and turns them into sleeping mats for homeless people. We also hold monthly events centered around sustainability.
summary: Sustainability Future Leaders is an organization centered around a community service project that collects unwanted plastic bags and turns them into sleeping mats for the unhoused. We also hold monthly events centered around sustainability.

name: Cover to Cover
description:
This is a group of students that want an outlet to relax and share our love of all things literary! We choose a book to read each month and have fun talking about it at our monthly meetings. We also host book related events so be on the lookout for those! 

A schedule will be posted on our Instagram (linked below).  

Follow our Instagram @covertocover_uky

Be sure to join GroupMe!

summary: This is a group of students that want to share our love of all things literary! We choose a book to read each month and have fun talking about it at our monthly meetings. We also host book related events so be on the lookout for those!

name: Marginalized Genders in Gaming
description:
Welcome to the Marginalized Genders in Gaming club at the University of Kentucky! We host social gaming events on campus and online :)

Our Mission: To create a safe space for all individuals that identify as marginalized genders to play video games and connect with each other in gaming spaces.

 

We are very excited to work with others as we build this amazing community through gaming.

 

To learn more about our events and meet our members join us on Discord - https://discord.gg/4cZPXYs96c!
summary:
Welcome to the Marginalized Genders in Gaming club at the University of Kentucky!

To learn more about our events join us on Discord!

 https://discord.gg/4cZPXYs96c!

name: Statistics Graduate Student Association
summary: The goal is to encourage an active social environment among statistics graduate students while supporting their academic and professional journey. We provide opportunities for professional development as well as comradery building events.

name: Roselle Hall
description: Opened in 2005, Roselle Hall was named for David P. Roselle, the ninth president of the University of Kentucky.
summary: Opened in 2005, Roselle Hall was named for David P. Roselle, the ninth president of the University of Kentucky.

name: Undergraduate Science Journal Club
summary: USJC enables undergraduates to diversify their knowledge of scholarly research across several disciplines and provides them opportunities to refine their skills as presenters and researchers through presentation of academic work.

name: Disability Resource Center
description: • University of Kentucky Disability Resource Center partners with qualified students with disabilities to assist them in gaining equal access to institutional programs and services consistent with their unique needs. The Center seeks to responsibly advocate the needs of students with disabilities to the campus community through consultation and outreach efforts with administration, faculty, students and University partners.
summary: The University of Kentucky Disability Resource Center partners with qualified students with disabilities to assist them in gaining equal access to institutional programs and services consistent with their unique needs.

name: Exercise in Medicine UKCOM
description:
Exercise in Medicine is a medical student-led interest group that aims to enhance physical health and well being not only for those within the College of Medicine but beyond for our current and future patients in Kentucky.

Our group was inspired by a nonprofit called Medals4Mettle that gathers volunteers to run distance races and donate their finisher’s medals to children who have demonstrated resilience and mettle despite illness/injury. Our medical students created our own version by running the Run the BlueGrass- Lexington at Keeneland! We chose to gift our medals to children battling orthopedic injuries at Shriners Hospital for Children in Lexington. Our goal through Exercise in Medicine is to host this event annually to give back to applaud and uplift patients and their families, give back to Shriners Hospital, and promote physical health within the College of Medicine to mitigate burnout.
summary: Exercise in Medicine is a medical student-led interest group that aims to enhance physical health and well being not only for those within the College of Medicine but beyond for our current and future patients in Kentucky.

name: Phi Alpha Honor Society
description: We’re a community of passionate social work students who believe in leadership, academic excellence, and making a real impact. Through service, scholarship, and connection, Xi Sigma brings Wildcats together to uplift others and grow as future change-makers in the field of social work!
summary: The purpose of Phi Alpha Honor Society is to provide a closer bond among students of social work and promote humanitarian goals and ideas.

name: Community Scholar Program
description: The Community Scholar Program (CSP) at the University of Kentucky is a cohort-based internship program built around the dual pillars of community service and leadership development. The program matches Federal Work-Study-eligible students with paid internship opportunities at community organizations, schools, or government agencies for an academic year.  These internship opportunities are combined with EXP-based academic credit, civic engagement and professional development workshops and leadership training to create a well-rounded service-learning and professional experience. With the development of student leadership at its core, the program aims to provide sustained partnerships with community organizations, meet community needs, educate students on social justice and their community, and develop future community leaders.
summary: The Community Scholar Program (CSP) at the University of Kentucky is a cohort-based internship program built around the dual pillars of community service and leadership development.

name: College of Design
description: The UK College of Design is home to the School of Architecture, School of Interiors, Department of Historic Preservation and Department of Product Design here at the University of Kentucky.
summary: UK College of Design

name: Compassionate Care Reaching yoU Outreach
description: CCRU Outreach is a medical student run organization that strives to provide care and education to the underserved population in Northern Kentucky. We are based at the University of Kentucky College of Medicine - NKY campus.
summary: CCRU Outreach is a medical student run organization that strives to provide care and education to the underserved population in Northern Kentucky. We are based at the University of Kentucky College of Medicine - NKY campus.

name: Student Parent Organization
description: The Student Parent Organization (SPO) is founded and based on the structure of the Student Parent Association (SPA) at the University of Louisville. The SPO brings together and creates a supportive environment for enrolled students at the UK who are actively raising children. We credit and use content from The Ohio State University's National Student Parent Support Symposium held every May. The Adult Student Learner Allies Week (ASLAW) is also a major program held since June 2021 to provide essential supplies to student parents, especially in Greg Page and Shawneetown residential communities.
summary: The Student Parent Organization brings together student parents, i.e. currently enrolled students who are also raising children at UK. The SPO consolidates advocacy efforts and plans events that create a supportive, nurturing environment for everyone.

name: Collegiate Farm Bureau
description: To develop the leadership skills and abilities of its members, preparing them for future leadership roles in Farm Bureau, other agricultural organizations, their communities, and state. To enable members to gain knowledge of Farm Bureau and agriculture.
summary: To develop the leadership skills and abilities of its members, preparing them for future leadership roles in Farm Bureau, other agricultural organizations, their communities, and state. To enable members to gain knowledge of Farm Bureau and agriculture.

name: Boxing Club
description:
President: Hunter Williams

Vice president: Emi Arzola

Treasurer: Adam Speridakos

Event Chair: Gabe Portsmouth 

social chair: Jamie Storm

summary: Boxing Club focused around training and participating in boxing events.

name: UKFCU Esports Lounge
description: Step into the world of gaming and be part of a thriving community at the University of Kentucky Esports Lounge! We offer a space where you can play your favorite video games, participate in high-stakes Esports tournaments, and connect with other players and gaming enthusiasts throughout the region.
summary: The UK Esports Lounge is a gaming lounge right by the Student Center in the Cornerstone Exchange. We have 50+ gaming PC’s with a large theater for many different types of events. Come visit us!

name: Taylor Swift Fan Club
description:
You're invited to my new group 'Taylor Swift Fan Club' on GroupMe. Click here to join:

https://groupme.com/join_group/89790453/bTvGrdKu
summary: This is a club for all the UK Swifties! Come listen to her music, do crafts, watch her documentaries and music videos, and obsess about everything she does.

name: HappyCats
summary: HappyCats is a student-run mental health group with the goal of promoting positive mental health on campus. With small activities to boost morale, HappyCats works to bring students together and make our college a happier place.

name: Automotive Industry Club
description: The Automotive Industry Club is dedicated to empowering students with knowledge and opportunities in the automotive, mobility, and motorsport sectors. We host accomplished industry professionals as guest speakers, organize recruitment events, and arrange visits to automotive facilities. Our primary objective is to ensure that our members are well-prepared for successful careers in the automotive industry and can turn their career aspirations into reality.
summary: Connecting University of Kentucky students to opportunities in the automotive industry.

name: Student Public Interest Law Foundation
description: SPILF is a student organization at the University of Kentucky College of Law dedicated to public interest work in areas of government, criminal, immigration, human rights, consumer protection, child advocacy and many more. Our goals are to promote awareness of public interest law, raise funds for summer public interest clerkships for UK Law students, and serve the local Lexington, KY community. Each year we sponsor various fundraisers to raise money for the summer grant for UK Law students, host a speaker series of attorneys in various public interest fields at UK Law, sponsor public service projects, and organize other social events for UK Law students to promote public interest.
summary: SPILF is a student organization at the University of Kentucky College of Law dedicated to public interest work in areas of government, criminal, immigration, human rights, consumer protection, child advocacy and many more.

name: Caduceus Committee
summary: This committee is dedicated to planning, hosting, and ensuring the continuation of the Caduceus Ball. A long-standing tradition, the Caduceus Ball is a formal event held annually that provides medical students the opportunity to celebrate one another.

name: Bonfire International
description:
Bonfire International is a student-led and faith-based organization that serves students from every nation by providing a caring community where they are welcomed and loved. We strive to become their extended family, fostering a loving community where they can experience their college life in a safe and healthy environment. We emphasize on four Ss - Spiritual, Social, Service, and Student Leadership. Spiritual - we offer an opportunity to explore spiritual life through reading the Bible together and provide a safe place where they can share their viewpoints. Social - we offer social and cultural events where they can experience and celebrate cultural diversity and build networks. Service - we offer an opportunity to serve one another and local communities through various volunteer work. Student Leadership - we offer an opportunity for students to develop leadership skills through leading various events and others.

This student group is a student chapter recognized by Bridges International (also known as Bridges) and International Students Inc. (hereafter ISI).

 

It is our purpose to develop a caring community of students who are transformed by Jesus Christ. We seek to welcome students from all nations, invite them to consider Christ. For those who choose to follow Christ, we help them to grow in faith, encourage them to passionately live life in a manner consistent with belief in God of the Bible, and inspire commitment to advancing the purposes of God in the world.
summary: Bonfire International is a student-led and faith-based organization that serves students from every nation by providing a caring community where they can explore spiritual life, serve local communities, enjoy cultural diversity, and lead other students.

name: University Advising
description: The UK Advising Community empowers students through individualized support, guidance, and connection to resources to realize and achieve academic, career, and personal goals.
summary: The UK Advising Community empowers students through individualized support, guidance, and connection to resources to realize and achieve academic, career, and personal goals.

name: John Locke Society
description: The John Locke Society is dedicated to the exploration of political philosophy. At the heart of our mission lie the principles of liberty, responsibility, and the common good. We facilitate lively debates and provide a welcoming space for students to engage in thoughtful discussions. Join us as we explore the ideas that shape our world and foster a culture of mutual respect and intellectual curiosity.
summary: The mission of the John Locke Society is to serve the University of Kentucky community by providing a forum for the friendly and robust exchange of ideas about liberty, responsibility, and the public good.

name: Financial Ombud Services
summary: Department page for Financial Ombud Services.

name: Family Medicine Interest Group - NKY
description: The Family Medicine Interest Group-NKY will be an opportunity for UKCOM-NKY medical students to network with fellow students and local leaders in primary care, participate in career development activities centered around Family Medicine and Primary Care, and develop skills that will be important for residency and future attending practice.
summary: FMIG-NKY allows UKCOM-NKY medical students to network with fellow students and local leaders in primary care, participate in career development activities, and develop skills for residency and attending practice.

name: BOOMCats
description:
Join the BOOMCats! A musical organization where members can imitate, explore, and create music. You will make new friendships and memories, as well as skills that can benefit your future. You will also be given the opportunities to work as a group to share you ideas and see what you can do with the world of music. No experience is necessary! Come see what the BOOMCats can do for you!

Our Mission Goals:

- Provide music to members interested of expanding their musicality skills and have the opportunity to play Boomwhackers.

- Educate the University of Kentucky campus community on how to properly handle certain instruments.

- Provide and facilitate opportunities to imitate, explore, and create when it comes to learning music.

- Provide opportunities to translate leadership skills, communication, and musicality attained in the organization to real-world environments.

- Connect with other members, make new friends, and gain valuable experience that benefits the student, with the organization at the University of Kentucky.
summary: A musical organization for members to develop imitate, explore, and create music when playing Boomwhackers®. Members will also make new friends, memories, and develop skills than can translate into the real-world environment. No experience is necessary!

name: University Christian Outreach
description:
University Christian Outreach is a strategic response to the Great Commission of Jesus Christ: changing campuses to change the world.

We build life-giving campus communities to reach university students and call them to respond to the Gospel by putting their faith in Jesus and receiving new life in the Holy Spirit.

We form students into mature, lifelong, missionary disciples and send them into the world to build Christian communities and call all nations to rise and live.
summary: University Christian Outreach builds life-giving campus communities with the aim of developing students into lifelong disciples of Jesus Christ.

name: Casino Night Committee
description: Casino night is a fundraiser for SAC (the free clinic run by UKCOM students), and all proceeds from this event benefit SAC. Professors are blackjack or poker dealers, and the goal is to fundraise as much as possible. Various local companies will be contacted to donate silent auction and raffle items.
summary: Casino night is a fundraiser for the Salvation Army Clinic (the free clinic run by UKCOM students)

name: First Generation Professionals
summary: First Generation Professionals is an organization at the J. David Rosenberg College of Law that provides resources and mentoring for first generation law students.

name: Queer POC Association
description: This organization is designed provide community, support, representation and advocacy for students that are BIPOC and are a member of the LGBTQ community, this organization is for all queer BIPOC minorities on the UKY campus. We have events meant to educate about the history of the queer community with a focus on the POC experience and we have events that are just to celebrate our intersectional identities.
summary: This organization is designed provide community, support, representation and advocacy for students that are BIPOC and are a member of the LGBTQ community, this organization is for all queer BIPOC minorities on the UKY campus.

name: Immigration Law Society
description: The Immigration Law Society at the University of Kentucky J. David Rosenberg College of Law is dedicated to educating and engaging law students and the broader legal community on critical issues within the American immigration system. We strive to foster a welcoming and diverse environment for all members and participants in our events, promoting a deeper understanding of immigration law and its impact on society.
summary: The Immigration Law Society is an organization within the UK Rosenberg College of Law that aims to educate and inform law students as well as the larger legal community about the issues within the American immigration system.

name: Futsal Club
description:
Futsal Club has a fun, welcoming environment for all people whether they are looking for a unique, new sport to try or if they are trying to improve their foot skills for futbol. 

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/89704234/FSoJvcha
summary:
Futsal Club has a fun, welcoming environment for all people whether they are looking for a unique, new sport to try or if they are trying to improve their foot skills for futbol.

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/89704234/FSoJvcha

name: Innovation Club
description:
To improve the social and professional acumen of its members through real-life application of business, STEM, and the social sciences.

Promote the welfare of these professions.

To provide fellowship between different majors
summary:
To improve the social and professional acumen of its members through real-life application of STEM, and the social sciences.

Promote the welfare of these professions.

name: UK Invests
description:
UK Invests is a new, first-of-its-kind initiative nationally that will help students build a foundation of financial literacy, allowing them to achieve greater financial security, career readiness and well-being.

Through this initiative, all UK students (undergraduate, graduate and professional students) will have the opportunity to deepen their understanding of financial wellness, practice financial investing and receive monetary rewards for institution-identified healthy behaviors.

UK Invests exists in collaboration with third parties, such as Fidelity Investments and its Fidelity Bloom app, ensuring our students will reap a lifetime of benefits for themselves, their families and their communities.

Fidelity Investments is the official investment solution provider for UK Invests.
summary: UK Invests is a new, first-of-its-kind initiative nationally that will help students build a foundation of financial literacy, allowing them to achieve greater financial security, career readiness and well-being.

name: Student’s Association of the Middle East and North Africa
description: The Student's Association of the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) aims to build a sense of community among students and showcase the richness of the MENA region through engaging events and social activities. Join us on this journey of cultural exploration and connection! Whether you're a seasoned enthusiast or just starting to discover the beauty of the MENA region, there's a place for you at MENA UKY.
summary: The Student’s Association of the Middle East and North Africa aims to build a community among students and showcase the richness of the MENA region through engaging events and social activities. People of all ethnicities and backgrounds are welcome!

name: UK Connecting and Networking for Suicide Prevention
summary: UK:CAN is led by Dr. Julie Cerel and Dr. Corrine Williams under a SAMHSA-funded campus suicide prevention grant. Our goal is to create specific trainings to help students at UK: Supporting Students Through Crisis Training.

name: Nigerian Graduate Students Association
description:
The Nigerian Graduate Students Association is a student-led organization at the University of Kentucky that is dedicated to serving the needs of Nigerian graduate students. The association strives to foster a sense of community and inclusivity among its members, as well as to promote academic and professional excellence. Through various programs and initiatives, the association works to empower its members and to support their personal and academic growth.

The NGSA was registered newly as a Registered Student Organisation (RSO) with the University of Kentucky on the 15th April, 2023
summary: The Nigerian Graduate Students Association is an association created “to holistically represent, unite, and cater to the needs of enrolled Nigerian graduate students at the University of Kentucky.”

name: Hiking Club
description: Welcome to the Hiking Club! We go on hiking trips, overnight camping trips, and service projects. We try to do at least 2 activities a month, so feel free to join anytime! We have been to Red River Gorge, Raven Run, Big South Fork, and have plans to go even further. To stay updated, make sure to join the GroupMe, and follow our Instagram.
summary: The Hiking Club is a place for all those who love hiking, camping, backpacking, and all things outdoors. Come hike with us!

name: Pre-Nursing Club
description: The Pre-Nursing Club is an organization that brings together students that are working towards Nursing School here at the University of Kentucky. Together, we host events focused on building community and connecting students to the College of Nursing, and other resources on campus.
summary: The Pre-Nursing Club at UK is an organization that gives students the opportunity to connect and grow alongside of other likeminded students.

name: Phi Delta Epsilon Medical Fraternity Graduate
description:
We develop physicians who positively impact the world, inspire others to greatness, and lead by example. Our guiding values can be seen below.

  • Philanthropy: Phi Delta Epsilon believes in giving back to our global community. Our international philanthropic and service partner is Children’s Miracle Network Hospitals (CMNH).
  • Deity: This is a reminder that there is a greater power in healing.
  • Education & Equity: Our chapters represent the diversity in medicine. We treat all members and patients kindly, nonjudgmentally and respectfully. We never stop learning and are students for life.
summary: Phi Delta Epsilon is an organization for medical school students and physicians. Phi Delta Epsilon Medical Fraternity creates physicians of integrity with a lifelong commitment to our guiding principles: philanthropy, deity, equity, and education.

name: American Chemical Society-Graduate Student Organization
description: This is a graduate student organization that will be affiliated with the American Chemical Society. The goals of the organization are outreach, networking, career preparation, professional development, and diversity. This organization is for any graduate student interested in chemistry.
summary: A graduate student organization that will be affiliated with the American Chemical Society. The organization will provide a community for graduate students interested in chemistry, career and professional development, diversity, networking, and outreach

name: Mycology Club
description: Mycology Club aims to be a space of learning and fun for fungal enthusiasts both on campus and in the Lexington community. We plan to inoculate logs with spores, forage in the field, hear from guest lecturers and more!
summary: Mycology Club aims to be a space of learning and fun for fungal enthusiasts both on campus and in the Lexington community. We plan to inoculate logs with spores, forage in the field, hear from guest lecturers and more.

name: Dermatology Interest Group - Northern Kentucky
summary: The Dermatology Interest Group - Northern Kentucky is an organization that provides UKCOM-NKY students the opportunity to explore the field of dermatology through monthly meetings, community outreach programs, networking opportunities, and much more!

name: Appalachian Career Training in Oncology
description: The Markey Cancer Center’s Appalachian Career Training in Oncology (ACTION) Student Organization offers students, faculty, and staff at the University of Kentucky the opportunity to learn about the cancer disparities in Appalachian Kentucky, cancer research opportunities, and participate in educational experiences that will enrich their interest in pursuing a biomedical career, with a focus on cancer-related careers. Students can engage in outreach activities, trainings, and fundraising opportunities.
summary: The Markey Cancer Center’s Appalachian Career Training in Oncology (ACTION) Student Organization offers students, faculty, and staff at the University of Kentucky the opportunity to learn about the cancer disparities in Appalachian Kentucky.

name: UK Healthcare
summary: BBNvolved page for UK Healthcare.

name: Gatton Global Leaders
description:
• Gatton Global Leaders, What We Do

    We are a student organization at UK’s Gatton College focused on global business, international engagement, and building a cross-cultural community on campus. Open to all years and all majors.

    Who can join our events:
    • Students interested in international business or study abroad
    • Domestic & international students looking for global perspectives
    • Prospective international students considering studying business at Gatton

    What our events are about:
    • Hear from guest speakers from multinational companies and global organizations
    • Learn how professionals have studied, interned, and worked abroad
    • Join us on a site visit to Kentucky-based companies and get a behind-the-scenes look at manufacturing in action.
    • Build friendships and expand your global network

    Bonus perks this semester:
    �� Free food at all events
    �� Students who attend our January & February events will be eligible for a site visit to Bullard
    �� Students who attend all 3 events (Jan, Feb, March) will be eligible for a site visit to Alltech | transportation included
    �� Be recognized as Student of the Month for outstanding engagement and win a stainless steel water bottle with our logo

    Join the community:
    All years and majors welcome join our GroupMe to become a member and come to our events on February 3, March 3 and March 31, from 7-8 PM at Gatton College of Business and Eco, Classroom 191. 
    Become a member! Your only role is to show up and have fun https://groupme.com/join_group/102821676/HnlhyXuv
summary: Expand your business horizons through international knowledge, focusing on Latin America.

name: The Helping Paw
description:
The Helping Paw is an organization that brings students together for group volunteering events at local shelters and humane societies. The goal of the organization is to bring together animal lovers from all walks of life at the University of Kentucky and create opportunities for them to develop leadership skills while making a positive impact in their community. During events students could be walking dogs, socializing with cats, or assisting with adoption events. If you’re interested in joining keep an eye on our instagram for meeting announcements and please join our group me for where we send out information on our events and meeting!

 https://groupme.com/join_group/96007503/MFLE6hFQ
summary: The Helping Paws mission is to provide University of Kentucky students opportunities to develop their leadership skill and bring support to local shelters and humane societies through group volunteering events. We are open to any and all animal lovers!

name: Class of 2026 at the College of Dentistry
summary: This org makes up the students of the class of 2026 at University of Kentucky College of Dentistry

name: Neonatology Interest Group
description: The Neonatology Interest Group provides medical students with the opportunity to learn more about the field of neonatology and what it is like to work in a NICU. We host monthly meetings on campus with fellows and attendings as well as workshops. Our meetings give medical students the opportunity to gain a new perspective on the field and get hands on experience.
summary: The Neonatology Interest Group provides medical students with the opportunity to learn more about the field of neonatology and what it is like to work in a NICU. We host monthly meetings and workshops on campus with fellows and attendings.

name: College of Dentistry
summary: The BBNvolved page for the College of Dentistry at the University of Kentucky.

name: HealthCare Cats
description:
Healthcare Cats is all encompassing healthcare organization here at UK. We hold a special focus upon rural health and the need to combat rural health disparities in the Commonwealth.

Guest Speakers
Group Network Events
Forums from Health Colleges
Presentations to High Schools
Mentoring Program
Community Service

This organization can greatly benefit you as you head into any of the healthcare fields in the future!
 
Join the GroupMe!
