summary: The mission of HealthCare Cats is to "Provide an avenue into college and healthcare opportunities for rural students of Kentucky in order to create a better Commonwealth." We present to rural high schools in Kentucky on healthcare careers and college.

name: Martin-Gatton College of Agriculture, Food and Environment Center for Student Success
shortName: M-G CAFE Center for Student Success
description: The Martin-Gatton College of Agriculture, Food & Environment Center for Student Success is dedicated to serving students by contributing to their academic success, health & wellness, and career goals with special programming, advising, and safe spaces.
summary: The BBNvolved page for the Martin-Gatton College of Agriculture, Food and Environment Center for Student Success.

name: Industrial Designers Society of America
description:
The Industrial Designers Society of America (IDSA) is a student organization established under the national IDSA.  Members will be able to attend interactive events, workshops, guest speakers, field trips, and collaborations with other IDSA student chapters. These activities are aimed at promoting the practice of Industrial Design by incorporating education, information, community, and advocacy.   

Here is an excerpt from https://www.idsa.org/communities/student-chapters/ further explaining the opportunities IDSA Student Chapters provide to students. To learn more about IDSA go to https://www.idsa.org/.  

IDSA Student Chapters provide rich academic and professional growth environments for students to organize around their career aspiration at colleges and universities across the United States. These localized, school-based communities create an invaluable support network for those who participate. Official IDSA Student Chapters enjoy the benefits of using IDSA’s globally recognized identity and receive operational support resources from IDSA headquarters in the fulfillment of their activities. Additionally, they are able to participate in the Society’s annual Student Merit Award (SMA) competition and other programs offered by IDSA that are built to enhance the college experience and assist in the transition to the professional workforce. 

IDSA Student Members, through their involvement with Student Chapters, have the opportunity to learn new skillsets, gain visibility, and connect to a larger network of other similarly motivated individuals. The legitimacy that the IDSA Student Chapter status provides allows the participants to effectively establish solid relationships with professional designers in their area and gives the group instant credibility in the community.
summary: The Industrial Designers Society of America is for students interested in the world of Industrial Design. Members will be able to attend interactive events, workshops, guest speakers, field trips, and collaborations with other IDSA student chapters.

name: College of Medicine
description: We provide holistic and inclusive support services that enrich your experience at the UK College of Medicine and help ensure your academic success. The UK College of Medicine offers a variety of student organizations, clubs, and specialty interest groups for medical students to engage with peers, hone their professional skills, and explore specific interests within medicine during their educational experience. Involvement in these groups can help medical students develop into more well-rounded physicians.
summary: We provide holistic and inclusive support services that enrich your experience at the UK College of Medicine and help ensure your academic success.

name: Center for the Enhancement of Learning and Teaching
description: The Center for the Enhancement of Learning and Teaching works collaboratively with instructors, staff, and administrators at the University of Kentucky to support engaging, inclusive, and innovative learning environments in which all students and instructors have the opportunity to succeed. We collaborate with faculty, instructors, graduate student teaching assistants & instructors to actualize new possibilities for teaching and learning.
summary: BBNvolved page for the Center for the Enhancement of Learning and Teaching.

name: Northern Kentucky Anesthesia Interest Group
description: This group is designed for students who have an interest in anesthesia, whether that be an interest in the specialty or an interest in the practice (perhaps for those who may take care of critical care patients who have undergone anesthesia, those interested in pain management, intubated patients, or for those who may be interested in learning about intubating patients). This group will also emphasize practicing medicine/anesthesia in the northern Kentucky area.
summary: for students who have an interest in anesthesia, whether that be an interest in the specialty or an interest in the practice (those who may take care of critical care patients who have undergone anesthesia, are interested in pain management, etc.) in NKY.

name: Office of Student Conduct
summary: BBNvolved page for the Office of Student Conduct.

name: Christian Veterinary Fellowship
description: Christian Veterinary Fellowship (CVF), an extension of the organization Christian Veterinary Mission, aims to provide unique ministry opportunities to pre-veterinary and veterinary students along with those in veterinary medicine. The CVF chapter at UKY will strive to provide a space for those with a passion for ministry and service along with veterinary medicine to connect through fellowship and diving deeper into ministry together.
summary: Christian Veterinary Fellowship exists as a way to form Christ centered community within veterinary medicine. As a club we welcome anyone who is interested in veterinary medicine. We exist under Christian Veterinary Mission.

name: Environmental Law Society
description: The Environmental Law Society is an organization directed towards University of Kentucky Rosenberg College of Law students that have an interest in energy and natural resource law. Environmental law is a broad practice area that deals mainly with administrative law and regulatory compliance, though it also frequently brings in aspects of contracts, property, and bankruptcy law. What is unique about environmental law is its intersection with economics and politics. This organization is for individual interested in these topics who wishes to learn more and find fellowship within the organization.
summary: The Environmental Law Society is an organization directed towards University of Kentucky Rosenberg College of Law students that have an interest in energy and natural resource law.

name: Folklore and Mythology Club
description: A welcoming and inclusive community of University of Kentucky students who share a mutual interest in folklore and mythology!
summary: A welcoming and inclusive community of University of Kentucky students who share a mutual interest in folklore and mythology!

name: Christian Dental Association
summary: The Christian Dental Association educates, encourages, and equips Christian dental professionals to glorify God in all that we do.  CDA is for UKCD faculty, staff, and current/prospective dental students.

name: International Friends
description: We gather twice a semester to connect with the international community here at UK!
summary: We gather twice a semester to connect with the international community here at UK!

name: Kentucky Undergraduate Journal for the Health Humanities
description:
We are a student-led Honors journal that publishes faculty-reviewed research, scholarship, and art of undergraduate students enrolled at any institution on UK's repository UKnowledge. The Health Humanities is an interdisciplinary study that analyzes how humanistic disciplines (arts, literature, law, history, philosophy/ethics, etc.) inform or intersect with health care and health-related subjects. Students may bring knowledge or skills from any discipline and connect it to the Health Humanities. This approach seeks to promote conversations that will lead to greater respect for disciplines outside of one’s own and to create policies for the betterment of human health and wellbeing.

 

There are a manifold of benefits and barriers associated with publishing work as an undergraduate student and we hope to make that an easier feat for Kentucky's college students. 

 

Both research and art-based submissions are welcome! Follow our Instagram or click the website icon below for more information.
summary: KUJHH will publish expert-reviewed research articles and art of students enrolled at a Kentucky institution. The scope of the journal is interdisciplinary and allows submissions from any discipline that shows an intersection with human health care ethics

name: Physicians for Human Rights
description: This club is a chapter of the PHR National Student Program which engages medical students and other health professionals from across the world dedicated to the promotion and advancement of human rights. The program provides students with the resources, training, and networks to develop the skills to advocate for human rights. At UK, our primary intention is organizing an asylum clinic to conduct forensic medical or mental health evaluations to uncover signs and sequelae of torture or trauma in patients seeking asylum in our city.
summary: Physicians for Human Rights is an organization for medical students who are interested in advocating for health and human rights at the local, national, and global level.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi
description: The group is intended to provide students with the opportunity to learn about careers in the legal field, information regarding the law school application process, help with LSAT preparation, and connecting students with numerous connections with admissions advisors, professors, attorneys, judges, and other students with similar interests and career paths.
summary: Learn about the vast array of careers in the legal field and gain valuable insights into the law school application process. Helps with LSAT prep, applications, and connections to further career opportunities!

name: Religious and Spiritual Life
description: UK Religious and Spiritual Life provides support for students in finding or creating a community consistent with their faith tradition, worldview, or spirituality.  We provide opportunities for students to explore and enrich their own identities, while connecting with individuals from different worldviews and traditions to enhance their knowledge and understanding.
summary: BBNvolved page for Religious and Spiritual Life.

name: Integrative Medicine and Health
summary: BBNvolved page for Integrative Medicine and Health.

name: Pre-Medical Women's Society
description: The Pre-Medical Women's Society is a student organization at the University of Kentucky, established by pre-medical women to support and empower others on their journey to a career in medicine. Our mission is to equip future women in medicine with the knowledge, confidence, and skills necessary for success. Through guest speakers, educational workshops, and service opportunities, this organization fosters a supportive community and network of women who can collaborate, inspire, and uplift one another throughout their pre-professional paths.
summary: Pre-Medical Women's Society is a student organization at the University of Kentucky open to all pre-medical women! We are committed to producing more prepared, confident women in medicine through guest speakers, workshops, and service events.

name: Kentucky Endodontic Study Club
description: This club is meant to introduce first- and second-year dental students to the field of endodontics and prepare them to treat patients. The club is also meant to improve the knowledge and skills of third- and fourth-year dental students and prepare them for applying to endodontic residencies.
summary: To provide an informative, fun and relaxed setting for UKCD dental students to grow in their knowledge and skills pertaining to the field of endodontics.

name: MD+NBA Program
summary: We are a medical school organization, interested in meeting up to play pickup basketball, and help medical students get more physical activity!

name: Class of 2027 at the College of Dentistry
summary: College of dentistry Class of 2027

name: Spanish in Medicine Club
description: The purpose of this club is to promote the knowledge and use of Spanish in healthcare. We will learn about Spanish in healthcare in meetings and coordinate service opportunities to help our local community.
summary: The purpose of this club is to promote the knowledge and use of Spanish in healthcare. We will learn about Spanish in healthcare in meetings and coordinate service opportunities to help our local community.

name: Radiology Interest Group-NKY
description: RIG is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering an appreciation and understanding of the field of radiology within the medical community. It provides a platform for medical students to explore various aspects of radiology, including diagnostic imaging techniques, interventional radiology, and radiation oncology. Members typically engage in activities such as lectures, workshops, case discussions, and hands-on experiences to develop their knowledge and skills in this specialized medical field. Additionally, the group often collaborates with radiologists, organizes guest speaker events, and offers networking opportunities to help students pursue careers in radiology or related disciplines. Overall, it serves as a valuable resource for aspiring physicians interested in the crucial role of medical imaging in patient diagnosis and treatment.
summary: RIG is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering an appreciation and understanding of the field of radiology within the medical community.

name: Information Technology Services
description:
Information Technology Services (ITS) provides IT resources utilized by University of Kentucky (UK) students, faculty, staff, visitors, members of the University community, citizens of the Commonwealth, and beyond.

Our nearly 380 staff members create and maintain vital systems such as student systems and payroll; provide enterprise-wide network, wireless, and telephone connectivity; computational resources used by numerous researchers (e.g. high-performance computing); staff and maintain student computing labs; and provide direct IT customer support. Through engagement with the University community, ITS is actively responding to the evolving technical needs and challenges of the institution.
summary: Information Technology Services (ITS) provides IT resources utilized by University of Kentucky (UK) students, faculty, staff, visitors, members of the University community, citizens of the Commonwealth, and beyond.

name: End Overdose
description: End Overdose is a 501c3 nonprofit organization headquartered in Los Angeles, California, with a mission to combat drug-related overdose deaths through a multifaceted approach that includes education, medical intervention, and raising public awareness. Within this organization, End Overdose at UK operates as one of the college chapters spread across the United States, with a primary goal of making essential education and resources, such as fentanyl test strips and Narcan/naloxone, readily accessible not only to the University of Kentucky community but also to a broader audience beyond the campus. Through extensive education campaigns, outreach efforts, and community engagement, End Overdose at UK aims to raise public awareness about the numerous ways in which drug-related overdoses can be prevented, fostering a safer, more informed, and compassionate community dedicated to preventing drug-related overdose tragedies.
summary: End Overdose at the University of Kentucky works to end drug-related overdose deaths via education, medical intervention, and public awareness. We aim to train individuals on how to use naloxone in the event of an overdose.

name: Patterson School of Diplomacy and International Commerce
shortName: Patterson School of Diplomacy and Int Commerce
summary: BBNvolved page for the Patterson School of Diplomacy and International Commerce.

name: Asian and Pacific American Law Student Association
summary: Our mission is to provide a national voice for APA law students, foster professional and personal development, and advocate for the dignity of our communities. We work closely with legal and affinity organizations across communities to advance advocacy.

name: The Community Development Society
summary: The Community Development Society is an organization that aims to strengthen and advance community development policy, practice, learning, and research through educational means.

name: Poli Sci Pals
description: Check the gallery for the Fall 2024 tutoring schedule and click the Linktree if you are interested in applying for a position!
summary: Poli Sci Pals is a peer-led mentor and tutoring based organization to help students enrolled in tough Political Science courses better understand the material being presented to them and provide mentoring services to "Poli Sci" majors and minors!

name: Doctoral of Social Work Student Community
description:
The Doctor of Social Work Student Community (DSWSC) was developed to bring together students across the cohorts enrolled and active in the Doctor of Social Work degree at the University of Kentucky. 

The DSWSC serves as an opportunity to develop relationships and connection between DSW students as often times in online programs there is not a chance for connection beyond the classroom. 

The DSWSC meets monthly and offers a time for students to engage with one another in a less formal environment, learn from one another, and build social and professional relationships.

 In addition, the DSWSC offers opportunities for engagement in social justice and advocacy opportunities, leadership, informal mentor/mentee relationships and sharing of information and support!
summary: The Doctor of Social Work Student Community (DSWSC) at the University of Kentucky brings together students enrolled in the Doctor of Social Work program. It provides a platform for students to connect beyond the online classroom, fostering relationships.

name: LeadHER Women Executives
description:
Mission Statement for LeadHER: Shaping Tomorrow's Executive Leaders

LeadHER's Mission is to cultivate leadership, confidence, and community among women at the University of Kentucky through mentorship, development, and engagement.

LeadHER Motto

Lead with Strength, Empower with Purpose

Our Vision

At LeadHER, we value inclusivity, authenticity, leadership, and community. We are committed to providing a space where women are seen, heard, and empowered to grow personally and professionally. By embracing authenticity and collaboration, we foster a community where strong, confident leaders thrive and empower each other.

Our Values

  • LeadHER values the empowerment of women through mentorship, development, and engagement, fostering a confident and connected community of leaders.
  • LeadHER values the professional and personal growth of women, creating a dynamic network that cultivates impactful leadership.
  • Guided by "Lead with Strength, Empower with Purpose," LeadHER values the bold and authentic development of female leaders at the University of Kentucky.
  • LeadHER values mentorship, development, and community as essential pillars for empowering women to lead with confidence and purpose.

Our Goals

  • Inspire Ambition: Encourage women to lead with confidence and purpose.

  • Develop Skills: Provide essential tools and training for impactful leadership.

  • Foster Mentorship: Connect members with experienced leaders for guidance and support.

  • Advocate for Belonging: Promote leadership that values inclusion and representation.

  • Reshape Leadership: Break down barriers and redefine what leadership looks like for women.

What We Offer

  • LeadHer prepares women to lead and serve through:

    • Speaker Series: Learn from top leaders across industries.

    • Workshops: Build and refine leadership skills.

    • Mentorship: Receive guidance from seasoned professionals.

    • Networking: Form lasting connections with fellow women leaders.

    • Community Projects: Lead initiatives that make a tangible impact in Lexington.

    • Supportive Community: Join a network of like-minded women dedicated to leadership and service.

LeadHER equips you to lead with purpose and make a lasting difference.

Lead with Strength, Empower with Purpose
summary: At LeadHer, our mission is to empower and equip women at the University of Kentucky for the vital role of executive leaders in the future.

name: Collaborative Art Group Engagement
summary: Art Education Student Chapter – Coll.a.g.e (Collaborative art group engagement)provides leadership, volunteer opportunities, professional career development and educational opportunities for art education students at the University of Kentucky.

name: Hero's Club
description: Hero's Club will focus on mental health in healthcare workers across several fields like first responders, nurses, doctors, etc. We do this by raising awareness through service and philanthropy events, having first-hand stories and experiences from healthcare workers, and by doing interactive and mindful workshops to better develop and remove the stigma of mental health issues in healthcare to create a better future in mental health amongst healthcare workers.
summary: Hero's Club is an organization focused on mental health in healthcare workers across several disciplines, including first responders, physicians, nurses, etc. It will focus on raising awareness and building skills to build a better future for healthcare.

name: Medical Behavioral Science
summary: Inaugural medical behavioral science organization at the University of Kentucky. We work to develop an more interdisciplinary approach to health and healthcare while understanding why people engage in certain health behaviors.

name: It's On Us
description:
It’s On Us at UK is building and leading a movement dedicated to ending sexual assault and interpersonal violence through student-centered engagement. We’re collaborating with the national It’s On Us movement to provide research-based education and resources to the University of Kentucky community. Our goal is to create a safe and inclusive space for dialogue and support for survivors of sexual and relationship violence. And we hope you will join us in this important mission to raise awareness and stop sexual assault.  

 

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/97739625/tYmqMYmq 

LinkTree: https://linktr.ee/itsonusuky
summary: It’s On Us at UK is part of a nation-wide movement to end sexual assault and interpersonal violence through student-centered engagement. Our goal is to create a safe and inclusive space to train and support the University of Kentucky community.

name: MediKids
description: MediKids is a student organization dedicated to serving the kids in Lexington, via hosting after-school workshops on health and wellness, volunteering with the Salvation Army and KY Children's Hospital, and much more!
summary: Medikids is dedicated to teaching children the basic principles of medicine! From implementing lessons on taking vitals to showing how to perform CPR, we hope to inspire kids in our community to engage and serve as kids in medicine.

name: Substance Use Disorder Support, Education, and Destigmatization
summary: We are an interprofessional student organization with the goal of combatting substance use disorder (SUD) through support of those affected, educating individuals about SUD and harm reduction, and reducing stigma surrounding SUD.

name: TED Talks Club
description:
The TED Talks Club assists with the development of the TEDxUKY event, a licensed local version of a TED Talk Event focused on the University of Kentucky community. The members of the Club can volunteer at the event and help with the organization. 

We will also have a few social events throughout the semester, so the club is a great opportunity of meeting new students also interested in TED Talks!

If you're interested, join the Groupme:https://groupme.com/join_group/109393152/F9o4UH4U
summary: We will have meetings to discuss TED Talks, either on campus or at coffee shops downtown. The club will also help with the organization of the licensed TEDxUKY, a local TED Talks event that will happen next Spring on Campus.

name: Middle Eastern Medical Student Association
summary: MEMSA is a collective medical student organization celebrating Arabic diversity and fostering avenues of community outreach between UKCOM and the Middle Eastern community in Lexington and across the Commonwealth.

name: Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery Interest Group
description: The purpose of this organization is to allow for the exploration of the specialty of Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery by the Medical Students enrolled in The University of Kentucky College of Medicine. The organization will serve to allow students exploration, educational enrichment, networking, and professional development opportunities in the field of Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery as well as its subspeciality fields.
summary: An interest group for medical students interested in careers in plastic and reconstructive surgery.

name: Bachelor's to Law Undergraduate Education (BLUE) Law Society
description: The BLUE Law Society is a student organization established to serve students enrolled in the Bachelor's to Law Undergraduate Education (BLUE) Program at the University of Kentucky as well as other students interested in the field of law. The organization's primary objectives are to promote academic scholarship and intellectual interest in disciplines such as history, English, political science, and law. The BLUE Law Society is dedicated to fostering a strong sense of community among its members, facilitating networking opportunities, and creating a supportive environment for students within the program.
summary: The BLUE Law Society is a student organization established to serve students enrolled in the Bachelor's to Law Undergraduate Education (BLUE) Program at the University of Kentucky.

name: Between The Pages
description:
Between the Pages is more than just a book club — it’s a space for both readers and creatives to come together. Whether you’re deep into your current read, doodling in your journal, crafting a new bookmark, or just looking to relax and connect, our club blends the love of stories with the joy of creating.

 

We host a mix of cozy reading sessions, bookish discussions, and hands-on creative events like DIY crafts, vision boards, and tote bag decorating — all inspired by the books we love and the energy we bring.

You don’t have to be a fast reader or an artist — just come as you are.
summary: We are a community that enjoys reading, conversing, and connecting with one another over shared interests. Love reading? Can’t come to every meeting? That’s okay, come check us out anyway!!

name: First-Generation in Medicine
description: First-Generation in Medicine is an organization which aims to provide support and guidance to first-generation medical students at the University of Kentucky College of Medicine. The goal of this organization is to create a safe space and community for first-generation medical students where they can receive guidance throughout their tenure in medical school. The organization serves as a networking tool for first-generation medical students to connect with each other and with faculty members as well, in hopes of building community and creating mentorship opportunities.
summary: First-Generation in Medicine is an organization which aims to provide support, guidance, networking, and mentorship opportunities to first-generation medical students throughout their tenure in medical school.

name: Pathology Interest Group
description: An interest group with the purpose of fostering and promoting an interest in anatomic and clinical pathology among medical students, providing educational opportunities, facilitating networking with professionals in the field, and contributing to the advancement of knowledge in pathology.
summary: Organization involved in the career advancement, of medical students interested in the field of pathology, as well as scholastic and scientific advancement of those students and provide them with more information about the specialty.

name: Inclusive Care Advocates
description:
Inclusive Care Advocates (ICA) will serve as a safe place for BIPOC students as they navigate the medical school experience by: 

  • Offering the ability to congregate and converse in a safe space 
  • Creating a Big Sib-Little Sib program for members 
  • Facilitating mentorship to BIPOC students with St. Elizabeth healthcare physicians  
  • Hosting events showcasing and advocating for BIPOC physicians and patients 
  • Bridging the gap between undergraduate and high school students through advocacy and campus involvement
summary: Inclusive Care Advocates is a student-led organization dedicated to connecting BIPOC healthcare professional students in service, patient advocacy, and fellowship in the Northern Kentucky region.

name: Movement Mentors
description: We are a volunteer organization that aims to close the gap in physical activity participation between girls and boys, while encouraging all youth to be active. Volunteers promote physical activity at local schools acting as active role models for them!
summary: We are a volunteer organization that aims to close the gap in physical activity participation between girls and boys, while encouraging all youth to be active. Volunteers promote physical activity at local schools acting as active role models for them!

name: Office of Student Financial Aid and Scholarships
summary: The BBNvolved page for the Office of Student Financial Aid and Scholarships.

name: Medical Education Interest Group
description: Interest Group providing support and community for students pursuing research and/or careers in medical education and academic medicine. The Med Ed IG seeks to connect students with one another and with UK faculty involved in Medical Education.
summary: Interest Group providing support and community for students pursuing research and/or careers in medical education and academic medicine. The Med Ed IG seeks to connect students with one another and with UK faculty involved in Medical Education.

name: Donovan Scholars Fellowship
description:
The Donovan Scholars Fellowship is committed to creating a vibrant and supportive community of mature learners at the University of Kentucky. Toward this end, DSF seeks to enhance the quality of life for current and past Donovan Scholarship recipients by providing opportunities for social connections.  It also intends to create an inclusive and welcoming environment that values the wisdom, experiences and contributions of its members while encouraging the sharing of their skills, stories and passions with the broader University community. By encouraging connections across generations of learners, DSF seeks to challenge age-related stereotypes and advocate for the interests of mature learners.
 
Interested in joining?
 
 CLICK HERE TO SUBMIT MEMBERSHIP APPLICATION
 
Helpful information
Find Services | Student Success (uky.edu)
summary: Donovan Scholars Fellowship is a community of students who are recipients of the Donovan Scholarship. This scholarship pays tuition and mandatory course fees for Kentucky residents age 65 years and older, taking academic courses at UK.

name: Alpha Delta Kappa Collegiate Club for Future Educators
description: The Alpha Delta Kappa Collegiate Club supports future educators while promoting educational excellence, professionalism, altruism, and world understanding in accordance with the University of Kentucky mission statement.
summary: The purpose of Alpha Delta Kappa Collegiate Club shall be to support future educators while promoting educational excellence, professionalism, altruism, and world understanding in accordance with the University of Kentucky mission statement.

name: Young Communist League
summary: The YCL is the Youth Wing of the Communist Party USA. We seek to educate in the history of the labor movement and the theory of class struggle, while also engaging students in current politics and class struggle in Lexington and on campus.

name: The Lucky Ones: Impact on Foster Youth
description: Our service organization strives to raise awareness about the issues of the foster care system and provide tangible support and resources for foster youth throughout the state. The way we do this is by volunteering with, raising funds for, and donating supplies to local foster care organizations.
summary: Our non-profit organization strives to raise awareness about the issues of the foster care system and provide tangible support and resources for foster youth throughout the state.

name: Caribbean Student Association
summary:
The Caribbean Student Association aims to enhance understanding and offer a gateway to experience the Caribbean, encompassing its culture, economy, and history, both within the university and the Lexington
community.

name: Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: MAE-GSA is an student association for all graduate students at the Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering department at University of Kentucky.

name: Transfer Year Experience
description: The Transfer Year Experience (TYE) provides incoming transfer students a community as soon as they step foot on the University of Kentucky campus. The program is designed to support transfer students through their transition to the university and connect them with fellow students, faculty and staff for additional support.
summary: Transfer Year Experience is for Transfers

name: Innovators in Medicine
description:
Innovators in Medicine is an interdisciplinary, student-led organization open to undergraduates, graduate students, and anyone curious about the future of healthcare. We bring together engineering, medicine, and AI to tackle real-world clinical challenges through innovation and collaboration.

 

Students have the opportunity to work alongside physicians and researchers on cutting-edge projects like AI-generated discharge instructions, ambient listening systems in the NICU, and interactive medical education chatbots. Our community offers hands-on research, mentorship, and outreach opportunities that support your journey—whether you're planning to apply to medical school, pursue graduate studies, or launch a career at the intersection of technology and medicine.

 

No prior experience is required—just curiosity, commitment, and a passion for transforming patient care through innovation.
summary: Innovators in Medicine is a student-led organization connecting undergrads and grads with physicians and researchers to solve real clinical problems using AI and engineering. No experience needed—just curiosity and a drive to innovate in healthcare.

name: UK Center for the Environment
description: The UK Center for the Environment serves as a hub for all research-related organizations and centers centers across the university and provides mentorship support for students, staff and faculty who are pursing environmental research.
summary: The UK Center for the Environment serves as a hub for all research-related organizations and centers centers across the university and provides mentorship support for students, staff and faculty who are pursing environmental research.

name: UK Healthcare Transplant Center
description: The UK Transplant Center specializes in the care of patients with advanced, end-stage organ disease. Each year, we perform more than 200 transplant procedures, including kidney, lung, liver, heart, and pancreas transplants, and kidney and lung transplants for children. As an advanced center, we also perform combined transplants for double-lung, liver-lung, kidney-pancreas, heart-lung, and heart-kidney.
summary: The UK Transplant Center specializes in the care of patients with advanced, end-stage organ disease. Each year, we perform more than 200 transplant procedures, including kidney, lung, liver, heart and pancreas transplants, and kidney and lung transplants

name: Management Hub
description: The Management Hub is a student-led organization for business-minded students looking to grow as leaders and future managers. We’re a community at Kentucky where students connect, learn, and grow together through guest speakers, workshops, networking, and collaborative events. Whether you’re exploring management roles or building leadership skills, the Management Hub offers a space to develop professionally and personally.

This is a great opportunity to get involved in the community, while gathering service hours.

name: MedConnect
description: Navigating the path to medical school can be very difficult at times and there are a million ways to get there. It is a valuable relationship to have with someone who has already been through it and can now give you advice on what worked with them. For medicine, this type of mentorship program does not exist at UK, we want to establish that.
summary: We are an organization that wants to set up mentorship relationships between current medical students and current pre-medical undergraduate students.

name: CATS For Ghana
description: CATS For Ghana is an organization brought about to reduce health and educational inequality in Ghana. Due to the direct link between educational outcomes and healthcare affordability, in Ghana, many rural poor families are unable to meet the demands of Ghana's out-of-pocket payment option in hospitals. CATS For Ghana mitigates this by offering free tutoring services to children in rural poor areas. Through our tutoring services, students are provided the necessary tools to succeed in school so that they can get a job that meets the demands of Ghana's healthcare system
summary: Offering free tutoring services for a rural village in Ghana. Through fundraising money(to pay for Internet and computers), books, and school supplies, we provide these donations to keep our tutoring services running.

name: Mindful Medics
description: Welcome to the mental health club for pre-professional students! Pre-professional programs are rigorous and induce a lot of stress. The officers of this club are all pre-med students who have experienced this stress and want to help other students who might be dealing with similar feelings. Mindful Medics values fostering an inclusive environment; therefore, anyone can join! Excelling academically is very important, but we believe that being healthy mentally is even more important. You cannot achieve greatness without being confident in yourself first! The weekly meetings of this club will focus on fun and lighthearted activities that will help take the edge off. We will also focus on stress-relieving techniques and activities to help you be your best self! This, along with connections with like-minded students, will create success and achievement for our members regarding their future careers.
summary: This club will help promote the mental health of premed students on campus. Weekly meetings will be centered around activities that help students get their mind off school and relieve stress.

name: UK Field Hockey Club
description: The Field Hockey Club at the University of Kentucky is dedicated to promoting the sport of field hockey within the university community. Our mission is to provide a welcoming environment for players of all skill levels, from beginners to experienced athletes, to develop their field hockey skills and techniques. We offer opportunities for competitive play through intercollegiate matches, tournaments, and friendly scrimmages, fostering teamwork, sportsmanship, and leadership among our members. The FHC is committed to inclusivity and respect, ensuring a safe and supportive environment for all participants. Join us to improve your game, make new friends, and be part of a dynamic and enthusiastic team!
summary: Join the Field Hockey Club at the University of Kentucky to develop your skills, cultivate teamwork, and engage in friendly competition.

name: Handball Club at The University of Kentucky
description:
Mission Statement: The HANDBALL CLUB AT THE UNIVERSITY OF KENTUCKY is dedicated to promoting the sport of handball among students at the University of Kentucky and within the broader community. Our mission is to provide a welcoming and inclusive environment for individuals of all skill levels to learn, play, and enjoy the exhilarating sport of handball.


About Us: The HANDBALL CLUB AT THE UNIVERSITY OF KENTUCKY is a dynamic and innovative student-led organization that has a passion for introducing and nurturing the sport of handball, a less commonly known sport in the United States. Established in 2024, we have steadily grown into a thriving community of handball enthusiasts who are committed to making this exciting sport accessible to everyone.


Why Handball? Handball is a fast-paced, physically demanding, and highly strategic sport that combines elements of soccer, basketball, and racquetball. While it enjoys immense popularity in Europe and other parts of the world, it remains relatively obscure in the United States. Our organization seeks to change that by exposing students and our local community to the thrill of handball.


What We Offer:
1. Training and Skill Development: We provide coaching and training sessions for beginners and experienced players alike. The focus is to have fun.
2. Friendly Competitions: We organize friendly matches, tournaments, and leagues, offering opportunities for healthy competition and camaraderie.
3. CommunityBuilding:Joining our club means becoming part of a close-knit community of handball enthusiasts. We foster friendships, teamwork, and a sense of belonging.
4. Outreach Programs: We are committed to spreading awareness of handball beyond our club. We collaborate with local schools, organizations, and the broader community to introduce handball to new audiences.
5. Inclusivity: We welcome individuals of all backgrounds and abilities. Our club is a safe space for everyone to enjoy handball, regardless of experience or skill level.
summary: The HANDBALL CLUB AT THE UNIVERSITY OF KENTUCKY is dedicated to promoting the sport of handball among students at the University of Kentucky and within the broader community. Our goal is to learn, play, and enjoy the exhilarating sport of handball.

name: Collective of Fine Artists and Scientists
description: It is our belief that science and art, though commonly perceived as separate fields of study, actually work harmoniously together! The goal of CFAS is to merge science and art in such a way that an interdisciplinary connection forms; improving both the artistic and scientific knowledge of the members. CFAS also hopes to engage in community education by utilizing the connection of science and art.
summary: CFAS is a club dedicated to merging science and art in an educational and recreational setting!

name: Hospitality Haven
description:
REQUEST TO JOIN OUR GROUPME HERE!

Hospitality Haven, we're all about forging close-knit bonds while indulging in our shared love for food. As hospitality majors, we understand that the best conversations happen over a meal, so we've made it our mission to explore the culinary wonders of Lexington together.

From trendy newcomers to beloved local joints, we'll venture out each week to taste the essence of the Bluegrass region. Whether it's sampling authentic Kentucky fare or diving into international cuisine, our adventures promise to be as exciting as our members.

But what truly sets Hospitality Haven apart is the sense of camaraderie we foster. Here, you'll find more than just dining companions – you'll find a supportive network of like-minded individuals who share your career aspirations and passion for hospitality.

So, if you're ready to blend your love for food with meaningful connections, come join us at Hospitality Haven. Together, we'll savor every bite, create lasting memories, and build a tight-knit community that feels just like home. After all, in the world of hospitality, the best experiences are always shared.
summary: Welcome to Hospitality Haven, the ultimate gathering place for Lexington's hospitality mavens! If you're passionate about the art of hospitality and crave connections beyond the classroom, then you've found your tribe.

name: Criminal Justice Association
description:
The Criminal Justice Association (CJA) is a professional organization dedicated to the advancement of knowledge and professional development within the field of criminal justice. The association serves as a network for students, professionals, and academics who are interested in or working in areas related to law enforcement, corrections, legal studies, forensic science, criminology, and more.

Members of the CJA have access to resources such as conferences, workshops, and networking events that provide opportunities to stay informed about current trends and issues in criminal justice. The association also promotes ethical practices, supports research initiatives, and encourages collaboration among its members to address challenges and improve the criminal justice system. Whether you are a student beginning your journey in criminal justice or a seasoned professional, the CJA offers a platform to grow, connect, and contribute to the field.
summary: The Criminal Justice Association (CJA) connects students and professionals, offering resources like workshops and networking to promote ethical practices, collaboration, and advancement in criminal justice.

name: Skitty Cats
description: Skitty Cats is an improv club that will use various games and team building activities to build individuals’ understanding of improv and how to use it. As the members start to master basic improv structure, we'll move onto more complex improv structures. The club will help promote and improve communication and collaboration, which can be applied not only to better improv teams, but also daily lives and situations in various other professions.
summary: Skitty Cats is an improv club welcome to all where we will learn the basic principles of improv to effectively practice the art with a team. We will also learn how to apply improv to daily life, regardless of profession.

name: Wellness Initiative
summary: An organization that promotes physical and mental health in the Rosenberg College of Law community.

name: Girls Club Kentucky
description: Girls Club National is committed to providing opportunities and resources to young women passionate about sports. Our mission is to empower girls and help them succeed in the world of sports. We offer various resources, networking events, and other tools/opportunities to help them achieve their goals. We are looking to break the stigma that sports are only for boys because it’s a girls' club, too. Girls Club Kentucky will be open to all female students on the University of Kentucky's Campus.
summary: Girls Club National is committed to providing opportunities and resources to young women passionate about sports. We offer various workshops, networking events, and other tools to help girls succeed in the sports industry. Sports are for the girls too!

description: Our mission is to foster a sense of community and belonging, celebrate the rich heritage of India, and support students academically, socially, and culturally. Through a variety of events, including cultural festivals, social gatherings, workshops, and networking opportunities, IWA aims to create a vibrant, inclusive space for students to connect, share experiences, and grow together as a community at UK. Whether you are looking to celebrate your roots or learn more about India, the Indian Wildcats Association welcomes you!
summary: The Indian Wildcats Association (IWA) is a student-run organization at the University of Kentucky, dedicated to bringing together Indian students and those interested in Indian culture.

summary: Like a run club but instead we walk! Walking Wildcats is for everybody. We are here to show that health and wellness does not have to be strenuous. We will go for low impact walks that are meant to just promote physical activity and wellbeing.

name: Letters of Love
description:
We want to change the world with kindness and empower young people to get involved in their communities. No kid should feel alone, especially when they’re sick. Our mission is to provide emotional support to sick and hospitalized kids, one card at a time.

We want each and every child to know that they are loved, supported, and never alone. And we can’t do that without our amazing clubs. Our clubs are the lifeblood of the work we do — without you, we wouldn’t be able to send thousands of cards every year to our little superheroes. 

We now donate to 60+ hospitals, and have 222 active chapters across the nation actively participating in Letters of Love. We have received cards from over 18 different countries.

​There are thousands of Letters of Love members across the world, spreading positivity and kindness one card and a time. I am so proud to say that Letters of Love has had the honor of providing emotional support to over 248,000 patients in Children’s Hospitals around the world. I am so thrilled to see how far this organization goes, as we continue to work towards our goal of providing emotional support for each and every patient in every Children’s Hospital across the world.


JOIN GROUPME:

https://groupme.com/join_group/103755924/ttkX4qIk
summary: There are thousands of Letters of Love members across the world, spreading positivity and kindness one card and a time. Through our card-making and fundraisers, we hope that each and every child knows that they are loved, supported, and never alone.

name: Society of Automotive Engineers Paducah
description:
As a student run organization, SAE is dedicated to cultivating both interest and proficiency in automotive engineering through experiential learning. By providing hands-on experience, SAE guides students through the comprehensive process of designing, building, and testing vehicles such as the Baja Buggy and Human Exploration Rover. Through these multiple processes, members are given the opportunity to apply theoretical knowledge to practical automotive challenges, competing in well renowned competitions. 

Operating through specialized sub-teams, SAE focuses on key areas such as chassis design, suspension systems, drivetrain mechanics, and more, enabling students to develop and hone their expertise in specific technical domains. Through the confrontation of complex engineering problems, students are tasked with developing innovative solutions while maintaining balance between cost efficiency, performance, safety standards, and environmental sustainability. 

In overcoming these challenges, students gain valuable experience in project management, communication, leadership, and budgeting as they work together on complex projects with stringent deadlines.

These experiences aim to not only bridge the gap between academic theory and industry application, but to also provide students numerous opportunities to network with industry professionals, attend technical seminars, and stay on the edge of emerging automotive technologies, enhancing their prospects for future careers in the field.
summary: As a student-run organization, SAE fosters innovation and project management skills through hands-on automotive engineering. Students design, build, and test vehicles, gaining expertise in a wide range of automotive related disciplines.

name: SPIE Student Chapter
description:
The SPIE Student Chapter at the University of Kentucky, housed within the Biomedical Engineering Department, is the university’s first and only chapter dedicated to optics and photonics. As a newly established global chapter recognized by SPIE, we connect students with a worldwide network of future changemakers in the field. Our mission is to foster leadership, technical expertise, and professional development through hands-on projects, outreach activities, and collaborations with industry leaders.
This chapter aims to promote optics and photonics across the university and the Commonwealth of Kentucky, enriching the academic community and supporting the University’s mission to advance STEM education. We engage undergraduate students and local high schools through workshops, research opportunities, and interactive events, empowering them to explore this innovative field.
Joining our chapter offers students the chance to develop leadership, communication, and networking skills while contributing to impactful innovations in optics, photonics, biomedical engineering and beyond. Be part of the future of optics and photonics at the University of Kentucky!
summary: The SPIE Student Chapter fosters student growth in optics and photonics through hands-on projects, workshops, and industry collaboration.We promote innovation in STEM and provide opportunities for leadership,technical skills, and professional development

name: Kentucky Women in Finance
description:
Our mission as Kentucky Women in Finance at the Gatton College of Business and Economics is to empower women in finance through education, networking, and professional development. We are dedicated to fostering a community that values diversity, inclusion, and the unique contributions of women in finance.

Our goal is to create a collaborative space that nurtures creativity, innovation, and leadership among women. Through a range of programs, events, and activities, we strive to engage our members and provide opportunities for both personal and professional growth tailored to their aspirations in the financial sector.

We are committed to delivering high-quality resources and support, ensuring that our members have access to valuable networking opportunities, professional development workshops, and hands-on experiences. Our emphasis on first-class service means that we continuously seek to meet the specific needs of women in finance, helping them build confidence and skills to succeed.

By upholding our core values of engagement, empowerment, and community, we aim to cultivate the next generation of women financial leaders and support them in achieving their professional ambitions. Our mission is to provide an enriching experience for women students, preparing them for impactful and successful careers in finance.
summary: An organization all about building connections with professionals in the Finance industry. This organization will be open to all majors and all undergrad students at UK. We will have one fun event a month along with one guest speaker a month and workshop

We provide structured support for students who want to launch and grow business ventures on campus. Through workshops, mentorship, validation reviews, and demo events, members gain real-world entrepreneurial experience while building ventures with measurable progress.

LVC connects student founders with university resources, ecosystem partners, and funding pathways to help transform ideas into executable ventures.
summary: Lewis Ventures Club (LVC) is a student-led organization at UK that helps students launch and grow ventures through mentorship, workshops, and resources, connecting founders with partners and funding to turn ideas into real businesses.

name: Northern Kentucky Military Interest Group
description: The Northern Kentucky Military Interest Group's purpose is to spread awareness and accurately inform students of military medicine and how they can become a part of it through the Health Professions Scholarship Program. We would like to debunk myths and clarify facts; share our knowledge and experiences; connect military members, prospective military members, and military member supporters to each other; and, above all, support students who have already decided to undertake the life-changing journey of joining military medicine. In addition, we would also like to encourage any non-military members or prior military members interested in military medicine to join our organization to learn more about military medicine and support those serving or who have served.
summary: The Northern Kentucky Military Interest Group's purpose is to spread awareness and inform students of military medicine and how they can become part of it through the Health Professions Scholarship Program and, above all, support military students.
