name: Asian Pacific American Medical Student Association
summary: APAMSA promotes cultural and mental health awareness, mentorship, and diversity in medical education. We celebrate Asian Pacific American identities, support mental wellness, and guide students for academic success and professional growth in medicine.

name: Rosenberg College of Law, Office of Student Services
shortName: Rosenberg College of Law - Student Services
summary: The BBNvolved page for the Office of Student Services within the Rosenberg College of Law.

name: Pakistani Student Association
summary: The purpose and mission of the Pakistani Student Association shall be to represent, preserve, and promote the Pakistani Culture while creating a friendly atmosphere in which students can network and interact with one another.

name: Paper & Purpose: A Journaling & Planning Club
description:
Welcome to Paper and Purpose, where we’re all about making creativity and productivity cozy! We’re a student-run club at UK for students who love to journal, plan, scrapbook, and express themselves in other mindful ways.

What our officers have always wanted from a club is a relaxed, calm space to work on our individual passion projects with likeminded people, and that's why we decided to bring Paper and Purpose to life at UK. If you are intimidated by other extracurriculars and want a low pressure way to meet new people, this is the perfect club for you. Through relaxed meetings and fun activities, Paper and Purpose is here to help you stay inspired, organized, and make some new friends! ✨

Come join us! All the information you need can be found on our Instagram or in our Linktree.
summary: Paper and Purpose is a creative club for UK students interested in journaling, planning, scrapbooking, bullet journaling, and other reflective practices. Our club provides a calm, supportive space for members to unwind and focus on self-expression.

name: Thursdays
description: Our goal is to create a collaborative space for students to engage in campus activities, develop leadership skills, manage projects effectively, and gain hands-on experience in event management. We aim to inspire new ideas and support their development from inception to execution, providing valuable experiences in event planning, cost analysis, marketing, and leadership.
summary: To promote continuous personal project development among club members, fostering entrepreneurial skills that enhance resumes and personal growth.

name: Latin Social Work Scholars
description:
To establish a space for scholarship for Latin scholars to connect academically and provide mentorship to COSW students (BSW/MSW/DSW).
Promote Latin student social connection through UK.
Promote and increase UKY COSW Latin student community and engagement
summary: To establish a space for unity for Latin scholars to connect academically through the social work practices.

name: Association for Women In Sports Media
description:
The Association for Women in Sports Media at the University of Kentucky is a student-run organization that promotes the advancement and support of women and gender-diverse individuals in the fields of sports television, marketing, public relations, and writing. Founded in 2024, the University of Kentucky chapter of AWSM regularly hosts speaker events to learn from women in the industry. The group also hosts regular meetings, allowing members to discuss the industry and give and receive advice about the sports media world. And despite its name, students of all gender identities are welcome at AWSM. 

For more information on the national AWSM organization, please visit http://awsmonline.org/.
summary: A organization whose membership supports the growth and advancement of women in sports media.

name: Orthopedic/Sports Medicine Interest Group
description: The Orthopedic/Sports Medicine Interest Group will be a new interest group for medical students at the Northern Kentucky Campus. The main goal of the group is to offer opportunities to volunteer, network, and get experience within the field of orthopedics and sports medicine. One of the main focuses will be volunteer opportunities within the community. Our interest group will work with Franciscan Friars in Cincinnati for regular volunteer experience. This is a facility that offers free foot care to the underserved population. This will be a great experience for students to work hands in an orthopedic related field and help the underserved population. OSMIG will provide networking opportunities through the monthly meetings. At the meetings, the group will feature clinicians that work within the orthopedic or sports medicine field. They will be able to provide current students with advise and connect them with potential shadowing or research opportunities. The practice within the field will come through our work with Franciscan Friars and potentially procedural workshops. For some of the group meetings, hands on workshops will be offered. OSMIG will bring in clinicians and/or upperclass medical students to teach basic procedures that will be used in orthopedics and/or sports medicine. Overall, OSMIG will be a great opportunity for medical students who would like to pursue an interest in orthopedics or sports medicine.
summary: OSMIG will serve as a way for medical students at the Northern Kentucky Campus to pursue an interest in orthopedics and/or sports medicine. The interest group will offer volunteering, networking, and practice within the field.

name: Distillation, Wine, and Brewing Club
description:
The Distillation, Wine & Brewing Science Club at the University of Kentucky is a dynamic and inclusive community for students passionate about the art and science of fermentation, distillation, and beverage production. Open to all students enrolled in the DWBS certificate program, our club serves as a hub for networking, learning, and exploration within the world of alcohol production.

Our mission is to foster an engaging environment where students can deepen their understanding of distillation, wine, and brewing sciences, while building connections with peers, professionals, and experts in the field. Through club events like educational tours of local distilleries, vineyards, and breweries, as well as guest speaker presentations from industry leaders, we provide unique opportunities for hands-on learning and real-world insight.

Whether you're looking to expand your technical knowledge, share ideas, or simply meet like-minded individuals, the DWBS Club offers a space for students to grow both academically and socially in the exciting world of beverage production. Join us to broaden your horizons, enhance your academic journey, and immerse yourself in the culture and science behind the alcohol industry.
summary: The DWBS club at the University of Kentucky is a dynamic organization dedicated to fostering a vibrant community for students enrolled in the DWBS certificate. Our club serves as a hub for collaboration, education, and growth in the beverage industry.

name: Cadet Association for Team Success
summary: About Booster club for the University of Kentucky Army Reserve Officer Training Corps cadet program. (Not affiliated with the United States Army or Army ROTC.)

name: Student Advocates for Rural Healthcare and Dentistry
description: Student Advocates for Rural Healthcare and Dentistry (SARHD) aims to educate urban populations, raise awareness of the disparities in rural healthcare and dentistry, and support rural students pursuing careers in these fields through education and resources. Our mission is to foster meaningful change within underserved communities.
summary: SARHD is a student-led organization that aims to empower students that wish to have a voice and meaningful impact within rural healthcare and dentistry. Our main goal is to make a difference in rural communities, no matter how big or small it is.

name: Rho Epsilon Iota
description:
Our Mission:

  • National Expansion: Become the future of professional real estate fraternities, nationally.
  • Inclusive Environment: Create a space for real estate minds to flourish regardless of prior experience.
  • Professional Advancement: Promote the professional advancement of all aspiring real estate professionals through commercial, residential, industrial, land, and special-use programs.
  • High Standards: Uphold the highest standards of honesty, equality, representation, knowledge, and professionalism on college campuses and within the community.

 

Core Values:

 

  • Honesty: Emphasize ethical behavior and transparency in all real estate dealings to build trust and credibility among members and with external stakeholders.
  • Innovation: Foster creativity and forward-thinking in the real estate market. Encourage the adoption of new technologies and strategies to stay ahead in a competitive industry.

 

Community: Highlight the importance of contributing to and improving local communities through real estate initiatives. This involves sustainable development, community service projects, or educational outreach programs.
summary: Rho Epsilon Iota is the nation’s first premier real estate fraternity, powered by FIRE (Future Investors in Real Estate). We are dedicated to fostering the next generation of real estate professionals by creating an inclusive environment for learning.

name: Step-Up Club
description: This organization exists to support K-12 students from migrant and refugee families in navigating educational challenges and preparing for post-secondary education. Through interdisciplinary service-learning, we aim to enhance existing community efforts, foster educational equity, and promote the self-sufficiency and academic success of migrant and refugee youth.
summary: This organization exists to support K-12 students from migrant and refugee families in navigating educational challenges and preparing for post-secondary education through service-learning

name: Keisha Fan Club
description: The purpose of Keisha Fan Club (KFC) shall be to promote the development, teaching, and growth of volleyball at the University of Kentucky and in the surrounding community.
summary: Pick up volleyball club.

name: Future of Sport Institute (FoSI) Connect
description: FoSI Connect is supported by the Future of Sport Institute (FoSI). Founded in 2023, FoSI exists to transform how sport is played, experienced, and led. FoSI Connect serves as the Institute’s hub for student support and engagement. We’re a student-led, interdisciplinary community who are eager to learn more about the sport industry. As a member, you’ll connect with industry leaders, explore internships and job opportunities, and collaborate with peers who share your passion for the future of sport.
summary: FoSI Connect - Where Future Sport Industry Leaders Come Together

name: The Wildcat Twirlers
summary: This organization gives twirlers the opportunity to exhibit their skills in competition and community performances. Twirlers will also have the opportunity to teach the next generation of twirlers.

name: Wildcat Law Society
description: The Wildcat Law Society is dedicated to fostering a supportive and engaging community for students passionate about pursuing a career in law. Our mission is to inform members of valuable opportunities, assist with academic or professional challenges, and create a collaborative space for future legal professionals to thrive. Through educational workshops, competitions such as mock trials and debates, networking events to meet professionals in the field, and even simply social gatherings, we aim to build connections, provide guidance, and inspire success in the legal field. Along with our other initiatives, we strive to focus on one main goal: to create relationships that will last a lifetime. Together, we aim to empower one another as we embark on our journey toward impactful careers in law.
summary: The Wildcat Law Society is dedicated to fostering a supportive and engaging community for students passionate about pursuing a career in law. Our mission is to inform members of valuable opportunities and assist with academic or professional challenges.

name: The Scratch Post
summary: The Scratch Post is a student-run arts and literature journal dedicated to publishing work produced by undergraduate students at the University of Kentucky.

name: The Wellness Society
description: Club for like minded college students to redefine the college experience by creating healthy habits in students. Together, let's thrive in mind and body throughout the transformative journey of higher education. Join us in shaping a college experience that values balance and the pursuit of overall health- both mental and physical.
summary: Welcome to The Wellness Society, where we redefine the college experience by establishing a culture that celebrates self-care, resilience, and holistic health.

name: The Kentucky Music Business Club
description: The Kentucky Music Business Club is designed to provide members with valuable, hands-on experiences that promote a love for music and the industry. Members of KMBC will participate in panels with industry executives, learn about internship and job opportunities, attend site visits, and engage in other fun events throughout the semester. Members will also learn how to succeed in respective industry sectors, communicate on a professional level, and market themselves in a way that welcomes success. Through our innate love for music, we aim to help members develop relative skills in an effort to create a deeper value for the artists and the industry that we represent.
summary: The Kentucky Music Business Club aims to promote a love for music and the industry. Members can expect to participate in panels with industry executives, learn about internship and job opportunities, attend site visits, and engage in other fun events.

name: Neurosurgery & Neurology Interest Group - Bowling Green
summary: The Neurosurgery & Neurology Interest Group - Bowling Green is an interest group open to all UKCOM students interested in a career in neurosurgery or neurology. Our group focuses on exposure and networking with current physicians in these fields.

name: Brighter Smiles
summary: Brighter Smiles is a pediatric oral health initiative dedicated to improving dental hygiene among children in Kentucky. By addressing the state's higher-than-average rates of dental decay, we aim to educate kids about the importance of oral care.

name: SeriousFun Volunteers Club
description:
The SeriousFun Volunteer Club at the University of Kentucky is dedicated to supporting the mission of SeriousFun Children’s Network, a global organization that provides life-changing camp experiences for children with serious illnesses. Our club’s goal is to raise awareness about the network’s work, mobilize volunteers, and offer students a meaningful way to contribute to the lives of children facing health challenges.

Through volunteering, fundraising, and organized meetings, we aim to support the camps, as well as the families and communities that benefit from them. Whether it's assisting with local events, helping to create inclusive experiences, or directly supporting children through various activities, our members work to ensure that every child has the chance to laugh, play, and discover their full potential—just like any other child.

Monthly Meetings:

The SeriousFun Volunteer Club will hold monthly meetings that are designed to inform, engage, and empower members. Each meeting typically covers a range of topics, including:

 

  • Volunteering Application Support – Many of our meetings will focus on assisting students with the application process for becoming a volunteer at SeriousFun camps. We walk members through the steps to apply, provide tips for writing strong applications, and answer any questions about the volunteer experience. We’ll also discuss what to expect from the application process and how to prepare for your time at camp.
  • History of SeriousFun Children’s Network – Understanding the roots and vision of SeriousFun is key to connecting with its mission. Some of our meetings delve into the history of the organization, founded by actor and philanthropist Paul Newman. We’ll explore the story behind the first SeriousFun camp, its growth into a global network, and how its core values—such as inclusivity, joy, and respect—continue to shape the way camps are run today.
  • Camp Preparation and Training – Meetings may also include training sessions or workshops that focus on the skills needed to be a successful volunteer at a SeriousFun camp. This could include child-centered approaches, adapting to the needs of children with serious illnesses, and how to create a supportive and fun environment at camp.
  • Social Events & Fundraising Initiatives – In addition to informative sessions, we may plan fundraising activities to raise awareness and funds for SeriousFun. These events provide members with the opportunity to connect, have fun, and work together to support the network’s programs.

 

The SeriousFunVolunteer Club will have the opportunity to make a direct impact, build a community of like-minded individuals, and gain hands-on experience in volunteerism, community outreach, and nonprofit work.
summary: This goal of this organization is to provide information and opportunity for UK students to attend and volunteer at SeriousFun children camps across the county. These camps serve children with serious illnesses and volunteers are in high demand.

name: Otolaryngology/ENT Interest Group
description:
The name of our organization is the Otolaryngology/ENT Interest Group, abbreviated ENT-IG. This is an organization at the University of Kentucky College of Medicine - Bowling Green campus.

This group exists for students interested in applying to an Otolaryngology residency or learning more about the field of Otolaryngology to be connected with other like-minded students. This group also serves to connect students with faculty members for shadowing and research opportunities, facilitate volunteering opportunities with organizations in the South-Central Kentucky area, and to educate students on other opportunities available to them in the field. Finally, we aim to help students planning on applying to Otolaryngology residencies by connecting them with faculty mentors to help strengthen their applications.
summary: Interest group at the UKCOM - Bowling Green Campus dedicated to promoting the field of Otolaryngology, participating in related shadowing/research/volunteer opportunities, and strengthening applications for Otolaryngology residencies

name: Wildcat Game Studios
description: We were founded in February 2025, and our mission is to help cultivate a community of game developers and designers in Lexington at UK. Comprised of people from art, engineering, and various other degree programs we work with students all across the university to help teach game design and teamwork through our own internal projects. Using the the game engine Godot, we have experience in leading teams to make game ideas a reality. All of our art, code, design, and music is created in house by our own members. We also help teach people development, and act as a helping force to break down the barrier and learning curve of game development. Meet us at one of our general meetings Tuesday nights in JSB 103 from 6:30-8, or see us at one of our many events we hold across campus. If you're interested in art, programming, design, or music this is the place for you to further your skills in a team setting while developing video games!
summary: We are a video game studio at the University of Kentucky. We are open to all students, and aim to help create, teach, and develop a community for making video games! We focus on programming, art, design, and music for our games.

name: Strut Society
description: Strut Society at the University of Kentucky is a dynamic student-led dance organization dedicated to empowering individuals through movement. While heel dancing is the main focus, the organization explores various dance styles to promote creativity, confidence, and inclusivity. Strut Society provides a welcoming space where students of all experience levels can develop their skills, connect with like-minded peers, and celebrate self-expression. The organization encourages artistic growth through engaging workshops, performances, and community events, fostering a supportive environment that embraces individuality and empowerment.
summary: Strut Society is a dance organization focusing on heel dancing while embracing various styles. It fosters confidence, creativity, and self-expression through workshops, performances, and community engagement in an inclusive

name: Med-Peds Interest Group
summary: Med-Peds Interest group will strive to promote knowledge of med-peds as a residency option, provide educational opportunities within this specialty, and provide current medical students with mentors in the field.

name: Students of Medicine in Aquatic Training
description: The organization addresses the growing UKCOM student interest in swimming while promoting student well-being. There is a significant community of students who were either former competitive swimmers seeking a less competitive setting or interested in learning swimming basics from a beginner and safety standpoint. This club would aim to address both populations in a fun, low-stakes environment. While there are a few clubs combining student wellness with physical activity, these clubs only offer a once-a-month activity. Our club will have weekly activities providing an important physical break from studying that promotes mental and physical health and are graduate student accessible.
summary: SMAC is a graduate student swim club that will have organized swim workouts for students of all levels at least once a week.

name: Engineering Wellness Center
description: The Engineering Wellness Center (EWC) was created to support the mental, emotional, and physical well-being of our students. We believe wellness is essential for success in both academic and professional life. Whether you're a student navigating coursework or a faculty member supporting others, EWC is here to help you thrive.
summary: Engineering Wellness Center: Wellness is success—empowering students to grow, balance, and thrive.

name: University of Kentucky Jiu-Jitsu Club
summary: This is a training group for Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu, wrestling, judo, and all forms of grappling. Anyone from complete beginners to experienced competitors is welcome; Our main focus is technique, strategy, and live-rolling

name: IBC College
description: IBC College is a group of students at University of Kentucky who love Jesus. We are actively across campus life at UK (different majors, Greek life, athletics, intramurals, etc.) Many of us go to Immanuel Baptist Church together. Our goal is that every student in the UK community would have a chance to know Jesus and grow in their faith. We welcome anyone to join in with our community!
summary: Leading college students to Jesus and helping them grow in their faith. Come join our community!

name: Sales Club
description:
The purpose of this organization is to:

  1. Develop members’ skills to prepare them for real-world sales situations.
  2. Train members to execute in sales competitions. 
  3. Discuss relevant topics to improve members’ job marketability.
  4. Provide mentorship & networking opportunities for members.
  5. Foster a collaborative environment for new ideas and professional growth.
summary: The University of Kentucky Sales Club empowers future sales leaders through mentorship, real-world experience, professional skill development, and strategic networking—equipping members with the tools, confidence, and connections needed to excel.

name: Women Business Leaders
description:
This program pairs Gatton undergraduates with prominent female business leaders to foster personal and professional growth. Students attend monthly sessions, have bi-weekly mentor meetings, and develop relationships with other mentees throughout the year.  Open to all Gatton Students.

 

 

WBL Mentee Application for 2026-2027  

APPLY HERE
summary: This program pairs Gatton undergraduates with prominent female business leaders to foster personal and professional growth. Students attend monthly sessions, have bi-weekly mentor meetings, and develop relationships with other mentees throughout the year

name: ConnectFirst
description:
ConnectFirst is a Mentoring student organization tailored towards student leader development. Our vision is to empower every student with the essential knowledge, connections, and resources needed to confidently navigate their academic journey, excel in career preparation, and successfully launch into their chosen professional path. We plan to implement our vision by empowering the participants by offering monthly workshops anchored to networking, including interactive activities such as site visits for developmental experiences, and intentional meet-ups to grow connections within the cohort.

 

If interested in joining our cohort as mentee, please use this LINK.
summary: ConnectFirst offers Gatton Business students a mentoring experience focused on building relationships and leadership skills for a future business career. This is tailored for first-gen and underrepresented students but open to all business students.

name: Onelove Foundation
description:
One Love is a nonprofit organization dedicated to educating youth about healthy and unhealthy relationships. One Love started in 2010 after Yeardly Love, a student at the University of Virginia, was tragically killed by her ex-boyfriend three weeks before she was supposed to graduate. 

One Love raises awareness about the warning signs of relationship abuse through community events, workshops, and education. Additionally, One Love's "Escalation" program, a film-based seminar, helps young people recognize and understand the signs of an unhealthy relationship. One Love also provides resources and tools to empower youth to create healthy relationships and intervene when there are signs of abuse. 

Join our GroupMe!
summary: One Love is a nonprofit organization dedicated to educating youth about healthy and unhealthy relationships. One Love started in 2010 after Yeardly Love, a student at the University of Virginia, was tragically killed by her ex-boyfriend.

name: Aviation Club
description:
The purpose of the Aviation Club is 
  1. To foster a current or new passion for aviation among students.
  2. To educate students about the aviation industry at the University of Kentucky.
  3. To provide a welcoming and supportive environment for students to explore their interest in the aviation industry.
  4. To share various knowledge about aviation through different viewpoints and experience levels through its members and guest speakers.

 

What you will be doing/learning in the club:
  • How to identify all commercial aircraft (some military)
  • Plane spotting at the Blue Grass Airport (LEX)
  • Watching live plane spotting on YouTube and other aviation videos
  • Visiting the Kentucky National Aviation Museum
  • Learning about aviation careers
  • Trivia 
  • Hang out meetings
  • Learning about flight fundamentals, plane and ATC terminology
  • Learning about airlines 
  • & more!

Want to become a member?

Click here to access the Aviation Club interest form

All majors and any level of knowledge of aviation is welcome!
summary: A welcoming club where any student can learn more about aviation at the University of Kentucky. Main club activities includes plane spotting at the Bluegrass Airport and watching live plane spotting videos. Students learn about all things aviation!

name: Gen 1 Physicians
description: Gen 1 Physicians is an organization meant to connect, support, and provide resources for first generation medical students at the UKCOM-NKY campus. First generation medical students are defined as students that are the first members of their family to attend medical school.
summary: Gen 1 Physicians is an organization at the UKCOM-NKY campus meant to connect, support, and provide resources for medical students who are the first in their family to attend medical school.

name: Urban Forest Initiative
description: The Urban Forest Initiative (UFI) is a collaborative group of University of Kentucky and Kentucky community stakeholders whose purpose is to promote and protect the urban tree canopy on the UK campus, in Lexington, and throughout the region. UFI is dedicated to maintaining the current tree canopy while promoting and planning for our future urban forests. We strive to raise awareness of the ecological, social, and economic benefits provided by urban trees.
summary: BBNvolved page for the Urban Forest Initiative.

name: Cats Vote Coalition: An Every Vote Counts Chapter
description: The Cats Vote Coalition: An Every Vote Counts Chapter exists to empower University of Kentucky students to learn about and engage with civic issues and voting. CVC aims to excite the broader campus community to participate in civic engagement and literacy activities to develop a deeper understanding and knowledge of voting. CVC is a chapter of the national nonprofit Every Vote Counts.
summary: The Cats Vote Coalition: An Every Vote Counts Chapter exists to empower University of Kentucky students to learn about and engage with civic issues and voting.

name: Egyptian Student Association
description: The Egyptian Student Association is dedicated to celebrating and promoting Egyptian culture, heritage, and unity within the campus community. Our mission is to foster a welcoming environment for students of all backgrounds to connect, engage, and grow through our cultural events, educational initiatives, and social gatherings. We aim to share the richness of Egypt’s history and traditions, and build lasting bonds that reflect the spirit of diversity, pride, and togetherness. Another focus of the organization is to help students achieve success in their academic careers through different activities hosted by the organization.
summary: The Egyptian Student Association is dedicated to celebrating and promoting Egyptian culture, heritage, and unity within the campus community. Our mission is to foster a welcoming community through cultural events, education, and social engagement.

name: Mind Over Matter
summary: A student led organization dedicated to spreading body neutrality and reminding campus that we are all worth more than what we look like.

name: Hebrew Club
summary: Hebrew (HJS) club is a space for students to come together, share their love for a beautiful language, and learn more about Jewish studies and language through fun activities.

name: Nepali Undergraduate Association
description:
The Nepali Undergraduate Association (NUA) is a student-led group at the University of Kentucky that builds community and celebrates Nepali culture while supporting students academically, socially, and professionally.

  • Creates a welcoming space for Nepali students to connect and support each other.

  • Hosts events that celebrate Nepali festivals, language, food, and traditions.

  • Supports new students with mentorship and guidance during their transition.

  • Provides career and academic resources through peer sessions and alumni connections.

  • Collaborates with other student organizations for cultural, social, and service-based events.
summary: The Nepali Undergraduate Association (NUA) at the University of Kentucky is a student-led organization that  supports Nepali students through cultural events, mentorship, career development, and community engagement.

name: Flex in Full Color
description: Flex in Full Color is a wellness community in support of black students and students of color (expansion), at all levels, to thrive in their active lives through all forms of exercise (calisthenics, weight-lifting, boxing, pilates, cross-fit, and more). Together, we grow into the best version of ourselves through movement, friendship, and mindfulness, while building a strong foundation of health, wellness, and balance. Our monthly meetings will feature wellness discussions, fun activities, trivia, and a special lifting event—blended with group bonding and physical movement. We also give back through community service and volunteering. All are welcome to support and uplift one another. Wanna learn more? Join our GroupMe and Follow our Instagram to get involved!
summary: Flex in Full Color is a supportive community for Black women and women of color to thrive in their active lives through all forms of exercise, and to grow into the best version of themselves through movement, friendship, and mindfulness.

name: Path to Practice
summary: An undergraduate research club, made with the intention to build future investigators. Through peer led instruction and events, we create a low pressure environment to collecting research experience to help students excel in their future endeavors.

name: Student Professionalism & Ethics Association in Dentistry
description:
The Student Professionalism and Ethics Association in Dentistry is a national, student-driven association that was established to promote and support students' lifelong commitment to ethical behavior in order to benefit the patients they serve and to further the dental profession.

The objectives of the Association are:

  • Act as a support system for students in strengthening their personal and professional ethics values by:
  • Providing a resource for ethics education and professional development
  • Fostering a non-punitive, open-forum environment for ethics communication
  • Promoting awareness of ethics standards and related issues within dentistry
  • Collaborating with leadership of the dental profession to effectively advocate for our member
summary: The Student Professionalism and Ethics Association in Dentistry strives to promote and support students' lifelong commitment to ethical behavior in order to benefit the patients they serve and to further the dental profession.

name: Student One Health Organization
description: The One Health (OH) concept states humans, animals and the environment are interactively linked and in order to address health issues in both animals and humans we must work across disciplines to obtain a healthy and sustainable planet. We facilitate connecting students from different fields of study to promote the OH concept.
summary: The Student One Health Organization is a student led body dedicated in serving and advancing the interests of all UK students through preserving the health of humans, domestic and wild animals, plants and the wider environment.

name: Prosthodontics Study Club
summary: The goal of the prosthodontics study club is to help students gain confidence with case presentation, treatment planning, materials selection, and more.

name: Witness Church International Campus Fellowship
description: Witness Church International (WCI) Campus Fellowship at the University of Kentucky is a student-led ministry focused on developing disciples who live out practical Christianity and reflect Christ in their lifestyle, character, and attitude. Rooted in Acts 1:8, our organization serves the growing community of WCI-affiliated students on campus, as well as any student seeking spiritual growth and a deeper relationship with Jesus Christ. Through regular Bible studies, worship services, leadership development initiatives, and outreach events, we are committed to fostering a vibrant Christian community marked by excellence, impact, and authentic faith.
summary: WCI Campus Fellowship at UK is a student ministry focused on raising disciples and equipping students through the undiluted Word of God. We serve WCI members and all students seeking spiritual growth through Bible studies and prayer.

name: Paws For Hope
description: Paws for hope club is united by a shared commitment to compassion, service, and community health, and was established to promote medical philanthropy through creative fundraising, outreach, and advocacy. We also aim to promote this among college student.
summary: Paws for hope club is united by a shared commitment to compassion, service, and community health, and was established to promote medical philanthropy through creative fundraising, outreach, and advocacy. We also aim to promote this among college student.

name: Something More
summary: Something More is designed to unify students through creating Christian based community events, to share the Gospel of Jesus with members of the campus community and to serve the campus in a variety of ways.

name: United Health
summary: This organization focuses on closing the gap between the medical and dental disciplines through an interdisciplinary care approach.

name: Remote Area Medical
description: The student-led Remote Area Medical chapter at UK is committed to serving rural communities by providing compassionate and high-quality healthcare to those who are underserved, isolated, or facing financial barriers. Through volunteer efforts, we strive to make a meaningful impact on the well-being of those in need. We are a campus chapter of the national organization, Remote Area Medical. Our events include fundraisers, awareness events, and travelling to RAM clinics to volunteer.
summary: is committed to serving rural communities by providing compassionate healthcare to those who are underserved, isolated, or facing financial barriers by working with the national RAM association

name: Greek Run Club
summary: At Greek Run Club, we are dedicated to creating a culture of self-improvement while bringing together all of Greek life on the University of Kentucky campus. We welcome every individual, regardless of background or experience level.

name: Recovery Cats
description: Recovery Cats is a student organization creating a community for all those affected by substance abuse. We work together to provide support for students in recovery, people affected by addiction, and allies to these individuals. Our goal is to create awareness through peer-led events, volunteer opportunities, and sharing our stories to help others.
summary: Recovery Cats is dedicated to providing community for students in recovery, people affected by addiction, and allies of these individuals. We work with university and local organizations to connect students with resources and spread awareness.

name: ISC Association
description: The ISCA represents a dynamic community of students who are passionate about Integrated Strategic Communication. We strive to empower students to develop critical communication skills that bridge media, creativity, and strategy, enabling professional growth and creative innovation.
summary: A dynamic community of students who are passionate about Integrated Strategic Communication.

name: Refuge
description: The purpose of Refuge is to provide a community where refugee students in Lexington connect with college-aged students at UK. This community will provide mentorship that promotes conversational English, assists in acclimation, promotes health literacy, and fosters intercultural exchange and dialogue. In addition to a forum where community members may share resources and experiences, Refuge matches each refugee with a UK student. Participants will work together in sessions to identify challenges for refugee populations and create opportunities to overcome these obstacles. These conversations will establish long-lasting relationships, foster intercultural understanding, and spur further community involvement.
summary: Refuge aims to help refugee students overcome resettlement hardships such as education accessibility, health literacy, and social navigation through mentorship between college students at UK and refugee students in Lexington.

name: Transfer Student Association
description: Our mission is to enhance the transfer student experience by fostering a strong sense of community and belonging. We know that transitioning to a new university can feel overwhelming, which is why we're dedicated to creating meaningful connections among transfer students and the wider UKY family.
summary: The Transfer Student Association strives to foster an inclusive environment for all transfer students at the University of Kentucky by offering academic, professional, and social opportunities on campus.

name: Lifestyle Medicine Interest Group
description: Lifestyle medicine is a patient-centered, evidence-based approach to medicine that is focused on six pillars: nutrition, exercise, sleep, stress management, substance avoidance, and social connection. As an interest group, we aim to expose students and promote the integration of lifestyle interventions into patient care, promote long-term health outcomes, and address the root causes of chronic disease.
summary: Lifestyle medicine is a patient-centered, evidence-based approach that is focused on six pillars: nutrition, exercise, sleep, stress management, substance avoidance, and social connection, which aim to prevent, treat, and reverse chronic disease

name: The Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu Club at the University of Kentucky
description: The Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu Club at The University of Kentucky is focused on growing the sport and creating community connections. KYBJJ encourages members to join local gyms and represent the university at local and national tournaments, via the competition team which focuses on no-gi grappling (Wrestlers Encouraged to Join). Contact Nathan White with interest and questions.
summary: The Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu Club at The University of Kentucky is designed to promote the practice and training of Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu and other grappling sports --such as wrestling-- within the university and the surrounding community.

name: Transportation Services
summary: The BBNvolved page for Transportation Services.

name: ForensiCats
summary: ForensiCats is a community of students interested in forensic science. Opportunities for networking and career exploration will be provided through guest lectures from forensics experts and social events.

name: Songwriters Circle
summary: A community on campus that strives to bring Songwriters together for support in professional pursuits, collaboration, and improving writing skills. Open to everyone interested.

name: Black Pre-Law Student Association
summary: BPLSA serves pre-law students by offering mentorship, resources, and law school guidance, while promoting equity, fostering inclusion, and supporting academic and professional development.

name: Wildcat Health Buddies
summary: To equip elementary school students across Kentucky with basic first aid knowledge, an understanding of personal wellness, and awareness of signs that may indicate common health concerns—building a healthier future from the ground up.

name: Psychology Access, Community & Engagement Club
description: The PACE Club is composed of undergraduate and graduate students majoring in Psychology or Neuroscience, and we are focused on promoting engagement within the Psychology Department, including but not limited to experiential learning, research, extracurricular activities, and service opportunities.
summary: The PACE Club is composed of undergraduate and graduate students majoring in Psychology or Neuroscience, and we are focused on promoting engagement within the Psychology Department, including experiential learning, research, extracurriculars, and service

name: Martin-Gatton College of Agriculture, Food and Environment Graduate Student Council
description: The M-G CAFE GSC exists to unify, represent, and advocate for graduate students within the Martin-Gatton College of Agriculture, Food and Environment in matters affecting quality of academic, professional, and personal life.
summary: A representative body for graduate students in the Martin-Gatton College of Agriculture, Food and Environment.

name: University of Kentucky Wildcat Polo
description: We are dedicated to improving the skill and knowledge for the sport of polo while maintaining a fun and social environment. The polo team is an entirely student run organization that helps promote commitment and responsibility to all of its members. There are no limits to who can play; whether you've been playing polo for your entire life or never been on a horse before, all are welcome! The team will work with each individual and adjust to their level of skill and experience so that you get the most out of the team that you possibly can. Most importantly, the Polo team provides students with an opportunity to get out and have fun, with a great group of people and forming lifelong friendships with your teammates.
summary: The University of Kentucky polo club is a student lead organization that promotes the sport of Polo and provides opportunities for students to learn practice and compete in intercollegiate matches.

name: Intersectional Feminist Collective
summary: The Intersectional Feminist Collective aims to educate students about gender and sexuality, engage in political action and mutual aid across Lexington, and provide a space for like-minded individuals to find community.

name: Judo Club at the University of Kentucky
description:
President: Seth Jean

Vice-President: Avery Duwe

Secretary: Vacant

Treasurer: Vacant

Social Media Manager: Vacant

Safety Officer: Avery Duwe

Coach: Gabriel Balazs (Sandan)

Assistant Coaches: Jason Brions, Nicolas Briot, and Tony Youssefi

The Judo Club at the University of Kentucky is a student-led organization focused on the practice and promotion of Judo. Open to all experience levels, our club provides a welcoming space to learn throws, groundwork, and competition skills. We emphasize discipline, respect, and community while promoting physical fitness, confidence, and self-improvement. Members have opportunities for both recreational training and competitive events throughout the year. (Wrestling, Jiu-Jitsu, athletes, and military encouraged to join)

Whether you’re looking forwards to exercise, learn how to defend yourself, competing, having fun, Judo is a great sport for everyone!

25$/Month Fee for Instructor
summary: Japanese Olympic Martial Art "Judo". From learning self-defense, or exercise, Judo is for anyone. 100% Beginner Friendly, at the cost of 25$/month for 4x practice a week. No other college in Kentucky has a Judo Club other than UK. 2 Free Trials.

name: Up & Up Kentucky
description: Follow us on Instagram to get involved and learn more about our mission!
summary: Up & Up is focused on connecting students to the music and DJ scene in Lexington. We aim to promote marketing and communication skills and foster connections with other universities.

name: Emergency Medicine Student Union
description: The Emergency Medicine Student Union is an organization founded by five student-EMTs eager to create impact on campus.We aim to foster a community of future healthcare leaders through clinical education, hands-on training, and peer mentorship. In our first year, we’ll host workshops, certification prep, speaker events, and simulation nights while building the foundation for a student-led EMS response team to serve the university in the future.
summary: EMSU is a student-led organization dedicated to emergency medicine, leadership, and campus safety preparedness. Founded by five student-EMTs, we offer EMT-related training, skills workshops, and a community for students passionate about prehospital care.

name: Allergy Cats
description:
Allergy Cats is the University of Kentucky’s student organization dedicated to raising awareness, education, and inclusion for those living with food allergies. We believe that safety and belonging should never be optional — every Wildcat deserves to eat confidently and without fear.

Our mission is to create a more allergy-aware campus through outreach events, epinephrine education, community partnerships, and advocacy initiatives. From our “Save Scratch” EpiPen trainings to the Allergy Cats Safe Eats Guide, we empower students, families, and staff to understand, support, and protect the food allergy community.

Join us to help build a safer, more compassionate UK — one bite at a time.
summary:
Student-led organization raising food allergy awareness at the University of Kentucky. 

Contains: Advocacy, Education, and Inclusion

name: Aperture Journal of Undergraduate Research
description:
Aperture is an open-access, interdisciplinary, peer and expert reviewed student journal at the University of Kentucky. The journal’s primary mission is to make more visible the rich and diverse scholarship conducted by University of Kentucky undergraduates and promote the advancement of knowledge by publishing reports of their unique experiential endeavors.

Aperture is edited and produced by an undergraduate student editorial board of peer editors. Our board is composed of individuals with rich writing, editing, and research experiences who are passionate about the publication of undergraduate work.
summary:
Aperture is an open-access, interdisciplinary, peer and expert reviewed student journal at the
University of Kentucky. Aperture is edited and produced by an undergraduate student editorial
board of peer editors.

name: Recovery, Education, Advocacy, Community, Hope
description: R.E.A.C.H. UK is an organization that provides drug addiction education through promoting harm reduction methods, research, and offering volunteer outreach opportunities. Working with existing organizations throughout UK and the surrounding Lexington area, students will be able to get involved in helping improve the lives of those with substance use disorder and addiction.
summary: R.E.A.C.H. UK is an organization that provides drug addiction education through promoting harm reduction methods, research, and offering volunteer outreach opportunities.

name: Building H.E.R. (Honorable.Educated.Respectable) Young Women
description:
Building H.E.R. envisions a thriving village where every girl, and every family that stands behind her, is surrounded by a supportive community committed to her growth. We believe that anyone who values the power of a caring village—a network of mentors, families, and friends—is welcome to join the Building H.E.R. family. Our founder, raised in a single-parent household and shaped by the encouragement of remarkable women and men, created Building H.E.R. to pay that support forward. Here, we unite to empower not just the girls we serve, but also the loved ones who walk alongside them. Together, we make sure no one walks alone on the journey to becoming Honorable, Educated, and Respectable. 

Join our Group Me: https://groupme.com/join_group/110099904/EeeikHIe
summary: Building H.E.R. empowers young women to be honorable, educated, and respectable through mentorship, community support, and leadership opportunities. Join us in inspiring the next generation to thrive and succeed!

name: MSW Lounge
description:
MSW Lounge is a 100% virtual student organization that creates a warm, judgment-free space for MSW students at the University of Kentucky to connect, recharge, and build community. While designed with online learners in mind, all MSW students—virtual, hybrid, or in-person—are welcome.

 

We recognize the unique challenges of graduate school and aim to foster belonging through monthly Zoom meetups, wellness check-ins, themed discussions, and interest-based group chats. Whether you're balancing fieldwork, classes, work, or family, MSW Lounge is here to support your journey.
summary: MSW Lounge's mission is to support student well-being, foster meaningful peer relationships, and cultivate a welcoming pace where members can grow personally and professionally—no matter where they are.

name: Class of 2029 College of Dentistry
description: The Class of 2029 College of Dentistry is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering connection, collaboration, and professional growth among dental students in the graduating class of 2029. Our mission is to support academic success, promote wellness, and encourage community involvement through events, service initiatives, and peer mentorship. We aim to build a strong, united class community that reflects the values of integrity, excellence, and compassion in dentistry.
summary: The College of Dentistry Class of 2029.

name: The Hobby Horses
summary: Hobby Horse is a hobby interest group that helps UKCOM-BG and WKU students gain exposure to hobbies for stress relief. We focus on different hobbies during each of our meetings and also do outreach within the Bowling Green community.

name: College of Pharmacy
summary: BBNvolved page for the College of Pharmacy

name: Dermatology Interest Group - UKCOM
description: The Dermatology Interest Group (DIG) is a student-led organization dedicated to promoting interest, knowledge, and professional development in the field of dermatology. Our mission is to provide medical students with opportunities to explore dermatology through educational events, clinical exposure, research involvement, mentorship, and community service. We host lectures, workshops, and networking sessions with faculty and residents to help students learn more about dermatologic conditions, procedures, and career pathways. DIG also strives to foster collaboration among students while serving the community through skin cancer screenings, awareness campaigns, and other outreach initiatives.
summary: The Dermatology Interest Group (DIG) is a student-led organization dedicated to promoting interest, knowledge, and professional development in the field of dermatology. Our mission is to provide medical students with opportunities to explore dermatology

name: Pre-Optometry Club at the University of Kentucky
description: This is the official Pre-Optometry club at the University of Kentucky. The purpose of this organization is to bring together students with a common interest in optometry. This club is a great resource for students to build resumes for graduate programs, meet new friends, get connected to optometrists, and possibly even find new research opportunities.
summary: This is the official Pre-Optometry club at the University of Kentucky. The purpose of this organization is to bring together students with a common interest in optometry.

name: Scrubs at the Capitol
summary: A non-partisan club for any undergraduate student interested in advocacy, lobbying, and policy on matters unique to healthcare.

name: Interprofessional Healthcare Residential Community Alumni
summary: Alumni page for former students of IHRC

name: Wildcat Art Club
description: The UKY Art Club is a creative space for students of all skill levels to come together and share their passion for art. Each week, we’ll take on fun and inspiring art prompts to challenge our creativity and grow our skills. We’ll also spend time talking about our favorite artists, exploring new styles and techniques, and sharing what we’ve been working on. Members are encouraged to bring their own projects to get constructive feedback and friendly critiques from peers. Whether you’re a painter, digital artist, sculptor, or just love to doodle, our club is all about community, growth, and celebrating art in all its forms.
summary: Join us for weekly art prompts, discussions on our favorite artists, and chances to share your own work. Get constructive critiques, explore new styles, and connect with fellow Wildcats who love creating in any medium!

name: Network of Enlightened Women
description:
The Network of Enlightened Women educates, equips, and empowers women to be principled leaders for a free society. 

 

  • Educate: Offers speakers, debates, and resources to promote freedom, opportunity, independent thinking, and intellectual diversity.

  • Equip: Provides leadership training through campus sessions, conferences, retreats, and professional development.

  • Empower: Builds a supportive community through chapters and online networks, encouraging women to confidently advocate for liberty and connect with career opportunities.

NeW is a space for conservative women at the University of Kentucky to build friendships, grow as leaders, and engage in meaningful dialogue.
summary: The Network of Enlightened Women educates, equips, and empowers women to be principled leaders for a free society.

name: Unfiltered
summary: “We’re a student-led organization committed to building community, promoting well-being, and empowering college students through service, conversation, and creativity. we’re here for the culture, the cause, and the connection.”

name: Student Nutrition and Health Collective
description: Formerly known as the Student Nutrition and Dietetic Association, we most closely work with the Dietetics and Human Nutrition Department here on campus, but are open to all students interested in Health Sciences/the Medical Field. We provide pre-professional help through guest speakers of a variety of Health Professionals, service through our committees and different opportunities through involvement both on and off campus, and social activities through different meetings and activities such as restaurant Nights and workout classes! Join us in learning more about our future careers/career opportunities, serving our community, and building friendships within our majors!
summary: We are a club providing pre-professional, social, and service outlets to students interested in the Health and Medical Fields!

name: Radiant Church Young Adults
description: Radiant Church Lexington is a non-denominational, Christ-centered church aiming to "lead people to become radiant followers of Jesus" through its services, community, and mission-driven approach. They hold worship services that incorporate high-energy worship, relevant messages, and community fellowship, while also emphasizing spiritual growth through their "Radiant Groups" and commitment to the local and global community. The church values the Word of God, the Holy Spirit, and community and is deeply committed to sharing God's love and hope with the city of Lexington.
summary: Radiant YA's is the young adult group of a nondenominational worship and praying church in Lexington.

name: GenetiCATS
summary: The GenetiCATS is a student-led organization dedicated to exploring the fascinating world of genetics, genomics, and molecular biology, as well as the careers and pre-professional opportunities associated with them.

name: Vascular Surgery IG
description: Vascular Surgery Interest Group (VSIG) provides medical students with opportunities to explore the field of vascular surgery through monthly meetings with faculty, resident panels, and hands-on workshops. Members gain insight into the day-to-day life of vascular surgeons while developing early exposure to surgical techniques, tools, and career mentorship.
summary: VSIG provides medical students with opportunities to explore the field of vascular surgery through monthly meetings with faculty, resident panels, and hands-on workshops.

name: The Fashion & Merchandising Collective
description: The Fashion & Merchandising Collective is a bold, student-driven initiative designed to unite MAT majors who are passionate about every dimension of fashion including design, merchandising, styling, sustainability, and business. It is a living classroom and creative laboratory where students learn, create, and lead together. Our mission is to provide immersive experiences, professional development, and inspiring creative outlets that position fashion as both an art form and a powerful career pathway. Although UK offers exceptional MAT/RTM courses and outlets such as KRNL Magazine, there has never been a dedicated student-run organization that brings this talent together. The Collective fills that void by giving students a platform to collaborate, innovate, and showcase their work at the highest level. Members will develop industry-ready portfolios, build leadership skills, and gain direct access to exclusive workshops, renowned guest speakers, and meaningful connections with professionals across the fashion world. Our vision is to make the University of Kentucky the premier incubator for the next generation of fashion leaders, transforming our campus into a nationally recognized center of creativity, collaboration, and industry-ready talent that attracts attention from across the country.
summary: The Fashion & Merchandising Collective connects MAT majors passionate about style, retail, and design through workshops, guest speakers, and networking to spark creativity, build skills, grow leadership, and open doors to careers in fashion.

name: University Fire Marshal
summary: BBNvolved page for the University Fire Marshal's events.

name: National Organization for the Professional Advancement of Black Chemists and Chemical Engineers, University of Kentucky, Student Chapter
description:
National Organization for the Professional Advancement of Black Chemists and Chemical Engineers, University of Kentucky, Student Chapter members will be recruited from the University of Kentucky, including undergraduate and graduate students from the Departments of Chemistry and Chemical and Materials Engineering, as well as related STEM disciplines across the university.

Planned activities include:

· Hosting local meetings and networking events on topics of interest to students in STEM.

· Organizing seminars and workshops on advanced topics in the molecular sciences and emerging technologies.

· Facilitating team-based projects and research collaborations among members.

· Arranging career development sessions, including resume clinics, interview preparation, and industry panels.

· Conducting outreach programs to inspire K–12 students, particularly from underrepresented groups, to pursue STEM careers.
summary: The NOBCChE University of Kentucky Student Chapter intends to organize activities that promote academic excellence, professional development, and community engagement in chemistry, chemical engineering, and related fields.

name: Society of Automotive Engineers - Paducah
summary: Society of Automotive Engineers - Paducah builds a Baja racing buggy and takes part in the Baja SAE Competition.

name: Lambda Chi Alpha
summary: BBNvolved page for Lambda Chi Alpha Fraternity.

name: Undergraduate Mathematics Club
description: Math Club at the University of Kentucky is a community for anyone curious about mathematics. We bring students together through a mix of academic and social activities, including talks by faculty and graduate students, professional development panels, problem-solving sessions, and fun events like game nights and casual hangouts. Our mission is to create a welcoming space where students of all majors and years can explore math, build connections, and feel part of the broader mathematics community.
summary: Math Club connects students through academic talks, panels, and social events. Open to all majors and years—anyone with an interest in math is welcome to join the University of Kentucky undergraduate math club.

name: Beaux Arts Foundation
description:
A completely student-run, non-profit organization within the University of Kentucky chapter of American Institute of Architecture Students, with the mission of:
- Uplifting various forms of arts and humanities in the Lexington community.

- Advocating for the accessibility of the arts to the public.

- Supporting charities that promote the livelihood of Kentuckians.
summary:
A completely student-run, non-profit organization within the University of Kentucky chapter of American Institute of Architecture Students, with the mission of:
- Uplifting various forms of arts and humanities in the Lexington community.

name: Queer Student Collective
summary: An organization for queer students to come together in community.

name: Collegiate DECA at the University of Kentucky
description:
Collegiate DECA at the University of Kentucky (CDECA @ UKY) is a student-led organization dedicated to preparing emerging leaders and entrepreneurs for careers in marketing, finance, hospitality, management, and beyond. As part of the internationally recognized DECA network, we provide our members with opportunities to develop professional skills, gain real-world experience, and build powerful connections with peers, mentors, and industry leaders.

Through our chapter, members can:

  • Compete at local, state, and international conferences in a variety of business-related events

  • Participate in workshops, guest speaker sessions, and professional development programs

  • Collaborate on community service and campus engagement projects

  • Network with like-minded students and professionals to grow personally and professionally

Whether you’re looking to enhance your résumé, explore career paths, or join a community of ambitious and supportive peers, CDECA @ UKY will help you unlock your potential. We welcome students from all majors and backgrounds who are ready to challenge themselves and make an impact.

Join us to gain experience, build confidence, and stand out as a leader of tomorrow!
summary: CDECA at UKY empowers emerging leaders and entrepreneurs in marketing, finance, hospitality, and management. Join a dynamic network, gain real-world experience, compete, and build the skills to lead in college, careers, and beyond!

name: Wildcat Worship
summary: Wildcat Worship is a UK student organization with the objective of bringing all christians across campus into worship together, regardless of denomination or creed. We will gather monthly to lift up the name of Jesus.

name: Cats for Reproductive Freedom
description: Organization advocating for reproductive freedom in the Bluegrass! We believe that reproductive care should be more accessible to anyone seeking it out. If you're interested in issues like abortion access, sex education, & more, consider joining!
summary: Fighting for reproductive freedom in the Bluegrass! We believe that reproductive care should be more accessible to anyone seeking it out.

name: Students Supporting Israel at The University of Kentucky
description:
Students Supporting Israel (SSI) is a pro-Israel international campus movement that supports the State of Israel. 

Our mission is to be a clear and confident pro-Israel voice on college campuses and to support students in grassroots pro-Israel advocacy.
summary: Students Supporting Israel (SSI) is a pro-Israel international campus movement that supports the State of Israel. Our mission is to be a clear and confident pro-Israel voice on college campuses and to support students in grassroots pro-Israel advocacy.

name: American Physical Therapy Association Student Special Interest Group
description: The purpose of the S-SIG shall be to provide networking opportunities that allow physical therapy and physical therapy assistant students from across Kentucky to discuss issues affecting the physical therapy profession and to become better informed about the roles and activities of APTA-KY/APTA. The S-SIG shall serve as a vehicle for enhanced communication among students, a tool for the cultivation of professional relationships and a means of promotion and education of the physical therapy profession and interests affecting its key stakeholders.
summary: The Student Special Interest Group (S-SIG) connects PT and PTA students across Kentucky, fostering networking, communication, and professional growth while promoting education and awareness of the physical therapy profession.

name: RaiseHER
description:
RaiseHER is a student-led organization at the University of Kentucky dedicated to promoting maternal and child advocacy through education, awareness, and community engagement. Our organization was created to inspire students to become informed advocates for mothers, children, and families by addressing issues such as maternal health, access to childcare, postpartum support, and overall family well-being. 

Through hands-on service projects, educational workshops, and collaborative events, RaiseHER connects students with local organizations like The Nest Lexington and other community partners that provide direct support to families in need. We strive to bridge the gap between campus and community by giving students opportunities to serve, learn, and lead in real-world advocacy. 

RaiseHER believes that empowering students to understand and support maternal and child health not only strengthens families but also builds a more compassionate and equitable society. Members of RaiseHER work together to raise awareness about maternal and child challenges, organize donation drives, and create initiatives that promote positive change. 

Our mission goes beyond simple service, it’s about cultivating empathy, leadership, and action. RaiseHER is a space where all students, regardless of major, can come together to make a meaningful impact on families in Lexington and beyond.
summary: RaiseHER is a student-led organization at the University of Kentucky that empowers women, mothers, and families through advocacy, education, and community service—raising awareness, support, and one another to create lasting change.

name: Foreign Language and International Economics Club
summary: FLIE is an interdisciplinary club for anyone interested in language learning and business. Our goal is to create a community for students interested in both learning a new language and achieving a successful career after graduation.

name: NAMI On Campus
description: NAMI is the National Alliance on Mental Illness. It is the nation's largest grassroots mental health organization, dedicated to providing support and education to those affected. NAMI On Campus' objective will aim to promote mental illness awareness, education, and opportunities for volunteerism. Our organization will hold mental health centered-events, with the opportunity for collaboration between UK's undergraduate and professional students, as well as NAMI representatives.
summary: NAMIOC's objective will be to promote mental health awareness, education, and volunteerism. The club will hold mental health-centered events, with the opportunity for collaboration between UK's undergraduate and professional students, and NAMI reps.

name: Gift of Life Bone Marrow Club
description:
The Gift of Life Bone Marrow Registry Club is a student-led organization dedicated to increasing awareness about the importance of bone marrow and stem cell donation. Our mission is to help grow the national donor registry in hopes of matching patients battling blood cancers and other life-threatening diseases with their lifesaving donors.

Through educational tabling events, campus outreach, and volunteer drives, we empower students to take part in meaningful service that can directly save lives. Members have opportunities to lead recruitment efforts, collaborate with the national Gift of Life organization, and advocate for donor diversity within the registry. By joining, students can make an impact that extends beyond campus—helping give someone their second chance at life.
summary: Gift of Life Bone Marrow Club is a national organization across college campuses that raises awareness for bone marrow donation and hosts registry drives to connect students to be matched to save a life

name: Baila Blue: Latin Dance Club
description: Baila Blue is a student organization that seeks to promote the appreciation of and interest in Latin dances (including but not limited to salsa, bachata, and cumbia). To do so, Baila Blue offers free Latin dances--and other educational opportunities pertaining to Latin dance--to the campus community. The organization also trains UK students to be peer instructors of Latin dance genres, particularly salsa, bachata, and cumbia. Finally, Baila Blue creates and promotes Latin dance events, both on campus and in the Lexington community. Through all of these related areas, Baila Blue seeks to 1) promote a sustainable and welcoming sense of community for students, faculty, and staff, particularly those with an interest in Latin dances and 2) encourage students, staff, and faculty to learn about the cultural and historical components of Latin dances. Baila Blue is open to all students and staff--come dance with us!
summary: Baila Blue is a student organization that promotes appreciation of and interest in Latin dances (e.g. salsa, bachata, and cumbia) by providing free dance classes and social events to the campus community.

name: United Nations Association-United States of America
summary: UNA chapter members advocate for UN goals, educate their campus and local community, and organize events to promote global citizenship, US-UN relations, and the Sustainable Development Goals

name: Military Medical Community at the University of Kentucky
description:
The Military Medical Community at the University of Kentucky (MMC) is a pre-professional organization for undergraduate and medical students interested in military medicine. Our primary focus is education: helping students learn what life is like as a military physician and what opportunities the military offers pre-meds and medical students, including the Health Professions Scholarship Program (HPSP), monthly stipends, tuition coverage, loan repayment options, and the Uniformed Services University of the Health Sciences (USUHS).

Each month, we host guest speakers such as active-duty physicians, HPSP students, residents, medical recruiters, and military medical leaders. We also offer workshops and informational sessions that provide firsthand insight into training, residency, service obligations, and career paths within military medicine.

We supplement this learning experience with mentorship, professional development, service involvement, and leadership opportunities. Networking happens naturally as members engage with speakers, mentors, and peers. Our mission is centered on education, exposure, and helping students make informed decisions about their future.

Membership is open to anyone interested in learning about military medicine; there is no military commitment or obligation to join MMC. Our goal is to support students as they explore military medical career paths, grow as leaders, and prepare for future medical careers. Service opportunities are available throughout the year.
summary: MMC is a pre-professional organization for undergraduate and medical students interested in military medicine. We host monthly guest military physicians and workshops to help students learn about military medical careers. No military commitment required.

name: Pharm cats
description: A club dedicated to the BSPS (Bachelors of Science in Pharmaceutical Sciences) students of the University of Kentucky. Students are given the opportunity to network, socialize, meet peers, and explore different options in not only pharmaceuticals, but other health pathways.  We will meet once a month on Thursdays at 6 pm in the Gatton Student Center. anyone in the BSPS program that would like to learn more or be involved is more than welcome to join.
summary: Club for BSPS students to network socialize, and explore different career options and pathways

name: Medical Students for Life
summary: Our mission is to educate medical students on the pro-life principles found in the Hippocratic oath, and to equip them to uphold these values throughout their training and in their medical practice.

name: Operation Smile Kentucky
description:
Founded in 1982 by Dr. Bill Magee and Kathy Magee, Operation Smile is a global nonprofit specializing in cleft surgery and care. Thanks to our generous donors, we provide medical training, education and mentorship to surgical teams around the world, along with expertise, research and care through our staff and volunteers, working alongside local governments, nonprofits and health systems.

Our team approach provides the highest quality care for patients living in countries with under-resourced health systems where safe surgery and cleft care may be unavailable or inaccessible to them. Our community-focused patient recruitment efforts locate children in need of essential cleft surgery and provide them with accessible follow-up treatments.

Operation Smile has believed that access to safe surgery is not a privilege, but a universal human right.
summary: Operation Smile is a global nonprofit specializing in cleft surgery and care. It works closely with communities worldwide to expand access to care through training and educational programs, and offering free, life-changing cleft surgeries and care.

name: Child Life Club
description: The Child Life Club strives to help facilitate opportunities to gain experience working with children and families experiencing unique stressors, including those related to healthcare for those interested in becoming a Child Life Specialist. Members will develop a positive relationship between students and local organizations that support children and families throughout challenging situations, ultimately allowing students the opportunity to begin gaining relevant information and experiences early in their collegiate career.
summary: The Child Life Club is a student-led organization focused on enriching the understanding of the Child Life Specialist profession.

name: College of Medicine Refugee Advocacy In Medicine Program
description:
The Refuge Advocacy in Medicine Program (RAMP) is a student-led organization dedicated to supporting the refugee community in Bowling Green, Kentucky. Our mission is to improve health literacy and increase access to essential resources for refugees, empowering them to lead healthier, more informed lives.

 

Through various service events, educational workshops, and collaborative efforts with local organizations, we aim to bridge gaps in healthcare understanding, advocate for equitable access to resources, and foster a sense of belonging for this underserved population. By promoting awareness and providing actionable support, we strive to create a healthier and more inclusive community for all.
summary: a student-led organization dedicated to supporting the refugee community in Bowling Green, Kentucky. Our mission is to improve health literacy and increase access to essential resources for refugees, empowering them to lead healthier, more informed lives

name: Delta Delta Sigma
description: Delta Delta Sigma is an undergraduate pre-dental honor society committed to supporting students who aspire to careers in dentistry. The organization helps undergraduates build a strong foundation through academic development, mentorship, and meaningful exposure to the dental profession. Members participate in guest lectures, skill workshops, service projects, and networking events that strengthen their understanding of dentistry and enhance their dental school applications. Delta Delta Sigma fosters a supportive, achievement-focused community where motivated undergraduates can grow together, gain leadership experience, and make a positive impact. Its mission is to promote integrity, excellence, and service among future dental professionals.
summary: Delta Delta Sigma is an undergraduate pre-dental honor society that supports students through academic growth, professional development, and service opportunities, helping them prepare for successful dental careers.

name: Bowling Green Oncology Interest Group
description: This organization serves as an interest group for medical students at the Bowling Green campus, providing structured opportunities to explore the field of oncology through connections with oncologists, community volunteer work, and research initiatives.
