summary: The aim of this club is to introduce dental students to the field of Orofacial Pain through sessions discussing research, cases within the field, lectures on pain pathways and discussions with current residents.

name: The Aesculapian Lumina Coalition for Medical Students with Disabilities and Chronic Illnesses
description: The Aesculapian Lumina Coalition (ALC) for Medical Students with Disabilities and Chronic Illnesses (MSDCI) at the University of Kentucky College of Medicine is a student-led organization dedicated to uplifting learners with disabilities, chronic illnesses, and neurodivergent conditions. Through a focus on advocacy, education, and peer support, the ALC fosters inclusive community-building and amplifies the voices of marginalized learners within the medical field. By coordinating outreach, leadership development, and specialized events, the coalition ensures that all medical students feel seen, supported, and empowered to succeed throughout their medical education.
summary: The Aesculapian Lumina Coalition (ALC) at UK COM supports students with disabilities, chronic illness, and neurodivergence. We foster inclusion through advocacy and peer support to empower marginalized voices in medical education.

name: Actuary Club
description:
Our goal is to provide a better understanding of the actuarial career, connect with other actuarial students, and build relationships with industry professionals that can lead to internships and jobs.

We provide career information, guidance with preliminary exams, and a space for aspiring actuaries to assist each other in the exam-studying process.
summary: Becoming an Actuary takes dedication, persistence, and a lot of work. The Actuary Club at the University of Kentucky provides support for students who are eager to take on the challenge.

name: Nu Rho Psi – The National Honor Society in Neuroscience
description:
What is Nu Rho Psi?

Nu Rho Psi is the National Honor Society in Neuroscience, founded in 2006 by the Faculty for Undergraduate Neuroscience and now an independent honor society. We are a non-profit, grass-roots organization comprised of neuroscientists, like you. Currently, charters have been awarded to over 60 chapters in all regions of the United States. The purpose of Nu Rho Psi is to:

  • encourage professional interest and excellence in scholarship, particularly in Neuroscience
  • award recognition to students who have achieved such excellence in scholarship
  • advance the discipline of Neuroscience
  • encourage intellectual and social interaction between students, faculty, and professionals in Neuroscience and related fields
  • promote career development in Neuroscience and related fields
  • increase public awareness of Neuroscience and its benefits for the individual and society
  • encourage service to the community.

Who may join?

Membership to the University of Kentucky's Nu Rho Psi chapter is by invitation and is open to undergraduate and graduate students, as well as faculty members who are making the study of Neuroscience one of their major interests and who meet the other academic qualifications.  Requirements for membership include:

  • Major, minor, other emphasis in Neuroscience
  • Completion of at least 3 semesters of the College course
  • Completion of at least 9 semester hours of Neuroscience-related courses
  • Undergraduate cumulative GPA of 3.2 and a minimum GPA of 3.5 in Neuroscience courses

Nu Rho Psi is also open to qualifying Neuroscience faculty and alumni of Neuroscience programs. Students and faculty are elected to membership by the chapter at participating institutions, according to the provisions in the national Nu Rho Psi Constitution and locally adopted By-Laws.  Any chapter, at its discretion, may establish higher academic standards for eligibility, but may not require lower standards for eligibility.  Membership in Nu Rho Psi is open to qualified candidates regardless of age, sex, sexual orientation, race, handicap or disability, color, religion, or national or ethnic origin.

What are the benefits of membership? 

Nu Rho Psi membership provides recognition of verified academic excellence in the interdisciplinary field of Neuroscience. Students who become members of Nu Rho Psi are selected based on their superior scholarly accomplishments as well as their interest and experience in research. Nu Rho Psi membership is for life, and it is often a springboard for networking and research collaboration. Nu Rho Psi offers competitive travel awards for members to attend and present their research at the annual Society for Neuroscience meeting. Nu Rho Psi also offers competitive research grants to facilitate our members’ senior theses or other scholarly projects. Our chapters may apply for Nu Rho Psi grants to promote their educational and community initiatives. We mentor our members through publications (e.g. The Nu Rho Psi Guide to Graduate School in Neuroscience) and provide support to foster other outreach activities of the Society. News and additional information about benefits are available to members via the Nu Rho Psi Newsletter. Members receive membership certificates and lapel pins as an indication of the honor they have earned.
summary: Nu Rho Psi is the National Honor Society in Neuroscience, founded in 2006 by the Faculty for Undergraduate Neuroscience and now an independent honor society. We are a non-profit, grass-roots organization comprised of neuroscientists.

name: Golfer's Organization at the University of Kentucky
description:
Golfer's Organization at the University of Kentucky is an inclusive organization designed for UK students who enjoy the sport and want to make connections through golf while improving their game. Our club is dedicated to planning events such as scrambles, matches, workshop clinics, and other social golf outings. 

One of our primary objectives is to make sure that every UK student who enjoys golf always has a group of likeminded students to golf with. There is no handicap or skill requirement to join Golfers Organization because we are primarily a social organization rather than competitive, but there are prizes that can be won at our events!
summary: Golfers Organization at the University of Kentucky is an inclusive organization designed for UK students who enjoy golfing and want to make new connections through golf while improving their game.

name: Association of Latino Professionals for America Student Chapter at the University of Kentucky
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to empower and develop leaders of character for the nation in every sector of the global economy. This organization shall serve as a hub for leadership development, community engagement, and professional growth by providing access to mentorship, internships, and career opportunities. ALPFA Student Chapter at the University of Kentucky will serve to open doors and connect students with the resources to foster long-term success at the University of Kentucky and beyond.
summary: ALPFA will serve as a platform at the University of Kentucky where every member can have the opportunity to lead, learn, and thrive through resources that will help strengthen leadership, networking, and professional skills within each of our members.

name: Robotics and Advanced Surgical Technology Club
description: We aim to provide early exposure to surgery through robotic platforms, augmented reality (AR), simulation-based training, and emerging operative technologies; create structured opportunities for students to engage with surgeons who utilize robotic systems; support involvement in clinical, translational, and technology-driven surgical research; highlight disparities in access to advanced surgical care, particularly in Kentucky and rural or underserved populations; and foster a collaborative, non-competitive environment for students exploring surgery and surgical technology at any stage of training.
summary: Provide early exposure to surgery through robotic surgery platforms, augmented reality (AR), simulation-based training, and emerging operative technologies.

name: Sisters in Science
description:
Sisters in Science is a women centered organization built to empower, support, and uplift women pursuing pre-med and healthcare careers. We believe women thrive in environments where sisterhood, encouragement, and shared ambition are at the core. Our community will offer a safe, motivating space where women can connect with one another, build confidence, and navigate the pre-health journey together.

As a women’s community, we will prioritize creating opportunities that promote academic, professional, and personal growth. We will provide access to shadowing, volunteering programs, peer tutoring, and study workshops tailored to the pre-healthcare pathways. Through these experiences, members will gain hands on exposure to healthcare settings, strengthen academic performance, and build relationships with other driven women in science.

Sisters in Science is a support system. We celebrate women’s achievements, amplify women’s voices, and foster mentorship at every stage of the journey to medicine. Our mission is to ensure that every woman pursuing a future in healthcare has the community, confidence, and resources she needs to succeed.

 

Join the GroupMe!! 

https://groupme.com/join_group/113759404/ulrr73DO
summary: Sisters in Science is a women centered group supporting pre health students through mentorship, shadowing, volunteering, and study workshops. We empower women in medicine by building confidence, community, and academic success.

name: Wildcat Tango at the University of Kentucky
description:
Wildcat Tango is a student-led organization at the University of Kentucky dedicated to building a welcoming, beginner-friendly Argentine tango community on campus.

Our goal is not only to teach tango, but to create a safe and inclusive space where students feel comfortable learning, practicing, and connecting through dance. No dance background is required, and students are never expected to bring a partner. We believe tango should be accessible, supportive, and enjoyable at every level.

Wildcat Tango offers:

  • A structured 4-week beginner series introducing the foundations of Argentine tango in a clear, approachable format

  • Additional beginner-friendly classes and guided instruction throughout the semester

  • Weekly student practica, providing a low-pressure space to practice, experiment, and build confidence

  • Occasional bootcamps and workshops for deeper skill development

  • Monthly Community Night Practica events that connect University of Kentucky students with the broader Lexington tango community in a respectful, student-centered environment

We emphasize consent, mutual respect, and inclusive participation. Our leadership team is committed to fostering a culture where students can grow at their own pace, feel supported by their peers, and experience tango as both an art form and a community practice.

Whether you are completely new to dance or looking to deepen your skills, Wildcat Tango provides a space to learn, move, and build lasting connections.
summary: Wildcat Tango is a welcoming UK student club offering beginner-friendly Argentine tango classes, weekly student practica, and select community events that connect students with the broader Lexington tango scene.

summary: Collegiate Future Business Leaders of America is a nationwide leadership program that offers students in college the opportunity to grow their business and leadership skills through leadership conferences, business competitions, and leadership positions.
